clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 60 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B-cell. Cancer burden allowed: bone marrow involvement with 20% or more lymphoblasts. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: venetoclax. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Philadelphia chromosome positive.
general_exclusion_criteria: Poor performance status (ECOG > 2). Liver dysfunction (Serum total bilirubin > 1.5 x upper limit of normal or > 3 x upper limit of normal for patients with Gilbert's disease, or Alanine aminotransferase or aspartate aminotransferase > 3.0 x upper limit of normal). Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance <= 50 mL/min). Pregnant or breastfeeding. Uncontrolled infection. Hepatitis B or C infection or human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) seropositivity. Symptomatic or untreated leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression. Active heart disease (New York Heart Association class 3-4, unstable angina, stroke, or myocardial infarction within the last 6 months). Cardiac ejection fraction less than 40%. History of another primary invasive malignancy that has not been definitively treated or in remission for at least 2 years. Concurrent use of warfarin. Malabsorption syndrome or other conditions that preclude enteral route of administration. Other severe acute or chronic medical or psychiatric conditions.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: Diffuse large B cell lymphoma not otherwise specified. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory. Prior treatment required: 2 or more lines of chemotherapy including rituximab or equivalent and anthracycline and (failure of autologous stem cell transplant or ineligible for autologous stem cell transplant or not intended for autologous stem cell transplant or not consenting to autologous stem cell transplant). Prior treatment excluded: Prior CAR-T therapy for any indication or systemic gene modifying therapy for B-cell lymphoma or prior allogeneic stem cell transplant for any indication or prior BITE antibodies for cancer therapy or prior T cell receptor-engineered T cell therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1 unless decrease is due to lymphoma).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance <= 45mL/min).
Cardiac dysfunction (ejection fraction < 45% or history of myocardial infarction, cardiac angioplasty or stenting, unstable angina, or other clinically significant cardiac disease within 6 months of enrollment).
Respiratory dysfunction (resting O2 saturation <= 90% on room air).
Liver dysfunction (ALT or AST >= 5 times the Upper Limit of Normal or total bilirubin >= 1.5 mg/dl except in Gilbert's syndrome).
Bone marrow suppression (absolute neutrophil count <= 1000/uL or absolute lymphocyte count <= 100/uL or platelet count <= 50,000/uL).
Known history of infection with human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) or active hepatitis B (HBsAg positive) or hepatitis C virus (unless viral load is undetectable per quantitative PCR and/or nucleic acid testing).
Pharmacologically uncontrolled seizures.
Known history or presence of autoimmune central nervous system disease (eg multiple sclerosis, optic neuritis) or other immunologic or inflammatory disease.
Presence of central nervous system disorder that may impair the ability to evaluate neurotoxicity.
Midline shift on MRI, abnormal high CSF opening pressure, or CSF protein > 150 mg/dL (for CNS cohort).
Recent (within 3 months) whole brain radiotherapy.
Active systemic fungal, viral, or bacterial infection.
Pregnant or breast-feeding women.
Previous or concurrent malignancy (with specific curative intent and remission exceptions).
Severely immunocompromised subjects (eg due to current treatment of non-neurologic autoimmune disease).
Medical condition requiring prolonged use of systemic corticosteroids equivalent to prednisone > 10 mg/day.
Concurrent radiotherapy.
Baseline dementia that would interfere with therapy or monitoring.
History of severe immediate hypersensitivity reaction to any of the agents used in the study.
Refusal to participate in additional lentiviral gene therapy LTFU protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: advanced and platinum-refractory or platinum-resistant. Prior treatment required: standard of care systemic therapy or no standard therapy available. Prior treatment excluded: antibody-drug conjugate with a topoisomerase I payload. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Life expectancy < 12 weeks
Active or progressing brain metastases or evidence of leptomeningeal disease
Persistent toxicities from previous systemic antineoplastic treatments of Grade > 1
Clinically significant lung disease requiring systemic corticosteroid treatment (eg interstitial pneumonia, pneumonitis, pulmonary fibrosis, or severe radiation pneumonitis)
Acute or chronic pancreatitis
Cirrhosis (except Child-Pugh class A)
Hepatic insufficiency manifesting as clinical jaundice, hepatic encephalopathy, or variceal bleed
History of liver transplant
Prior allogeneic bone marrow transplantation
Significant cardiac disease (eg recent myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndromes, congestive heart failure New York Heart Association class III or IV, uncontrolled hypertension, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias, or severe aortic stenosis)
History of thromboembolic or cerebrovascular events (eg transient ischemic attacks, cerebrovascular accidents, deep vein thrombosis, or pulmonary emboli)
Acute or clinically significant bacterial, fungal, or viral infection including uncontrolled hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or HIV
Pregnancy or breast-feeding

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic or locally recurrent unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: radiological or objective evidence of progression on any CDK 4/6 inhibitor-containing regimen in the metastatic setting or relapse/progression during or within 12 months of completion of any CDK 4/6 inhibitor-containing regimen in the adjuvant setting AND radiological or objective evidence of progression on fulvestrant in the metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: MEK inhibitor or abemaciclib discontinued for toxicity at any dose or fulvestrant discontinued for toxicity. Biomarkers required: hormone receptor positive (ER+ or PR+) and HER2 negative (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG >= 2).
Inadequate bone marrow function (absolute neutrophil count < 1.5 x 10^9/L, platelets < 100,000/microliter, or hemoglobin < 9 g/dL).
Liver dysfunction (total bilirubin > 1.5 mg/dL, except Gilbert syndrome, or AST/ALT > 2.5 x ULN).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance rate < 50 mL/min).
Coagulopathy (INR > 1.5 or PTT > 1.5 x ULN without anticoagulation).
Elevated creatine phosphokinase (> 2.5 x ULN).
Poor cardiac function (left ventricular ejection fraction < 50% or baseline QTc interval >= 480 ms).
Active brain metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.
Pre-existing ocular pathology (glaucoma, retinal vein occlusion, retinal pathology, intraocular pressure > 21 mm Hg, corneal erosion, corneal degeneration, active or recurrent keratitis, or serious ocular surface inflammatory conditions).
History of rhabdomyolysis or neuromuscular disorders associated with elevated CK.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina pectoris, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, congestive heart failure NYHA Class III or IV, active ischemic heart disease, myocardial infarction within the previous six months, syncope of cardiovascular etiology, ventricular arrhythmia, uncontrolled diabetes mellitus, severe obstructive pulmonary disease, or severe chronic liver or renal disease).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG >= 2).
Liver dysfunction (direct bilirubin >= 2 x ULN, ALT and/or AST >= 3x ULN unless deemed to be related to underlying leukemia).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance <= 30 ml/min).
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to ivosidenib or venetoclax.
Concurrent uncontrolled clinically significant medical condition including infection, laboratory abnormality, or psychiatric illness.
Use of concomitant strong CYP3A inducers (avasimibe, carbamazepine, phenytoin, rifampin, rifabutin, St. Johns wort).
Active graft-versus-host-disease status post stem cell transplant.
Severe gastrointestinal or metabolic condition which could interfere with the absorption of oral study medications.
Concurrent active malignancy under treatment.
QTcF interval > 450 msec.
Known active hepatitis B (HBV) or Hepatitis C (HCV) infection or known HIV infection.
White blood cell count > 25 x 10^9/L.
Nursing women, women of childbearing potential with positive urine pregnancy test, or women of childbearing potential who are not willing to maintain adequate contraception.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Short life expectancy (eg 3 months or less).
Symptomatic brain metastases requiring > 10 mg/day of prednisolone.
Mesenchymal tumors of the uterus.
History of clinically meaningful ascites (defined as history of paracentesis or thoracentesis with therapeutic intent).
Uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), hepatitis B, or hepatitis C infection.
Clinically meaningful coagulopathy or bleeding diathesis.
Cardiovascular disease (including uncontrolled hypertension, symptomatic heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction, severe cardiovascular disease, transient ischemic attack, or cerebrovascular accident).
Significant electrocardiogram abnormalities or congenital long QT syndrome.
History of major surgery within 4 weeks of screening.
Bowel obstruction related to current cancer within the last 6 months or symptoms of intestinal obstruction within the last 4 weeks.
Inadequate organ function (including inadequate absolute neutrophil count, hemoglobin, platelets, creatinine clearance, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, or serum albumin).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent disease with pN0 or pNX pathologic stage and no radiographic evidence of local, regional, or distant metastatic disease via PSMA PET or Axumin fluciclovine F18 scan and not pathologic T4 disease. Prior treatment required: Radical prostatectomy (must be intra or interfascial and successful bilateral nerve sparing). Prior treatment excluded: Cryotherapy or brachytherapy or post-prostatectomy radiation therapy or pelvic radiation therapy or anorectal surgery (including low anterior resection, abdominoperineal resection, or absence of a rectum). Biomarkers required: PSA 0.1 ng/mL or higher. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1).
Known allergy to hyaluronic acid.
Planned elective pelvic lymph node radiation therapy.
Inflammatory bowel disease requiring treatment with steroids (e.g. Crohn's disease or ulcerative colitis).
Active connective tissue disorder including lupus or scleroderma.
Urogenital abnormality that could limit access to the Barrigel injection site.
White blood cell count < 4000/uL or > 12,000/uL.
Hemoglobin < 10 g/dL.
Active bleeding disorder or clinically significant coagulopathy (defined as PTT > 35 seconds or INR > 1.4 or platelet count < 100,000/mm3).
Liver dysfunction (Serum AST/ALT > 2.5 times institutional upper limit of normal or Bilirubin > 2.0 mg/dL).
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine > 2.0 mg/dL or history of chronic renal failure).
History of uncontrolled diabetes (e.g. symptomatic hyperglycemia not controlled with medical management or fasting blood glucose > 300 mg/dL).
Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) or HIV infection with CD4+ T-cell counts < 350 cells/uL or history of AIDS-defining opportunistic infections.
Contraindication to having an MRI or PSMA/PET scan.
Presence of bilateral hip implants.
Subject unable or unwilling to comply with study requirements.
Any condition that prevents administration or completion of study therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2).
Neurologically unstable, symptomatic, or corticosteroid-dependent central nervous system metastases.
Concurrent acute kidney injury or chronic kidney disease.
Baseline xerostomia Grade 2 or higher.
History of uncontrolled hypertension, myocardial infarction, angina pectoris, or coronary artery bypass graft within 6 months, or clinically active significant cardiac disease.
History of lymphoproliferative disease or any other malignancy within the past 5 years.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: <= 30 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Wilms tumor. Histology allowed: Diffuse anaplastic. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of bilateral Wilms tumor.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including active infection or symptomatic congestive heart failure (CTCAE grade 2 or higher).
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Life expectancy less than 8 weeks.
Low peripheral absolute neutrophil count (< 750/uL).
Low platelet count (< 75,000/uL).
Low hemoglobin (< 8.0 g/dL).
Liver dysfunction (total bilirubin > 1.5 x upper limit of normal and direct bilirubin > upper limit of normal, or AST or ALT > 2.5 x upper limit of normal, or AST or ALT > 5 x upper limit of normal for patients with liver metastases).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance or radioisotope GFR < 60 mL/min/1.73 m^2, or serum creatinine exceeding age-based maximums).
Poor cardiac function (shortening fraction < 27% or ejection fraction < 50%).
Chronic inflammatory bowel disease or bowel obstruction.
Concomitant use of St. John's wort.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Refusal to use effective contraceptive methods.
Renal tubular acidosis (serum bicarbonate < 16 mmol/L and serum phosphate < 2 mg/dL without supplementation).

Active uncontrolled systemic fungal, bacterial, or viral infection
Recent vaccination with any live attenuated vaccine or planned vaccination with live attenuated vaccine

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 to 84 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: high grade serous ovarian carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent ovarian cancer isolated to the abdomen or pelvis with no extra-abdominal metastases. Prior treatment required: at least 1 line of prior systemic therapy AND (platinum resistant or platinum intolerant). Prior treatment excluded: prior immune checkpoint blockade with grade 3 or higher toxicities OR prior immune checkpoint blockade with immune-related toxicity that resulted in permanent discontinuation of therapy OR prior immune checkpoint blockade with immune-related toxicity requiring intensive or prolonged immunosuppression to manage (except well-controlled endocrinopathy). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: germline or somatic BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutations.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Inadequate marrow function (Absolute neutrophil count < 1,000/mcL or platelets < 75,000/mcL).
Liver dysfunction (AST/ALT > 3 x ULN or total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN, except Gilbert's or disease-related hemolysis where > 3 x ULN).
Renal dysfunction (Serum creatinine > 2.0 mg/dL AND glomerular filtration rate < 40 mL/min/1.73 m2).
Poor pulmonary function (Oxygen saturation < 90% on room air).
Poor cardiac function (Left ventricular ejection fraction < 40% or New York Heart Association Functional Classification worse than class 2B).
Ongoing hemolysis.
Bowel obstruction within the last 3 months or high risk for bowel obstruction.
Requirement for parenteral nutrition or dependence on intravenous fluids.
Receipt of other concurrent investigational agents.
Solid organ transplant (allograft) recipients.
Known additional malignancy that is progressing, requires active treatment, or occurred within 2 years of first dose (except cured basal cell or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, superficial bladder cancer, prostate intraepithelial neoplasm, carcinoma in situ of the cervix, other non-invasive or indolent malignancy, or cancers disease-free for > 1 year after curative treatment).
History of severe or anaphylactic allergic reactions to compounds similar to N-803, murine-based antibody therapy, or iron dextran.
Autoimmune disease including inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis or Crohn's disease) or symptomatic disease (rheumatoid arthritis, systemic progressive sclerosis, systemic lupus erythematosus, autoimmune vasculitis) or motor neuropathy of autoimmune origin (Guillain-Barre syndrome or myasthenia gravis).
Systemic corticosteroid therapy (> 10 mg of prednisone or equivalent).
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness/social situations limiting compliance.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
HIV-positive.
Active uncontrolled hepatitis B or C.
Non-infectious pneumonitis or history of interstitial lung disease.
Prior history of Grade 2 or higher hemolytic anemia.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: Soft tissue sarcoma. Histology allowed: Leiomyosarcoma. general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2 or Karnofsky < 60%).
Bone marrow suppression (Absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mcL or Platelets < 100,000/mcL or Hemoglobin < 8 g/dL).
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin >= 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal or AST or ALT >= 3 x institutional upper limit of normal).
Renal dysfunction (Glomerular filtration rate < 51 mL/min/1.73 m^2).
Clinically significant cardiac disease or poor cardiac function (NYHA Functional Classification worse than class 2B or left ventricular ejection fraction below the institutional lower limit of normal).
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease requiring immediate central nervous system specific treatment.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Residual toxicities from prior anti-cancer therapy greater than grade 1 (except alopecia).
Concurrent use of other investigational agents.
History of allergic reactions to peposertib (M3814) or compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition.

Inability to discontinue concomitant proton-pump inhibitors.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness.
Inability to swallow tablets whole.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: histologically or cytologically confirmed invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Progressed on 0 to 1 prior lines of therapy in the metastatic setting AND (if HR-positive: progressed on or within 12 months of adjuvant endocrine therapy or received at least one line of endocrine therapy in the metastatic setting, and received prior CDK4/6 inhibitor). Prior treatment excluded: Prior topoisomerase I inhibitor therapy in any setting. Biomarkers required: HER2-low (IHC 1+ or IHC 2+/ISH non-amplified) known prior to study registration AND HR-positive (ER expression >= 1% or PR expression >= 1%) known prior to study registration. Biomarkers excluded: HER2-positive (IHC 3+ or ISH-amplified).
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
History of non-infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease that required steroids, or current pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease.
Lung-specific illnesses including pulmonary emboli within 3 months, severe asthma, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, restrictive lung disease, pleural effusion, autoimmune or inflammatory disorders with pulmonary involvement, or prior complete pneumonectomy.
Cardiac conditions within 6 months including coronary artery bypass graft, angioplasty, vascular stent, myocardial infarction, angina pectoris, congestive heart failure (NYHA Grade >= 2), or stroke.
Corrected QT interval (QTcF) prolongation > 470 msec for females or > 450 msec for males.
Baseline LVEF < 50% (for T-DXd cohorts).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min/1.73m2).
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN, AST/ALT > 2.5 x ULN or > 5.0 x ULN for participants with liver metastases).
Known uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection.
Known active or uncontrolled hepatitis B or C virus infection.
Active infection, including tuberculosis.
Clinically significant corneal disease.
History of severe hypersensitivity reactions to trastuzumab deruxtecan, datopotamab deruxtecan, or other monoclonal antibodies.
History of a second malignancy (unless disease-free for at least 3 years or deemed low risk by investigator).
Pregnant or breastfeeding women.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: Clear cell. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced or metastatic. Prior treatment required: At least one immune checkpoint inhibitor and at least one tyrosine kinase inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: Prior PSMA-targeted therapy (eg. 177Lu-PSMA-617). Biomarkers required: Presence of at least one PSMA-avid lesion (with uptake greater than liver) and absence of any measurable PSMA-negative lesions (nodal, soft tissue or visceral lesions greater than 1 cm) on baseline/screening 68Ga-PSMA-11 PSMA-PET/CT. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Inadequate organ or marrow function (ANC < 1.5x10^9/L, Platelets < 100x10^9/L, Hemoglobin < 9 g/dL, Total bilirubin > 1.5x ULN, ALT or AST > 3-5x ULN, or eGFR < 50 mL/min/1.73 m2).
Untreated brain metastases, active brain metastases, or leptomeningeal disease.
Residual toxicities from prior anti-cancer therapy greater than Grade 1 (excluding alopecia and sensory neuropathy grade <= 2).
History of allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to 177Lu-PSMA-617.
Symptomatic or impending cord compression.
Concurrent active malignancy.
Serious and/or uncontrolled preexisting medical conditions (eg. interstitial lung disease, severe dyspnea at rest or requiring oxygen therapy, severe renal impairment with estimated creatinine clearance < 30 ml/min, history of major surgical resection involving the stomach or small bowel, preexisting Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, or a preexisting chronic condition resulting in baseline Grade 2 or higher diarrhea).
Concurrent use of other investigational agents.
Pregnancy or lactation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 0-21 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: acute leukemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory (excluding isolated extramedullary disease, isolated central nervous system relapse, and symptomatic central nervous system disease). general_exclusion_criteria:
Down syndrome
Acute promyelocytic leukemia
Juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia
Isolated extramedullary disease
Isolated central nervous system relapse
Symptomatic central nervous system disease
Malabsorption syndrome or any other condition that precludes enteral administration of a menin inhibitor
Use of proton pump inhibitors
Currently receiving another investigational drug
Any known congenital bone marrow failure syndrome
Known prior allergy to any of the medications used in protocol therapy
Documented active, uncontrolled infection at the time of study entry
Active or uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection, hepatitis B virus (HBV), or hepatitis C virus (HCV)
Positive pregnancy test or lactating female participants
Pre-existing disorder predisposing the participant to a serious or life-threatening infection (e.g., cystic fibrosis, congenital or acquired immunodeficiency, bleeding disorder, or cytopenia not related to the leukemia or its treatment)
Significant congenital cardiovascular disease (e.g., long QT syndrome or fundamental uncorrected cardiac defect)
Hypersensitivity to fludarabine or cytarabine
Recent live vaccinations within 6 months
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2, Lansky < 50%, or Karnofsky < 50%)
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min)
Liver dysfunction (Direct bilirubin >= 3 x upper limit of normal or alanine transaminase > 5 x upper limit of normal, unless due to leukemia infiltrate)
Cardiac dysfunction (Fractional shortening < 25% or ejection fraction < 40%, or signs of congestive heart failure)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Women and men. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic breast cancer with at least 1 measurable lesion outside the brain (brain metastases allowed but not required) and documented intra- and/or extra-cranial radiological progression or recurrence while on or after the most recent therapy. Prior treatment required: At least one endocrine therapy and up to two chemotherapy regimens and up to two prior CDK 4/6 inhibitors (excluding abemaciclib) in the metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: Abemaciclib in the metastatic setting and elacestrant or other investigational SERDs, or investigational alike agents such as SERMs, SERCANs, CERANs, and PROTACs in the metastatic setting. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG >2).
Inadequate bone marrow or organ function (Absolute neutrophil count <1.5 x 10^9/L, platelets <100 x 10^9/L, hemoglobin <9.0 g/dL, creatinine clearance <50 mL/min, or serum albumin <3.0 g/dL).
Liver dysfunction (ALT or AST >3.0 x ULN without liver metastases or >5 x ULN with liver metastases, or total serum bilirubin >=1.5 x ULN unless Gilbert's syndrome is present).
Electrolyte abnormalities (Potassium, sodium, calcium, or magnesium CTCAE Grade >1).
Immediate CNS-specific treatment likely to be required.
Imminent organ failure and/or visceral crisis.
Leptomeningeal metastases.
Concurrent malignancy or malignancy within 3 years of enrollment (except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, carcinoma in situ of the cervix, or second primary breast cancer).
Currently participating in another breast cancer intervention clinical study.
Uncontrolled significant active infections (including HBV, HCV, or HIV with detectable viral load).
Major surgery within 4 weeks of starting trial therapy.
Inability to take oral medication, history of malabsorption syndrome, or any other uncontrolled gastrointestinal condition.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Failure to agree to highly effective contraception.
Known intolerance to study drugs or excipients.
Any severe medical or psychiatric condition that would preclude participation in a clinical study.

Known alcohol or drug abuse.

Calculated QTc average > 470 msec.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Liver dysfunction (AST/ALT > 2.5 x institutional ULN or total bilirubin > 1.5 institutional ULN).
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min and GFR < 60 mL/min).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: urothelial carcinoma of the bladder, renal pelvis, ureter, or urethra. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic or locally advanced unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: platinum containing therapy (defined as within the adjuvant/neoadjuvant setting with >= ypT2 disease at surgery, or recurrent or progressive disease within 12 months, or receiving treatment with platinum in locally advanced or metastatic setting) and a checkpoint inhibitor (defined as a PD-1 or PD-L1 inhibitor given in locally advanced or metastatic urothelial cancer setting, or received in the neoadjuvant/adjuvant setting with recurrent or progressive disease either during or within 12 months of therapy completion) and progression on or since most recent therapy. Renal dysfunction (glomerular filtration rate < 30 mL/min/1.73 m2).
Active cardiac disease (myocardial infarction or unstable angina pectoris within 6 months, history of serious ventricular arrhythmia, high-grade atrioventricular block, other cardiac arrhythmias requiring anti-arrhythmic medications except controlled atrial fibrillation, history of QT interval prolongation, New York Heart Association Class III or greater congestive heart failure, or left ventricular ejection fraction < 40%).
Active chronic inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis, Crohn's disease) or gastrointestinal perforation within 6 months.
Active serious infection requiring antibiotics.
Conditions requiring high doses of steroids (> 10 mg/day of prednisone or equivalent) or other immunosuppressive medications.
History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug-induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or evidence of active pneumonitis.
Known active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.
Active second malignancy.
Uncontrolled diabetes (hemoglobin A1C > 8% or 7-8% with associated diabetes symptoms).
Uncontrolled tumor related bone pain or impending spinal cord compression.
Pregnancy or lactation.
History of allergic reactions to study drug compounds or excipients.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness or psychiatric illness/social situations that would limit compliance with study requirements.
History of autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
LVEF < 50%.
Impaired bone marrow function (eg absolute neutrophil count < 2,000/mcL, platelets < 100,000/mcL, or hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dl).
Hepatic dysfunction (eg total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN or AST/ALT > 2.5 x ULN).
Renal dysfunction (eg serum creatinine > 1.5 x ULN and creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min/1.73m2).
Coagulopathy (eg INR/PT/aPTT > 1.5 x ULN).
Low serum albumin (< 2.5 g/dL).
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina pectoris, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, serious chronic gastrointestinal conditions associated with diarrhea, or psychiatric illness).
Chronic systemic steroid therapy (> 10 mg of prednisone daily or equivalent) or other immunosuppressive medication within the last 2 years.
Active or prior documented autoimmune or inflammatory disorders.
History of or current interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis.
Clinically significant lung-specific illnesses (eg pulmonary emboli within 3 months, severe asthma, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, restrictive lung disease, or pleural effusion).
Corrected QT interval (QTcF) prolongation > 470 msec or factors increasing the risk of QTc prolongation.
Recent major cardiovascular events within 6 months (eg coronary artery bypass graft, angioplasty, vascular stent, myocardial infarction, angina pectoris, congestive heart failure New York Heart Association Grade 2 or higher, or stroke).
History of another primary malignancy.
History of venous thromboembolism in the past 3 months.
Active primary immunodeficiency.
Active infection including tuberculosis, hepatitis B (HBsAg positive), or hepatitis C (HCV RNA positive).
HIV positive.
History of leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.

general_exclusion_criteria:
- Other invasive malignancies within the last 5 years, except non-melanoma skin cancer with no evidence of disease within the past 5 years and localized breast cancer with previous adjuvant chemotherapy treatment completed more than 5 years ago.
- Synchronous primary invasive ovarian or cervical cancer.
- Active or history of autoimmune disease or immune deficiency.
- History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug-induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis.
- Active tuberculosis.
- Severe infections.
- Significant cardiovascular disease.
- Prior allogeneic bone marrow transplantation or solid organ transplant.
- History of treatment with systemic immunostimulatory agents.
- History of treatment with systemic immunosuppressive medications.
- History or clinical evidence of any untreated CNS disease, seizures not controlled with standard medical therapy, or history of cerebrovascular accident (stroke), transient ischemic attack, or subarachnoid hemorrhage within 6 months.
- Life expectancy 12 weeks or less.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: mucosal melanoma. Histology allowed: mucosal melanoma. general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2)
Absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mm^3
Platelet count < 100,000/mm^3
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine >= 1.5 x upper limit of normal AND creatinine clearance < 50mL/min/1.73 m^2)
Albumin < 2.8 g/dL
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin >= 1.5 x upper limit of normal OR Aspartate aminotransferase/alanine aminotransferase >= 2.5 x upper limit of normal OR Child-Pugh class B or C liver impairment OR ascites OR coagulopathy OR bleeding due to liver dysfunction)
Acute coronary syndromes (including myocardial infarction and unstable angina), coronary artery bypass graft, coronary angioplasty, or stenting within 6 months prior to study entry
Current class II or higher congestive heart failure (NYHA classification)
Refractory hypertension (systolic > 140 mmHg and/or diastolic > 90 mmHg despite anti-hypertensive therapy)
History of myocarditis
Syncope of cardiovascular etiology, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, Mobitz II second degree or third degree heart block without a permanent pacemaker, NYHA class II to IV heart failure, or stroke/transient ischemic attack within the past 3 months
Corrected QT interval by Fridericia's formula (QTcF) > 500 msec
Congenital bleeding diathesis
Gastrointestinal bleeding requiring intervention within the past 6 months (unless related to mucosal melanoma)
Active hemoptysis within 42 days prior to study enrollment
Active tumor lesions with cavitations or tumor lesions which invade, encase, or abut major blood vessels
Pulmonary emboli or deep vein thromboses (DVT) requiring an active anticoagulation regimen
Cytopenia of greater than 3 months duration with unknown cause, myelodysplastic syndrome, or hematologic malignancies
Active bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring treatment
Gastrointestinal disorder affecting absorption of oral medications
Active autoimmune disease or condition requiring systemic corticosteroids (> 10 mg daily of prednisone equivalents) or other immunosuppressive medications
History of autoimmune motor neuropathy (eg Guillain-Barre syndrome, myasthenia gravis) or non-infectious pneumonitis
Severe allergic reactions to study drugs or excipients
History of gastrointestinal perforation or abdominal fistula
Clinically suspected central nervous system (leptomeningeal or parenchymal) metastases (excluding treated CNS metastases that are stable and asymptomatic for 28 days)
History of seizure or conditions increasing seizure risk (eg cortical stroke, significant brain trauma) within 2 years
Untreated spinal cord compression or spinal metastases with risk of impending fracture or spinal cord compression
Chronic concomitant treatment with strong inhibitors or inducers of CYP3A4
Pregnant or nursing
Concurrent malignancy requiring ongoing systemic treatment (excluding hormone therapy for NED cancers)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: oropharyngeal cancer. Histology allowed: squamous cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: locoregionally recurrent or second primary in a previously irradiated field (defined as >50% of the presurgical tumor volume having prior radiation dose of >45 Gy) and candidate for curative intent surgery and no known distant metastatic disease (including brain metastases). Prior treatment required: curative intent treatment for head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (surgery and/or radiation therapy with or without platinum chemotherapy or cetuximab targeted therapy). general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Inadequate organ and marrow function (leukocytes <3,000/mcL, absolute neutrophil count <1,500/mcL, platelets <100,000/mcL, total bilirubin > institutional upper limit of normal, AST or ALT > 3x institutional upper limit of normal, creatinine > institutional upper limit of normal and glomerular filtration rate <50 mL/min/1.73 m2).
Severe cardiac dysfunction (New York Heart Association Functional Classification > 2B).
Immunodeficiency.
Chronic systemic steroid therapy (exceeding 10 mg daily of prednisone equivalent).
Immunosuppressive therapy.
Known additional malignancy that is progressing or has required active treatment within the past 2 years.
History of allergic reactions to agents used in study.
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years.
History of non-infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Active infection requiring systemic therapy.
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection.
Hepatitis B (HBsAg reactive) or active Hepatitis C virus infection.
Known psychiatric or substance abuse disorders.
Residual toxicities > Grade 2 due to prior anti-cancer therapy.
Allogeneic tissue or solid organ transplant.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: epithelial ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: any histology except mesonephric or mucinous or neuroendocrine/small cell or undifferentiated. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent platinum-resistant disease and measurable disease and four or fewer lines of prior systemic therapy. Prior treatment required: at least one prior platinum-based chemotherapeutic regimen. Prior treatment excluded: weekly paclitaxel in the recurrent setting or any BCL-XL inhibitor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >2).
Known active central nervous system involvement with metastatic cancer (unless stable for at least one month on imaging with no neurologic symptoms and not receiving pharmacologic doses of glucocorticoids).
Ongoing treatment with chronic immunosuppressants or systemic steroids > 10 mg of prednisone daily.
Prior organ transplantation (except certain allogeneic or autologous stem cell transplantations).
History of major surgery within 8 weeks.
History of small or large bowel obstruction within 8 weeks.
History of clinically significant ascites or pleural effusion requiring recurrent paracentesis or thoracentesis within 4 weeks.
Dependence upon TPN or regular IV fluid resuscitation.
Ongoing or active infections (Grade >= 2).
Baseline prolongation of QTc interval (> 470 msec) or history of Long QT Syndrome.
History of a bleeding complication within the past 4 weeks or a clinically significant bleeding predisposition.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg symptomatic congestive heart failure, symptomatic angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or requiring dialysis).
Allergic reactions or hypersensitivity to paclitaxel or compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to DT2216.
Use of medications or substances that are strong inhibitors or inducers of CYP3A4 or CYP2C8.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Liver dysfunction (eg total bilirubin > 1.5x ULN, or > 4.0x ULN in Gilbert's disease, or > 5.0x ULN with documented hepatic involvement; AST/ALT > 3x ULN).
Renal dysfunction (eg eGFR < 60 mL/min).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 to 65 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory and >=5% blasts in bone marrow or extramedullary sites. Prior treatment required: at least 1 prior line of therapy (for example, 3+7 chemotherapy or hypomethylating agent therapy). Prior treatment excluded: Mylotarg or other therapies associated with increased risk of hepatic veno-occlusive disease or prior allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation or prior autologous hematopoietic cell transplantation or prior solid organ transplantation. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Inadequate pulmonary function (FEV1, FVC, or DLCO < 60% of predicted).
Inadequate cardiac function (Cardiac Ejection Fraction < 45% or evidence of pulmonary hypertension).
Hepatic dysfunction (Total bilirubin above institutional limits except in Gilbert's Syndrome, or AST or ALT >= 2x institutional upper limit of normal).
Renal dysfunction (Serum Creatinine above institutional limits and creatinine clearance <= 50 mL/min/1.73 m2).
Extramedullary disease at immune privileged sites (for example, central nervous system, testes, or eye).
Residual non-hematologic toxicities > Grade 1 from prior anti-cancer therapy.
Known prior or active hepatic veno-occlusive disease.
Myocardial infarction within 2 years prior to enrollment.
Venous thromboembolic event of deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism within 1 year prior to enrollment (except line-associated deep vein thrombosis if anticoagulation therapy is completed).
Stroke or transient ischemic attack within 1 year prior to enrollment.
History of bleeding peptic ulcer disease, erosive gastritis, intestinal perforation, clinically significant gastrointestinal hemorrhage, or hemoptysis within the prior 6 months.
History of thrombotic microangiopathy or hemolytic uremic syndrome or thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura.
History of life-threatening reactions to iron infusions or murine antibody-containing products.
Known donor-specific antibodies of clinical significance.
Inability to withhold agents that interact with hepatic cytochrome P450 enzymes involved in cyclophosphamide and/or thiotepa metabolism during the study window.
Uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infections.
Prior radiation exposure or medical conditions (for example, Fanconi syndrome) that preclude the use of myeloablative radiation.
HIV-positive participants on combination antiretroviral therapy.
Seropositivity for hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection.
Psychiatric illness or social situations that limit compliance with study requirements.
Pregnancy.
History of another non-hematologic malignancy (unless disease-free for at least 5 years and deemed low risk for recurrence, or history of cervical cancer in situ, or basal cell or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin diagnosed and treated within the past 5 years).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Inadequate organ or marrow function (low ANC, low platelet count, low hemoglobin, elevated serum creatinine or low creatinine clearance, elevated total bilirubin, or elevated ALT/AST).
Untreated, symptomatic brain metastases or brain metastases requiring therapy to control symptoms.
Major surgical procedures within 28 days of treatment start.
Current residual grade >= 2 neuropathy or grade >= 2 toxicity (except alopecia or anorexia) from prior therapy.
History of allergic reactions attributed to azenosertib, carboplatin, or pembrolizumab.
Severe hypersensitivity (>= Grade 3) to pembrolizumab.
History of non-infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease that required steroids or current pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Use of a percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) tube or total parenteral nutrition (TPN).
Receipt of an investigational agent or use of an investigational device within 4 weeks prior to study intervention.
Receipt of a live vaccine or live-attenuated vaccine within 30 days before the first dose.
Additional malignancy that is progressing or has required active treatment within the past 3 years.
Concurrent treatment with strong or moderate cytochrome P450 3A4/3A5 inhibitors or P-gp inhibitors or strong or moderate cytochrome P450 3A4/3A5 inducers.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including ongoing or active infection, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina pectoris, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association Class III or IV), active ischemic heart disease, myocardial infarction within the previous six months, uncontrolled diabetes mellitus, gastric or duodenal ulceration diagnosed within the previous 6 months, chronic liver or renal disease, or severe malnutrition.
Psychiatric illness or substance abuse disorder that would limit ability to comply with study requirements.
Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, or previous significant bowel resection precluding adequate absorption of azenosertib.
Baseline ECG mean resting corrected QT interval (QTcF) >= 470 msec, congenital long QT syndrome, or family history of long QT syndrome or Torsade de Pointes.
Taking medications with a known risk of Torsade de Pointes or medication that leads to significant QT prolongation.
Active infection or active tuberculosis requiring systemic therapy.
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years.
Diagnosis of immunodeficiency, receiving systemic steroid therapy (exceeding 10 mg daily of prednisone or equivalent), or other immunosuppressive therapy within 7 days prior to treatment.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
History of allogenic tissue or solid organ transplant.

Prior treatment required: platinum-based chemotherapy AND definitive surgical resection AND response to first-line therapy and no evidence of progression. Prior treatment excluded: prior radiotherapy to the brain or whole brain radiotherapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Zubrod > 2)
Contraindication to MR imaging
Contraindication to gadolinium contrast administration
Other metastatic malignancies requiring current active treatment
Unstable angina or congestive heart failure requiring hospitalization within 6 months prior to randomization
Transmural myocardial infarction within 6 months prior to randomization
Acute bacterial or fungal infection requiring intravenous antibiotics at the time of randomization
Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease exacerbation or other acute respiratory illness precluding study therapy at the time of randomization
Severe hepatic disease (Child-Pugh class B or C)
HIV positive with CD4 count < 200 cells/microliter
Pregnancy

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 to 39 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: craniopharyngioma. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Newly diagnosed. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any tumor-directed therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Karnofsky < 50 for participants > 16 years or Lansky < 50 for participants <= 16 years).
Inadequate bone marrow function (absolute neutrophil count < 1000/mm3 or platelet count < 100,000/mm3).
Inadequate renal function (serum creatinine >= 1.5 Upper Limit Normal).
Inadequate liver function (bilirubin > 1.5 x Upper Limit Normal, alanine aminotransferase > 3 x Upper Limit Normal, or serum albumin < 2 g/dL).
Uncontrolled seizure disorder or use of enzyme-inducing anticonvulsants.
Inadequate pulmonary function (dyspnea at rest, exercise intolerance due to pulmonary insufficiency, or pulse oximetry <= 92% on room air).
Rapidly progressive symptoms requiring urgent surgery or radiation therapy.
Uncontrolled symptoms of neuroendocrine dysfunction (eg diabetes insipidus, hypothyroidism, or panhypopituitarism).
Clinically significant active cardiovascular disease, history of myocardial infarction, deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism within 6 months, ongoing cardiomyopathy, or QTcF interval > 440 ms.
History of allergic reactions to Tovorafenib or similar compounds.
Grade 2 or higher nausea and vomiting, malabsorption requiring supplementation, or significant bowel or stomach resection.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including active infection.
Current treatment with other investigational agents.
Pregnancy or breast-feeding.
Current treatment with a strong cytochrome P4502C8 inhibitor or inducer.
Inability to return for follow-up visits or obtain follow-up studies.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 12 to 17 years of age (after Safety Review Committee approval) or 18 years of age or older. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumors. Histology allowed: Solid. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic. Prior treatment required: One or more lines of anticancer therapy and progressive disease. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: TP53 Y220C mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Primary CNS tumor.
History of leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression.
Brain metastases unless neurologically stable and not requiring steroids to treat associated neurological symptoms.
Stroke or transient ischemic attack within 6 months prior to screening.
Heart conditions such as unstable angina, uncontrolled hypertension, heart attack within 6 months prior to screening, congestive heart failure, prolongation of QT interval, or other rhythm abnormalities.
Use of strong CYP3A4 inducers.
History of gastrointestinal disease that may interfere with absorption of study drug or inability to take oral medication.
History of prior organ transplant.
Known active malignancy except for treated cervical intraepithelial neoplasia or non-melanoma skin cancer.
Known active uncontrolled Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Prior therapy with an anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, or anti-PD-L2 agent or agent directed to another stimulatory or co-inhibitory T-cell receptor that was discontinued due to a Grade 3 or higher immune-related adverse event.
Received a live or live-attenuated vaccine within 30 days prior to the first dose of study intervention.
Diagnosis of immunodeficiency or receiving chronic systemic steroid therapy within 7 days prior to the first dose of study drug.
Hypersensitivity (Grade 3 or higher) to pembrolizumab and/or any of its excipients.
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in past 2 years.
History of radiation pneumonitis.
History of non-infectious or active pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease that required steroids.
Active infection requiring systemic therapy.
Known history of HIV infection.
Previous treatment with rezatapopt.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years of age or older. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma of the prostate without pathologic evidence of small cell differentiation. Cancer burden allowed: High-risk localized prostate cancer defined as (prostate specific antigen > 20 and any Gleason score or Gleason score > 8) or (Gleason pattern 4 in 6 or more systematic cores where pattern 4 is dominant and at least 50 percent average across those cores) or (at least 50 percent Gleason pattern 4 in 3 or more systematic or Magnetic Resonance Imaging-targeted cores and prostate specific antigen at least 20) or (at least 25 percent Gleason pattern 5 in 3 or more systematic or Magnetic Resonance Imaging-targeted cores) or (Gleason > 8 on minimum of one targeted or systematic biopsy and prostate specific antigen > 20) or (oligometastatic less than 3 metastases by Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen imaging only and deemed candidates for radical prostatectomy). Must not have stage T4 prostate cancer or M1 disease by conventional imaging (computed tomography or bone scan). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Systemic or localized treatment for prostate cancer (including androgen deprivation or chemotherapy or surgery or radiation). Biomarkers required: Genomic profiling results (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Life expectancy less than 3 years
Inadequate end-organ function
Hypogonadism or severe androgen deficiency
Active infection or chronic liver disease requiring systemic therapy
Active or known human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) with detectable viral load
Uncontrolled or recent clinically significant cardiac disease (including angina pectoris, symptomatic pericarditis, coronary artery bypass grafting, coronary angioplasty, stenting, or myocardial infarction in the previous 12 months), history of documented congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association functional classification III-IV), cardiomyopathy, or cardiac arrhythmias (ventricular, supraventricular, nodal arrhythmias, or conduction abnormality in the previous 12 months)
Uncontrolled hypertension
Inability to swallow oral medication or impairment of gastrointestinal function or gastrointestinal disease that may significantly alter the absorption of study drugs (eg ulcerative diseases, uncontrolled nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, malabsorption syndrome, or small bowel resection)
History of hypersensitivity to any of the study drugs or any excipient
History of non-compliance to medical regimen
Severe concurrent disease, infection, or co-morbidity

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable or metastatic. Prior treatment required: If hormone receptor-positive, progressed on and no longer benefit from endocrine therapy, and treated with at least 1 and at most 2 prior lines of chemotherapy in the recurrent or metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: Anti-HER2 therapy in the metastatic setting and antibody-drug conjugate consisting of an exatecan derivative that is a topoisomerase I inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Low HER2 expression defined as IHC 2+ /ISH-negative or IHC 1+ (ISH-negative or untested). Biomarkers excluded: HER2-positive defined as IHC 3+ or IHC 2+/ISH+.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Previous treatment with any enhancer of zeste homolog inhibitors.
Uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease.
Spinal cord compression or clinically active central nervous system metastases (untreated and symptomatic, or requiring therapy with corticosteroids or anticonvulsants).
Leptomeningeal carcinomatosis or metastasis.
Clinically severe pulmonary compromise resulting from intercurrent pulmonary illnesses.
Current use of moderate or strong cytochrome P450 (CYP)3A inducers.
Systemic treatment with corticosteroids (greater than 10 mg daily prednisone equivalents).
History of severe hypersensitivity reactions to other monoclonal antibodies.
Evidence of ongoing uncontrolled systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring treatment with intravenous antibiotics, antivirals, or antifungals.
Female who is pregnant or breastfeeding or intends to become pregnant during the study.
Psychological, social, familial, or geographical factors that would prevent regular follow-up.
Poor performance status (eg ECOG PS > 1).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: VEXAS syndrome. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Current or documented evidence of past involvement within 6 months prior to enrollment of at least one of the following organ systems: cutaneous (e.g., neutrophilic dermatosis, cutaneous vasculitis), vasculature (e.g., vasculitis), musculoskeletal (e.g., chondritis, arthritis), ocular (e.g., uveitis, scleritis), periorbital (e.g. periorbital edema), genitourinary (e.g., epididymitis), or pulmonary (e.g., alveolitis). Prior treatment required: Ongoing glucocorticoid therapy (stable prednisone or prednisolone dose of 15-45 mg/day). Prior treatment excluded: Allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplant or solid organ transplant (other than corneal) or exposure to more than 4 cycles of hypomethylating agents at any time. Biomarkers required: Pathogenic mutation at methionine-41 (M41) or neighboring splice site mutation (c.118-1 or c.118-2) in UBA1 assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Karnofsky Performance Status <50%)
Inadequate organ function (aspartate aminotransferase or alanine aminotransferase >3 x upper limit of normal, total bilirubin >4 x upper limit of normal [or >8 x upper limit of normal in the setting of Gilbert's syndrome], creatinine clearance <30 mL/min, absolute neutrophil count <500/uL, prothrombin time or international normalized ratio >1.5 x upper limit of normal, partial thromboplastic time or activated PTT >1.5 x upper limit of normal, platelet count <25 x 10^9/L, or peripheral blasts >=5%)
Current use of systemic glucocorticoids for conditions other than VEXAS syndrome
More than one prior admission to an intensive care unit due to a VEXAS syndrome flare within the prior 6 months
Received 9 or more units of intensive red blood cell transfusions in the 90 days prior to enrollment
Known concurrent myelodysplastic syndrome requiring antineoplastic treatment, or known high-risk or very high-risk myelodysplastic syndrome based on the Revised International Prognostic Scoring System (IPSS-R)
Malignancy within 1 year prior to enrollment (except curatively treated non-melanoma skin cancer, curatively treated carcinoma in situ, or pre-malignant hematologic conditions such as monoclonal gammopathy of unknown significance or clonal cytopenia of unknown significance)
Known concomitant multiple myeloma, or serum M-protein >=3 g/dL, or involved-to uninvolved free light chain ratio >=100, or involved free light chain level >=100 mg/dL
Significant recent bleeding history (CTCAE grade >=2) within 3 months prior to enrollment
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease, including QT corrected by the Fridericia method (QTcF) > 480 msec, severe cardiac event (CTCAE grade >=3) within 3 months prior to enrollment, or heart failure resulting in limitations during ordinary activity
Arterial or venous thrombotic or embolic events (including deep vein thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, or cerebrovascular accident) within 60 days prior to enrollment
Moderate or severe hepatic impairment meeting criteria for Child-Pugh Class B or C, or active viral hepatitis
Uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus (HIV)
Positive Quantiferon or other interferon gamma release assay during screening
Known history of disseminated mycobacterial infection
Pregnancy, intention to become pregnant, or currently breastfeeding/lactating
Acute, active infection requiring systemic antimicrobial treatment at the time of enrollment
Known hypersensitivity to pacritinib or its inactive ingredients (microcrystalline cellulose, polyethylene glycol, or magnesium stearate)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: any histological or cytological evidence of prostate cancer (including neuroendocrine, small cell, and sarcomatoid). Cancer burden allowed: metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) defined as castrate levels of serum testosterone (< 50 ng/dL) and (PSA progression or radiographic progression per RECIST 1.1 or bone metastasis progression per Prostate Cancer Working Group 3). Prior treatment required: androgen receptor signaling inhibitor (ARSI) and (taxane therapy or taxane ineligible or taxane refusal) and cabazitaxel and carboplatin. Prior treatment excluded: EZH inhibitors. Biomarkers required: CLIA-certified next generation sequencing (NGS) assay (during screening) and no known germline or somatic deleterious BRCA 1/2 mutations and no prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) positivity. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Current use of moderate or strong cytochrome P450 (CYP)3A inducers.
Known or suspected hypersensitivity to valemetostat tosylate or any of the excipients.
Chronic hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection with detectable viral load.
Hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection that has not been treated and cured or currently active infection with detectable viral load.
HIV infection with detectable viral load within 6 months.
Imminent or established spinal cord compression.
Known brain metastases or cranial epidural disease unless adequately treated and stable for at least 4 weeks.
Myocardial infarction within 6 months prior to enrollment.
Uncontrolled angina pectoris within 6 months prior to enrollment.
New York Heart Association Class 3 or 4 congestive heart failure.

Uncontrolled hypertension (resting systolic blood pressure > 160 mmHg or diastolic blood pressure > 100 mmHg).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1).
Prior treatment with any anti-TGF beta therapy.
Prior history of Grade 2 or higher intolerance or hypersensitivity reaction to cetuximab, other anti-EGFR therapy, or other murine proteins, or prior discontinuation of therapy due to toxicity.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Requirement for systemic treatment with corticosteroids (greater than 10 mg daily of prednisone or equivalent) or other immunosuppressive medication.
Known history of a hematologic malignancy or solid tumor other than those indicated for the study (unless potentially curative therapy was completed with no evidence of disease for 2 years).
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection with a CD4+ T-cell count less than 250 cells/uL, or failure to be on established antiretroviral therapy for at least four weeks, or an HIV viral load greater than or equal to 400 copies/mL.
Chronic hepatitis B infection with active disease who meet criteria for anti-HBV therapy and are not on suppressive antiviral therapy.
Known history of hepatitis C who have not completed curative antiviral treatment or have a HCV viral load above the limit of quantification.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: Intracranial diffuse glioma. Histology allowed: Diffuse astrocytoma. Cancer burden allowed: First radiographic relapse. Prior treatment required: chemotherapy including temozolomide (no more than 12 cycles of temozolomide). Prior treatment excluded: procarbazine, lomustine and vincristine (PCV) or any of its components, carmustine wafer implants, bevacizumab, and PARP inhibitors. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Current enrollment in another interventional clinical trial.
Current treatment with other anticancer agents or devices.
Current treatment at recurrence with carmustine wafer implants or proteasome inhibitors.
Prior radiotherapy with a dose over 65 Gy, stereotactic radiosurgery or brachytherapy (unless recurrence is histologically proven).
Use of full-dose anticoagulants (unless INR or aPTT is within therapeutic limits and patient has been on a stable dose for at least two weeks).
Treatment with enzyme-inducing anti-epileptic drugs (EIAED).
Treatment with concomitant medications known to be sensitive substrates of CYP2D6 and CYP2C19 that cannot be replaced.
Pregnancy or breast-feeding.
Known hypersensitivity to any component of NMS-03305293 or temozolomide.
Known active infections requiring systemic treatment (including HIV).
Prolonged QTc interval (>= 460 milliseconds for women, >= 450 milliseconds for men) or risk factors for torsade de pointes (e.g., uncontrolled heart failure, uncontrolled hypokalemia, history of prolonged QTc interval or family history of long QT syndrome).
Active gastrointestinal disease impacting drug absorption.
History of myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, symptomatic congestive heart failure, cerebrovascular accident or transient ischemic attack, or active bleeding disorder in the past 6 months.
Prior invasive malignancy (except for non melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ or localized cancer) unless disease-free and off therapy for >= 3 years.
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2).
Other severe acute or chronic medical or psychiatric conditions or laboratory abnormalities.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 2 to 39 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: diffuse midline glioma. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: radiation therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: thalamic and cerebellar H3K27M mutant diffuse midline glioma.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Diagnosis of a histone H3 wildtype grade II diffuse astrocytoma.
Current receipt of another investigational drug.
Current receipt of other anti-cancer agents.
Known disorder affecting the immune system, such as human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or an auto-immune disorder requiring systemic cytotoxic or immunosuppressive therapy.
Uncontrolled infection or other uncontrolled systemic illness.
Pregnancy or breast-feeding in female participants.
Active illicit drug use or diagnosis of alcoholism.
History of allergic reactions to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition as the study agents.
Evidence of disseminated disease, including diffuse leptomeningeal disease or evidence of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) dissemination (except for Cohort 5).
Known additional malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment within 3 years of start of study drug.
Concomitant use of potent CYP3A4/5 inhibitors.
Concomitant use of potent CYP3A4/5 inducers, including enzyme inducing antiepileptic drugs (EIAEDs).
Inadequate organ function, defined as: peripheral absolute neutrophil count less than 750/mm3, or platelet count less than 75,000/mm3, or creatinine clearance/GFR less than 70 mL/min/1.73 m2 and serum creatinine outside normal limits for age, or bilirubin greater than 1.5 x upper limit of normal (ULN) for age, or serum glutamate pyruvate transaminase (SGPT/ALT) greater than 3 x ULN, or serum albumin less than 2 g/dL.
Pulmonary insufficiency, defined as dyspnea at rest, exercise intolerance, or pulse oximetry of 92 percent or less while breathing room air.
Diarrhea grade 2 or higher by CTCAE version 5.0.
History of congestive heart failure or family history of long QT syndrome.
Corrected QT Interval (QTc) greater than or equal to 470 msec.
Inadequate cardiac function, defined as shortening fraction less than 27 percent.
Uncontrolled seizure disorder.
Poor performance status, defined as Karnofsky less than 50 for participants older than 16 years or Lansky less than 50 for participants 16 years of age or younger.
Inability to provide adequate tissue (minimum of 10-20 paraffin embedded unstained slides or 1 block with tumor content of 40 percent or greater).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Clinically significant drug or alcohol abuse within the last 6 months.
Clinically significant neurologic, psychiatric, endocrine, metabolic, immunologic, cardiovascular, pulmonary, or hepatic disease within the last 6 months.
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 12 years and above. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: B-cell Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia. Histology allowed: B-cell Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: Bone marrow infiltration with greater than or equal to 5% blasts and relapsed or refractory disease and must not have active central nervous system involvement and must not have isolated extramedullary disease relapse and must not have testicular leukemia. Prior treatment required: Greater than or equal to 2 prior lines of therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: CD19 expression (assessed by local lab) and Philadelphia chromosome negative. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status greater than 2 for participants older than 16 years or Lansky score less than 50% for participants 16 years or younger).
Clinically relevant central nervous system pathology such as epilepsy, seizure, paresis, aphasia, stroke, severe brain injuries, dementia, Parkinson's disease, cerebellar disease, organic brain syndrome, or psychosis.
Prior Grade 4 neurotoxicity with CAR-T or T-cell engager therapy.
History of other malignancy.
Unresolved adverse events grade 2 or higher from prior therapies.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 60 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: confirmed prostate cancer excluding small cell prostate cancer. Cancer burden allowed: advanced (defined as at least one lesion >= 10 mm on CT or MRI, or metastatic lesions on bone scan, or metastatic disease on Axumin or PSMA PET) and must not have metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior combination hormonal therapy with abiraterone acetate. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG >= 2)
Expected time to next treatment < 12 months or life expectancy < 18 months
Hematologic dysfunction (leukocytes < 3,000/mcL, absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mcL, or platelets < 100,000/mcL)
Liver dysfunction (total bilirubin >= institutional ULN, or AST or ALT > 3 x institutional ULN)
Renal dysfunction (creatinine >= institutional ULN and glomerular filtration rate < 50 mL/min/1.73 m^2, or glomerular filtration rate < 30 mL/min/1.73 m^2)
Cardiac dysfunction (New York Heart Association Functional Classification > 2B)
Brain metastases
Residual toxicities > grade 1 from prior anti-cancer therapy (except alopecia)
Receiving any other investigational agents
History of allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to agents used in the study
Symptomatic skeletal event complication of prostate cancer (eg cord compression, fracture, or need for radiation or surgery to a bone lesion) within 6 months
Uncontrolled pain related to prostate cancer or separate chronic condition
Visceral crisis from prostate cancer
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness
Concurrent second active malignancy
Severe sleep disorders (eg Narcolepsy)
Eye diseases which limit the ability of light to be processed (eg untreated cataracts, severe glaucoma, macular degeneration, blindness, pupil dilation problems or other retinal disorder)
Severe psychological impairment (eg bipolar disorder or manic episodes)
Current employment in night shift work
Previous use of light therapy to alleviate fatigue or depressive symptoms
Currently recovering from previous eye surgery within the past 6 months that causes eye irritation
Sensitivity to light, epilepsy, or a history of seizures

Biomarkers required: Estrogen-receptor expression <= 5% and progesterone-receptor expression <= 5% and HER2-negative status and (PD-L1 expression < 1% on tumor-infiltrating immune cells (IC) by the PD-L1 IHC SP142 assay or Combined Positive Score (CPS) < 10 by the PD-L1 IHC 22C3 assay). general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Untreated, symptomatic brain metastases or brain metastases that require therapy to control symptoms.
Known history of human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), Hepatitis B (HBsAg reactive), or active Hepatitis C virus (detected HCV RNA) infection.
History of (non-infectious) pneumonitis that required steroids or current pneumonitis.
Medical condition that requires chronic systemic steroid therapy (> 10 mg of prednisone daily or equivalent) or any other form of immunosuppressive medication in the last 2 years.
Documented history of autoimmune disease or syndrome that currently requires systemic steroids or immunosuppressive agents.
History of a second malignancy (except for cervical/prostate carcinoma in situ, superficial bladder cancer, or non-melanoma cancer of the skin diagnosed and treated within the past 3 years, or other malignancies where the patient has been disease-free for at least 3 years or is low risk for recurrence).
Breast-feeding.
Impaired organ or marrow function (defined as absolute neutrophil count < 1,000/mcL, platelets < 100,000/mcL, hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dl, INR/PT/aPTT > 1.5 x ULN unless on anticoagulants, total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN (or > 2.0 x ULN for Gilbert's Syndrome), AST/ALT > 2.5 x ULN (or > 5 x ULN for liver metastases), or serum creatinine > 1.5 x ULN and creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min/ 1.73m2).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >2 for participants >=16 years of age or Lansky <50% if <16 years of age).
Poor bone marrow function (eg hemoglobin <8 g/dL, absolute neutrophil count <1.5 x 10^9/L, or platelets <1 x 10^11/L).
Liver dysfunction (eg total bilirubin >=1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal, or aspartate aminotransferase or alanine aminotransferase >=2.5 x institutional upper limit of normal).
Renal dysfunction (eg serum or plasma creatinine >=1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal and calculated creatinine clearance <50 mL/min for participants >=18 years old or <60 mL/min/1.73m^2 for patients 12-17 years).
Uncontrolled HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C infection.
Unstable or progressing brain metastases.
Cardiac dysfunction (eg New York Heart Association Functional Classification class >2B, history of myocardial infarction or unstable angina within 6 months, syncope of cardiovascular etiology, ventricular arrhythmia of pathological origin, or sudden cardiac arrest).
Treatment with strong inhibitors or strong inducers of CYP3A4, substrates of CYP1A2 with narrow therapeutic windows, or proton pump inhibitors.
Active infection requiring systemic therapy.
Interstitial lung disease or severe dyspnea at rest or requiring oxygen therapy.
Severe renal impairment (eg estimated creatinine clearance <30ml/min).
History of major surgical resection involving the stomach or small bowel, preexisting Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, or preexisting chronic condition resulting in baseline grade 2 or higher diarrhea.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
QTcF >= 450 msec on screening electrocardiogram.
Treatment with Factor Xa inhibitors or Factor IIa inhibitors.
Radiation to > 25% of the bone marrow.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) < 50%.
Inadequate organ function (hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL, absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mm3, platelets < 100,000/mm3, total bilirubin > 1.5 institutional ULN unless liver metastases or Gilbert syndrome are present, AST/ALT > 2.5 x institutional ULN unless liver metastases are present, or serum creatinine > 1.5 mg/dL or glomerular filtration rate < 30 ml/min).
Visceral crisis or impending visceral crisis.
CNS complications requiring urgent neurosurgical intervention or indication for immediate local therapy to CNS lesions.
Symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage or two or more seizures within 4 weeks prior to registration.
Ongoing or persistent toxicities caused by previous anti-cancer therapy that have not improved to Grade <= 1.
Known contraindication to MRI (unless head CT is used as an alternative for cohorts A and B).
Concurrent administration of other anti-cancer therapy.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (including active infection, severely compromised pulmonary function, unstable angina pectoris, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, active ischemic heart disease, myocardial infarction within the previous six months, gastric or duodenal ulceration diagnosed within the previous six months, chronic liver or renal disease, or severe malnutrition).
Requirement for ongoing systemic treatment with corticosteroids (> 4 mg daily dexamethasone or bioequivalent) or other immunosuppressive medications.
History of non-infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis that required steroids, current interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis, or suspected interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis that cannot be ruled out by imaging.
History of uncontrolled seizures, CNS disorders, or psychiatric disability that adversely affects compliance.
History of malignancy other than breast cancer, except for adequately resected non-melanoma skin cancer, curatively treated in situ disease, or other solid tumors curatively treated with no evidence of disease for >= 3 years.
Clinically significant corneal disease.
Severe hypersensitivity reactions to datopotamab deruxtecan, its inactive ingredients, or other monoclonal antibodies.
Pregnancy or lactation.
