clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: at least 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Histology allowed: World Health Organization Grade IV IDH wildtype glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent glioblastoma (first or second relapse) with unequivocal tumor progression by MRI scan per modified RANO criteria, excluding tumors primarily localized to the brainstem or spinal cord, and excluding diffuse leptomeningeal disease or extracranial disease. Prior treatment required: Initial therapy (radiation and/or chemotherapy) and no more than 2 total prior therapies. Prior treatment excluded: PARP inhibitors (e.g. olaparib or niraparib) and anti-VEGF targeted agents (e.g. bevacizumab or cediranib or aflibercept or vandetanib or cabozantinib or sunitinib) and anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 or anti-PD-L2 or anti-CD137 or anti-CTLA-4 antibodies and viral therapy or vaccines for glioblastoma. Biomarkers required: IDH wildtype (assessed via immunohistochemistry and/or next-generation sequencing). Biomarkers excluded: 1p/19q co-deletion and IDH mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Karnofsky Performance Status < 70 or ECOG > 1).
History of known additional malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Inadequate hematological function (Leukocytes < 3,000/uL or absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/uL or platelet count < 100,000/uL or absolute lymphocyte count < 500/uL or hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL).
Inadequate liver function (AST or ALT > 2.5 x institutional ULN or serum bilirubin > 1.5 x institutional ULN).
Inadequate renal function (Serum creatinine > 1.5 x institutional ULN or calculated 24-hour creatinine clearance <= 50 mL/min).
Inadequate coagulation (INR > 1.5 x institutional ULN or PTT > institutional ULN).
Requirement for high dose systemic corticosteroids (dexamethasone > 2 mg/day or bioequivalent for at least 3 consecutive days within 2 weeks of start of study drug).
Prior systemic immunosuppressive treatments aside from systemic corticosteroids (e.g. methotrexate or chloroquine or azathioprine) within six months of start of study drug.
Use of enzyme-inducing anti-epileptic drugs (e.g. phenobarbital or phenytoin or fosphenytoin or primidone or carbamazepine or oxcarbazepine or eslicarbazepine or rufinamide or felbamate).
Use of strong CYP3A inhibitors or inducers.
Current use of other investigational agents.
Current use of herbal preparations (e.g. cannabinoids or St. John's wort or kava or ephedra or gingko biloba or dehydroepiandrosterone or yohimbe or saw palmetto or ginseng).
Current use of warfarin sodium or other coumadin-derivative anticoagulants.
History of allergic reactions attributed to olaparib or temozolomide or pembrolizumab.
History of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukemia.
History of significant intratumoral or peritumoral hemorrhage.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (including ongoing or active infection, chronic liver disease, chronic renal disease, pancreatitis, chronic pulmonary disease, or psychiatric illness).
Active infection requiring systemic therapy or known viral infection (e.g. HIV or hepatitis B or hepatitis C).
Known history of active tuberculosis.
Diagnosis of immunodeficiency.
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years.
Psychiatric or substance abuse disorders that interfere with cooperation with trial requirements.
Live vaccine within 30 days prior to start of study treatment.
Diarrhea >= CTCAE grade 2.
Active cardiac disease (e.g. angina pectoris requiring medication, ventricular arrhythmias, supraventricular and nodal arrhythmias requiring pacemaker or uncontrolled with medication, conduction abnormality requiring pacemaker, valvular disease with compromised cardiac function, symptomatic pericarditis, or unstable ischemia).
QTc prolongation > 500 ms.
Cardiac dysfunction (e.g. myocardial infarction within the last 6 months, congestive heart failure NYHA Class III-IV, documented cardiomyopathy, or congenital long QT syndrome).
Impairment of gastrointestinal function or GI disease that may significantly alter the absorption of olaparib and temozolomide.
Major systemic surgery < 4 weeks prior to starting study drug.
Known history of coagulopathy that increases risk of bleeding or history of clinically significant hemorrhage within 12 months of start of study drug or gastrointestinal bleeding or other hemorrhage CTCAE Grade > 3 within 6 months of start of study drug.
Known history of or evidence of active non-infectious pneumonitis.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Karnofsky Performance Status < 70 or ECOG > 1).
History of known additional malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Inadequate hematological function (Leukocytes < 3,000/uL or absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/uL or platelet count < 100,000/uL or absolute lymphocyte count < 500/uL or hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL).
Inadequate liver function (AST or ALT > 2.5 x institutional ULN or serum bilirubin > 1.5 x institutional ULN).
Inadequate renal function (Serum creatinine > 1.5 x institutional ULN or calculated 24-hour creatinine clearance <= 50 mL/min).
Inadequate coagulation (INR > 1.5 x institutional ULN or PTT > institutional ULN).
Requirement for high dose systemic corticosteroids (dexamethasone > 2 mg/day or bioequivalent for at least 3 consecutive days within 2 weeks of start of study drug).
Prior systemic immunosuppressive treatments aside from systemic corticosteroids (e.g. methotrexate or chloroquine or azathioprine) within six months of start of study drug.
Use of enzyme-inducing anti-epileptic drugs (e.g. phenobarbital or phenytoin or fosphenytoin or primidone or carbamazepine or oxcarbazepine or eslicarbazepine or rufinamide or felbamate).
Use of strong CYP3A inhibitors or inducers.
Current use of other investigational agents.
Current use of herbal preparations (e.g. cannabinoids or St. John's wort or kava or ephedra or gingko biloba or dehydroepiandrosterone or yohimbe or saw palmetto or ginseng).
Current use of warfarin sodium or other coumadin-derivative anticoagulants.
History of allergic reactions attributed to olaparib or temozolomide or pembrolizumab.
History of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukemia.
History of significant intratumoral or peritumoral hemorrhage.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (including ongoing or active infection, chronic liver disease, chronic renal disease, pancreatitis, chronic pulmonary disease, or psychiatric illness).
Active infection requiring systemic therapy or known viral infection (e.g. HIV or hepatitis B or hepatitis C).
Known history of active tuberculosis.
Diagnosis of immunodeficiency.
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years.
Psychiatric or substance abuse disorders that interfere with cooperation with trial requirements.
Live vaccine within 30 days prior to start of study treatment.
Diarrhea >= CTCAE grade 2.
Active cardiac disease (e.g. angina pectoris requiring medication, ventricular arrhythmias, supraventricular and nodal arrhythmias requiring pacemaker or uncontrolled with medication, conduction abnormality requiring pacemaker, valvular disease with compromised cardiac function, symptomatic pericarditis, or unstable ischemia).
QTc prolongation > 500 ms.
Cardiac dysfunction (e.g. myocardial infarction within the last 6 months, congestive heart failure NYHA Class III-IV, documented cardiomyopathy, or congenital long QT syndrome).
Impairment of gastrointestinal function or GI disease that may significantly alter the absorption of olaparib and temozolomide.
Major systemic surgery < 4 weeks prior to starting study drug.
Known history of coagulopathy that increases risk of bleeding or history of clinically significant hemorrhage within 12 months of start of study drug or gastrointestinal bleeding or other hemorrhage CTCAE Grade > 3 within 6 months of start of study drug.
Known history of or evidence of active non-infectious pneumonitis.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: at least 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Histology allowed: IDH wildtype diffuse glioma with molecular features of glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent glioblastoma (first or second relapse) with unequivocal tumor progression by MRI scan per modified RANO criteria, surgically resectable disease at progression (greater than 2 cubic centimeters enhancing tissue available for resection), excluding tumors primarily localized to the brainstem or spinal cord, and excluding diffuse leptomeningeal disease or extracranial disease. Prior treatment required: Initial therapy (radiation and/or chemotherapy) and no more than 2 total prior therapies. Prior treatment excluded: PARP inhibitors (e.g. olaparib or niraparib) and anti-VEGF targeted agents (e.g. bevacizumab or cediranib or aflibercept or vandetanib or cabozantinib or sunitinib) and anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 or anti-PD-L2 or anti-CD137 or anti-CTLA-4 antibodies and viral therapy or vaccines for glioblastoma. Biomarkers required: IDH wildtype (assessed via immunohistochemistry and/or next-generation sequencing) and (EGFR amplification or TERT promoter mutation or [concurrent gain of Chromosome 7 and loss of Chromosome 10]). Biomarkers excluded: 1p/19q co-deletion and IDH mutation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: renal cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: node-positive unresectable (TxN1Mx) or metastatic (TxNxM1) and IMDC intermediate risk (1-2 factors) or IMDC poor risk (>= 3 factors) and primary renal tumor measuring 20 cm or less in anterior to posterior dimension and measurable disease (node positive or metastatic) excluding the primary renal tumor. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any systemic therapy for metastatic renal cell carcinoma initiated more than 90 days before registration and prior radiotherapy to the kidney that would result in overlap of radiation therapy fields treatment of the primary tumor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Karnofsky performance status < 60).
Low hemoglobin (< 8 g/dL).
Low platelet count (< 50,000/mm^3).
Low absolute neutrophil count (< 1500/mm^3).
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min).
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin >= 1.5 x upper limit of normal, except subjects with Gilbert Syndrome who can have total bilirubin < 3.0 mg/dL, or Aspartate aminotransferase and alanine aminotransferase >= 3 x upper limit of normal, or >= 5 x upper limit of normal if hepatic metastases are present).
Untreated or unstable brain metastases or cranial epidural disease.
Active autoimmune disease requiring ongoing therapy including systemic treatment with corticosteroids (> 10 mg daily prednisone equivalents) or other immunosuppressive medications daily.
History of severe allergic, anaphylactic or other hypersensitivity reactions to chimeric or humanized antibodies.
Active tuberculosis.
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic blood pressure >= 190 mmHg or diastolic blood pressure > 110 mmHg).
Major surgery requiring hospital admission within 28 days prior to registration.
Any serious non-healing wound, ulcer, or bone fracture requiring hospital stay or long term rehab within 45 days prior to registration.
Any arterial thrombotic events (e.g., ST elevation myocardial infarction, non-ST elevation myocardial infarction, cerebrovascular accident) within 180 days prior to registration.
Active New York Heart Association class 3-4 heart failure symptoms.
Moderate or severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B or C).
Any history of untreated pulmonary embolism or deep venous thrombosis within 180 days prior to registration.
Unstable cardiac arrhythmia within 180 days prior to registration.
History of abdominal fistula, gastrointestinal perforation, intra-abdominal abscess, bowel obstruction, or gastric outlet obstruction within 180 days prior to registration.
History of or active inflammatory bowel disease.
Malabsorption syndrome within 45 days prior to registration.
Pregnancy or individuals unwilling to discontinue nursing.
Planned treatment of all metastatic disease with definitive therapy rendering the patient without extra-renal measurable disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 29 days to 21 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia (excluding acute promyelocytic leukemia and juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia). Histology allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: untreated first relapse and unable to receive additional anthracycline containing chemotherapy per investigator discretion, and no isolated central nervous system 3 disease and no symptomatic central nervous system 3 disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: FLT3/internal tandem duplication (ITD) mutation negative. Biomarkers excluded: FLT3/internal tandem duplication (ITD) mutation positive.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance or radioisotope glomerular filtration rate < 60ml/min/1.73 m^2 or abnormal serum creatinine based on age/sex).
Liver dysfunction (Direct bilirubin >= 1.5 x upper limit of normal, or alkaline phosphatase > 2.5 x upper limit of normal, or alanine aminotransferase > 2.5 x upper limit of normal, unless elevation up to 5x upper limit of normal is due to radiographically identifiable leukemia infiltrate or steatosis on echography).
Cardiac dysfunction (History of congestive heart failure in need of medical treatment, or diminished left ventricular function on echocardiography with shortening fraction < 25% or ejection fraction < 40%, or signs of congestive heart failure at presentation of relapse).
Participants with Down syndrome.
Participants with Fanconi anemia, Kostmann syndrome, Shwachman syndrome, or any other known congenital bone marrow failure syndrome.
Documented active, uncontrolled infection at the time of study entry.
Hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection, hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection (HBsAg positive), or human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection.
Malabsorption syndrome or any other condition that precludes enteral administration of venetoclax.
Currently receiving an investigational drug other than those specified for this study.
Known prior allergy to any of the medications used in protocol therapy.
Pregnancy or breast-feeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 65 years old or older. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Histology allowed: World Health Organization grade 4 glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: Newly diagnosed. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior head and neck radiation or cranial radiation or radiation to any other site resulting in significant overlap in radiation fields or systemic or radiosensitizing therapy for a prior head and neck or central nervous system malignancy or investigational cancer drug for glioblastoma or non-surgical local or field treatment overlapping with the anticipated radiation field. Biomarkers required: IDH wild-type assessed by immunohistochemistry or sequencing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2)
Inadequate hematological function (eg platelet count < 100 x 10^9/L)
Renal dysfunction (eg serum creatinine > 1.5 times the upper limit of normal)
Liver dysfunction (eg total serum bilirubin > 1.5 times the upper limit of normal or ALT > 2.5 times the upper limit of normal or AST > 2.5 times the upper limit of normal)
Recurrent glioma
Spinal, leptomeningeal, or more distant disease
Another active central nervous system malignancy requiring treatment
Second invasive malignancy that is incompletely treated, requires ongoing treatment, or is reasonably anticipated to be associated with a median overall survival of less than 1 year
Major medical illnesses or psychiatric treatments that prevent administration or completion of protocol therapy
Inadequate mental capacity to provide informed consent
Inability to receive gadolinium
Serious active infection or other acute medical conditions that impair the ability to receive protocol treatment

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Adults. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: chronic graft-versus-host disease. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Moderate to severe active disease. Prior treatment required: allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant and at least 2 prior lines of systemic therapy for chronic graft-versus-host disease. Prior treatment excluded: colony-stimulating factor 1 receptor (CSF1R) inhibitors for chronic graft-versus-host disease. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Karnofsky Performance Scale <60).
Inadequate organ function.
Inadequate bone marrow function.
Uncontrolled infection.
Severe illness.
Malabsorption syndrome.
History of malignancy other than the underlying malignancy for which the transplant was performed or a malignancy treated with curative intent with no evidence of active disease present for more than 3 years prior to enrollment and felt to be at low risk for recurrence.
Failure to follow contraception requirements for participants of reproductive potential.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: cutaneous melanoma. Histology allowed: cutaneous melanoma (excluding mucosal melanoma and uveal or choroidal melanoma). Cancer burden allowed: AJCC 8th edition Stage III (defined as Tx or T1-4 and (N1b or N1c or N2b or N2c or N3b or N3c)) or Stage IV oligometastatic disease (defined as M1a or M1b). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: IDO inhibitor or anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 or anti-PD-L2 or anti-CD137 (including nivolumab or pembrolizumab or ipilimumab/nivolumab combination). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Inadequate organ function.
Systemic immunosuppression for active autoimmune disease (eg corticosteroids > 10mg daily prednisone equivalent).
Grade 3 or higher immune-related adverse events with prior immunotherapy (unless resolved or non-clinically significant lab abnormalities) or Grade 4 autoimmune disorders while on prior immunotherapy.
Active central nervous system or leptomeningeal metastases (including treated stable lesions).
Known additional malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment.
Invasive cancer diagnosed and treated less than 2 years prior to current presentation.
Interstitial lung disease or active, non-infectious pneumonitis.
Active infection requiring systemic therapy.
Psychiatric or substance abuse disorders.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Known history of Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV).
Active hepatitis B virus or hepatitis C virus infection.

Uncontrolled intercurrent illness.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor for which PARP inhibitors are approved as standard-of-care. Histology allowed: Solid tumor. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable. Prior treatment required: PARP inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Pathogenic mutation in BRCA1 or BRCA2 or PALB2 or RAD51C or RAD51D or ATM or BARD1 or BLM or BRIP1 or CDK12 or FANCA or FANCC or FANCD2 or FANCE or FANCF or FANCM or MRE11A or NBN (NBS1) or RAD50 or RAD51B. Biomarkers excluded: UGT1A1 homozygous A(TA)7TAA (*28) or UGT1A1 homozygous G71R (*6) or UGT1A1 compound alterations of *28 and *6 (assessed during screening).
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Poor bone marrow function (Absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mcL or leukocytes < 3,000/mcL or platelets < 100,000/mcL).
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin >= 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal or AST or ALT >= 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal or acute liver disease or poorly controlled liver disease or cirrhosis).
Renal dysfunction (GFR < 60 mL/min).
Cardiac dysfunction (QTcF >= 480 ms or New York Heart Association Functional Classification class 3 or higher).
Active or poorly controlled brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease (unless asymptomatic and immediate CNS treatment is not required).
Uncontrolled HIV, hepatitis B virus, or hepatitis C virus infection.
Residual toxicities from prior anti-cancer therapy > grade 1 (except alopecia).
Concurrent use of other investigational agents.
History of allergic reactions to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to novobiocin.
Use of strong CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or inducers.
Use of herbal or dietary supplements with known hepatotoxicity risk.
Concurrent medications associated with a risk of corrected QT interval (QTc) prolongation and/or Torsades de Pointes.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness.
Active or poorly controlled alcohol use disorder.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

Symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA Classes II-IV), unstable angina within the past 6 months, or cardiac arrhythmia requiring antiarrhythmic treatment. Resting systolic blood pressure greater than 180 mmHg or diastolic blood pressure greater than 110 mmHg. Active Hepatitis B and/or Hepatitis C infection. Known HIV infection that is not well controlled. Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1). Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

Prior treatment required: at least two prior systemic therapies including 1 selective KRAS-G12C-targeted therapy. Prior treatment excluded: targeted therapy for an actionable genomic alteration other than KRAS-G12C, anti-HER3 antibody, or antibody drug conjugate that consists of an exatecan derivative that is a topoisomerase I inhibitor. Biomarkers required: KRAS-G12C mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Small cell histology or combined small cell and non-small cell histology. History of other active malignancy within 3 years prior to enrollment (except adequately treated non-melanoma skin cancer, superficial bladder tumors Ta, Tis, T1, or curatively treated in situ disease). Spinal cord compression or clinically active central nervous system metastases. History of myocardial infarction within the past 6 months. Symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA Classes II-IV), unstable angina within the past 6 months, or cardiac arrhythmia requiring antiarrhythmic treatment. Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50%. Clinically important abnormalities in rhythm, conduction or morphology of resting electrocardiogram. Corrected QT interval (QTcF) prolongation greater than 470 ms for females or greater than 450 ms for males. Factors that increase the risk of corrected QT interval prolongation or arrhythmic events. History of, current, or suspected interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis. Clinically significant corneal disease. Leptomeningeal disease. Clinically severe respiratory compromise. Chronic systemic corticosteroids dose greater than 10 mg/day prednisone or equivalent anti-inflammatory activity or any form of immunosuppressive therapy. Resting systolic blood pressure greater than 180 mmHg or diastolic blood pressure greater than 110 mmHg. Active Hepatitis B and/or Hepatitis C infection. Known HIV infection that is not well controlled. Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1). Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 - 30 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Neuroblastoma or Ganglioneuroblastoma. Histology allowed: Neuroblastoma or Ganglioneuroblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: High-risk disease that is relapsed or refractory, excluding isolated bone marrow only disease. Prior treatment required: Completion of frontline high-risk therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Naxitamab or allogeneic stem cell transplant or irinotecan or temozolomide discontinued due to unacceptable toxicity or GD2 directed immunotherapy discontinued due to unacceptable toxicity other than allergic reaction. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (e.g., Karnofsky < 70% for patients aged 16 years or older, or Lansky < 70% for patients younger than 16 years).
Inadequate bone marrow function (Peripheral absolute neutrophil count < 750/uL or Peripheral platelet count < 75,000/uL).
Inadequate renal function (Radioisotope GFR < 70ml/min/1.73 m2 or serum creatinine exceeding age and sex-based thresholds).
Inadequate liver function (Total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN, ALT > 3.0 x ULN, or Albumin <= 3 g/dL).
Inadequate cardiac function (Shortening fraction < 27% and Ejection fraction < 50%).
Poor blood pressure control (Grade 2 or higher hypertension or requiring more than one standing antihypertensive).
Inadequate pulmonary function (dyspnea at rest, exercise intolerance, room air O2 saturation <= 94%, or requiring chronic oxygen therapy).
Inadequate pancreatic function (lipase >= 1.5 x ULN).
Chronic diarrhea (more than 2 weeks duration) greater than grade 1.
Untreated central nervous system metastatic disease.
Pregnancy or currently breast feeding.
Clinically significant arrhythmias.
Prior allergic reaction to irinotecan or temozolomide.
Serious intercurrent illness.
Active uncontrolled infection.
Existing major organ dysfunction CTCAE > Grade 2.
Receipt of immunosuppressive medications including pharmacologic doses of glucocorticoids or immunomodulatory agents.
Concurrent receipt of anti-cancer agents or radiotherapy.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Current or history of central nervous system involvement by B-cell malignancy including the brain, spinal cord, leptomeninges, and cerebrospinal fluid.
Use of systemic corticosteroids (greater than or equal to 10 mg/day of prednisone or equivalent).
Prior malignancy within the past 2 years (except in situ malignancies curatively resected, localized breast cancer treated with curative intent with no evidence of active disease for more than 3 years and receiving adjuvant hormonal therapy, localized Gleason score less than or equal to 6 prostate cancer undergoing observation or androgen deprivation, or other cancer treated with curative intent not on adjuvant treatment unlikely to recur).
Ongoing systemic treatment for any other malignancy.
Known active plasma cell neoplasm, prolymphocytic leukemia, T-cell lymphoma, Burkitt lymphoma, acquired immunodeficiency syndrome-related B-cell lymphoma, Castleman disease, post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorders, or hairy cell leukemia.
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma subtypes including EBV+ diffuse large B-cell lymphoma not otherwise specified, primary central nervous system diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, primary cutaneous diffuse large B-cell lymphoma leg type, diffuse large B-cell lymphoma associated with chronic inflammation, primary mediastinal large B-cell lymphoma, intravascular large B-cell lymphoma, ALK+ large B-cell lymphoma, primary effusion lymphoma, high-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangements, high-grade B-cell lymphoma not otherwise specified, or B-cell lymphoma unclassifiable with features intermediate between diffuse large B-cell lymphoma and classical Hodgkin lymphoma.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1).
Bone marrow suppression (leukocytes < 3,000/mcL or absolute neutrophil count < 1,000/mcL or hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL or platelets < 100,000/mcL).
Liver dysfunction (total bilirubin > 2.0 g/dL or AST/ALT > 2.5x institutional upper limit of normal).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance < 50 mL/min/1.73 m2).
Left systolic ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) < 50%.
Active or uncontrolled infection.
Pregnant or lactating women.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, or cardiac arrhythmia.
Psychiatric illness or social situations that limit compliance.
Known additional malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment.

Major surgery within 4 weeks of study entry or planned during study.
Ongoing anticancer therapy.
Ongoing radiation therapy.
Uncontrolled or active clinically relevant bacterial, fungal, or specific viral infection requiring systemic therapy.
Symptomatic spinal cord compression.

Pregnant or breastfeeding females.
Actively receiving systemic treatment or direct medical intervention on another therapeutic clinical study.
Uncontrolled brain metastases (active central nervous system metastases or primary central nervous system tumors associated with progressive neurological symptoms or needing increased doses of corticosteroids).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 1 year and < 22 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: B-acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease with >= 5% bone marrow blasts (M2 or M3) and (second or greater relapse or primary refractory disease with at least 2 prior induction attempts or first relapse refractory to at least one prior re-induction attempt) and without Down syndrome and without prior hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: inotuzumab ozogamicin. Biomarkers required: CD22 surface expression (assessed during screening at time of relapse). Biomarkers excluded: Philadelphia chromosome.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance < 70 mL/min/1.73 m^2 or serum creatinine exceeding age-specific maximums).
Liver dysfunction (Direct bilirubin >= 1.5 x ULN or ALT >= 5 x ULN).
Known human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), hepatitis B, or hepatitis C infections.
Prior history of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (SOS).
Active optic nerve and/or retinal involvement.
Currently receiving another investigational drug or other anti-cancer agents (except hydroxyurea and intrathecal chemotherapy).
Receiving agents to prevent graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) or organ rejection post-transplant (e.g., cyclosporine, tacrolimus).
Active uncontrolled infection (positive bacterial blood culture within 48 hours, fever > 38.2 Celsius with signs of infection, positive fungal culture within 30 days, or active viral/protozoal infection requiring IV treatment).
Concomitant genetic syndromes (Bloom syndrome, ataxia-telangiectasia, Fanconi anemia, Kostmann syndrome, Schwachman-Diamond-Blackfan syndrome, or other known bone marrow failure syndromes).
Pregnancy or lactating females.
Isolated central nervous system (CNS), testicular, or other extramedullary site of relapse.
History of allergic reaction to compounds of similar biologic composition to inotuzumab ozogamicin.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: non-small cell lung cancer (excluding small cell lung cancer, mixed tumors with small cell elements, neuroendocrine tumors with large-cell components, sarcomatoid carcinoma, and pancoast tumors). Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Resectable Stage II, Stage IIIA, or Stage IIIB (N2) and did not achieve a pathological complete response after neoadjuvant therapy. Prior treatment required: Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab and platinum-based doublet chemotherapy followed by surgery. Prior treatment excluded: Prior neoadjuvant therapy other than pembrolizumab and platinum-based doublet chemotherapy or prior anti-programmed cell death 1 (PD-1) agent other than pembrolizumab or prior anti-programmed cell death ligand 1 (PD-L1) agent or prior anti-programmed cell-death ligand 2 (PD-L2) agent or prior agent directed to another stimulatory or coinhibitory T-cell receptor (such as cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated protein [CTLA-4], OX-40, or CD137) or prior systemic anticancer therapy other than what is specified in the protocol or prior treatment with a cancer vaccine. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Tumor-activating epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) mutations (such as DEL19 or L858R) or anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK) gene rearrangements.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1).
Uncontrolled Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection.
Hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg) positive without antiviral therapy or with detectable HBV viral load.
Hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection with detectable HCV viral load at screening.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 year or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: primary immunodeficiency lymphoproliferative disease. Histology allowed: Epstein-Barr virus-positive lymphoproliferative disease. Cancer burden allowed: systemic disease only, systemic and central nervous system disease, or central nervous system disease only. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplant or solid organ transplant. Biomarkers required: Epstein-Barr virus-positive (confirmed by biopsy or positive cerebrospinal fluid cytology). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 3 for participants aged >= 16 years or Lansky score < 20 for participants from >= 1 year to < 16 years).
Inadequate organ function tests.
Currently active Burkitt lymphoma, T-cell lymphoma, natural killer/T-cell lymphoma/lymphoproliferative disease, Hodgkin lymphoma, plasmablastic lymphoma, or transformed lymphoma.
Active hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.
Other malignancies requiring systemic therapy.
Serious known active infections (eg uncontrolled adenovirus infection or infections requiring systemic therapy).
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection.
Grade >= 2 acute graft-versus-host disease or extensive chronic graft-versus-host disease.
Need for vasopressor or ventilatory support.
Any prior treatment with Epstein-Barr virus-cytotoxic T lymphocytes (EBV-CTLs), except tabelecleucel.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Unwillingness to comply with contraceptive or reproductive restrictions.
Ongoing need for daily steroids (> 0.5 mg/kg prednisone or equivalent), ongoing methotrexate, or extracorporeal photopheresis.
Life expectancy < 60 days or any life-threatening illness, medical condition, or organ system dysfunction.

Inadequate organ function tests.

Active hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.
Other malignancies requiring systemic therapy.
Serious known active infections (eg uncontrolled adenovirus infection or infections requiring systemic therapy).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Any solid tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic. general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Known or suspected history of untreated or uncontrolled central nervous system (CNS) involvement.
History of increased risk of prolonged QT or significant arrhythmia.
Significant cardiovascular disease.
Active and/or treated for an additional primary malignancy within 2 years prior to enrolment.
Pregnant, breastfeeding, planning to breastfeed, expecting to conceive, or fathering children during study or within 6 months after the last dose of study intervention.
History of active autoimmune diseases, history of allogenic stem cell/organ transplant, or compromised immune system within past 2 years.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 6 months to 21 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: completion of planned upfront treatment. Prior treatment excluded: prior EZH2 inhibitors and anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 or anti-PD-L2 or anti-CTLA4 agent or agent directed to another stimulatory or co-inhibitory T-cell receptor (eg OX40 or CD137) and allogeneic stem cell transplant. Biomarkers required: Loss of INI1 confirmed by immunohistochemistry (IHC) assessed during screening or molecular confirmation of tumor bi-allelic SMARCB1 (INI1) loss or mutation assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of non-infectious pneumonitis that required steroids or current pneumonitis.
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 12 months or documented history of clinically severe autoimmune disease.
Known history of HIV, hepatitis B, and/or hepatitis C.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including ongoing or active infection.
Poor performance status (Karnofsky < 50% for subjects >= 16 years of age or Lansky < 50% for subjects < 16 years of age).
Inadequate bone marrow function (Absolute neutrophil count < 1,000/uL, Hemoglobin < 8 g/dL, Platelets <= 75K/uL for non-relapsed subjects or <= 50K/uL for relapsed subjects, or platelet transfusion dependence).
Inadequate hepatic function (Total bilirubin > 1.5 x upper limit of normal, ALT or AST > 3 x upper limit of normal).
Inadequate renal function (Serum creatinine above age/gender maximums or Creatinine clearance < 70 mL/min/1.73 m2).
Inadequate pulmonary function (dyspnea at rest, exercise intolerance, or pulse oximetry <= 92% while breathing room air).
Inadequate neurologic function (uncontrolled seizure disorder or Nervous system disorders > Grade 2 resulting from prior therapy).
Pregnancy or breast-feeding.
Prior solid organ transplantation.
Prior history of myeloid malignancies including myelodysplastic syndrome or prior history of lymphoblastic lymphoma or leukemia.
Current use of drugs that are strong or moderate inducers or inhibitors of CYP3A4.
Screening CBC differential showing morphologic abnormalities concerning for MPN/MDS or ALL.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: adults. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: advanced and unresectable or metastatic. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Locally advanced solid tumor that is a candidate for curative treatment through radical surgery and/or radiotherapy, or chemotherapy.
History of another malignancy (with some exceptions).
History of lymphohistiocytic or lymphoid hyperplasia; hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.
Failed to recover from effects of prior anticancer treatment therapy to baseline or Grade <= 1 severity.
Clinically significant cardiovascular event within 6 months prior to start of treatment.
Known active central nervous system metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis.
Active interstitial lung disease currently requiring treatment.
History of uveitis, retinopathy or other clinically significant retinal disease.
Active or chronic corneal disorders, other active ocular conditions requiring ongoing therapy, or any clinically significant corneal disease.
Active wound healing from major surgery within 1 month or minor surgery within 10 days before first dose.
Known human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), active hepatitis B or C infection.
Prior investigative treatment with a selective or nonselective CDK2 inhibitor or degrader.
Childs-Pugh class B or C cirrhosis or any other clinically significant liver disorder.
Palliative radiation therapy within 14 days or other radiation therapy within 4 weeks prior to treatment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: melanoma. Histology allowed: histological or cytological confirmation of melanoma. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Pregnant or breast-feeding
Active central nervous system metastases
Other current malignancies (except basal cell skin cancer, squamous cell skin cancer, in situ cervical cancer, ductal or lobular carcinoma in situ of the breast, or malignancies that have been disease-free for > 3 years)
Poor hematologic function (eg white blood count < 3,000/uL, absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/uL, platelet count < 100,000/uL, or hemoglobin < 9 g/dL)
Renal dysfunction (eg serum creatinine >= 1.5 x upper limit of normal and serum creatinine clearance < 40 ml/min)
Liver dysfunction (eg AST or ALT >= 3 x upper limit of normal, alkaline phosphatase >= 2 x upper limit of normal, or total bilirubin >= 1.5 x upper limit of normal)
Elevated serum lactate dehydrogenase (LDH >= 10 X upper limit of normal)
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection
Active hepatitis B virus (HBV) or hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection
Autoimmune disorders or conditions of immunosuppression requiring current ongoing treatment with systemic corticosteroids or other systemic immunosuppressants
History of symptomatic autoimmune disease (eg rheumatoid arthritis, systemic progressive sclerosis, systemic lupus erythematosus, Sjogren's syndrome, autoimmune vasculitis, motor neuropathy of autoimmune origin, or CNS autoimmune disease)
History of inflammatory bowel disease or diverticulitis
Serious or unstable pre-existing medical conditions
Receipt of live vaccines
Administration of steroids for management of brain metastases within 14 days of randomization

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia. Histology allowed: lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: meeting criteria for initiation of treatment per IWWM2 criteria (hyperviscosity syndrome or peripheral neuropathy or cold agglutinin disease or cryoglobulinemia or amyloidosis or cytopenias due to bone marrow infiltration or symptomatic or bulky lymph nodes or symptomatic splenomegaly or constitutional symptoms or signs of organ dysfunction secondary to Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia) and disease must not have transformed to aggressive lymphoma. Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of treatment that was discontinued due to intolerance or disease progression, which must have included an anti-CD20 antibody and a BTK inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: CD20+ assessed during screening by immunophenotype or immunohistochemistry confirmed by bone marrow biopsy/aspirate and serum IgM level > 2x upper limit of normal. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Active (PCR-positive) Hepatitis B virus or Hepatitis C virus infection.
Unrecovered adverse events from prior anti-cancer therapy (residual toxicities > Grade 1 except for alopecia and peripheral neuropathy).
Uncontrolled intercurrent active infection requiring hospitalization or intravenous antimicrobial agents within 4 weeks of start of treatment.
Uncontrolled underlying cardiac conditions including congestive heart failure grade III or IV, ejection fraction < 45%, unstable angina pectoris, acute myocardial infarction < 6 months, or uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia.
History of uncontrolled neurologic condition including seizure disorder, stroke, psychosis, dementia, CNS vasculitis, or encephalitis.
Need for supplemental oxygen at rest to maintain SaO2 > 92%.
Chronic immunosuppressive therapy for non-Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia-related indication within 28 days of initiation of treatment, including systemic corticosteroids 20 mg/day or greater prednisone-equivalent.
Known or suspected CNS involvement or leptomeningeal disease (BingNeel Syndrome).
Pregnant or breastfeeding women.
Known current alcohol or drug abuse, psychiatric illness, or unstable social situation.
History of allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to epcoritamab.
Exposure to a live or live attenuated vaccine within 4 weeks.
Symptomatic or suspected hyperviscosity syndrome or IgM levels greater than 4000 mg/dL and unable to undergo plasmapheresis.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Short life expectancy (less than 12 weeks).
Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50%.
Inadequate organ and bone marrow function.
Uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease.
Corrected QT interval prolongation.
History of non-infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis that required steroids, current interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis, or suspected interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis that cannot be ruled out by imaging.
Spinal cord compression or clinically active central nervous system metastases.
Multiple primary malignancies within 3 years, except adequately resected non-melanoma skin cancer, curatively treated in situ disease, other solid tumors curatively treated, or contralateral breast cancer.
Severe hypersensitivity reactions to drug substances, inactive ingredients, or other monoclonal antibodies.
Uncontrolled infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, antivirals, or antifungals.
Active primary immunodeficiency, known uncontrolled active human immunodeficiency virus infection, or active hepatitis B or C infection.
History of receiving a live, attenuated vaccine within 30 days prior to first exposure to study drug.
Unresolved toxicities from previous anticancer therapy (defined as toxicities other than alopecia not yet resolved to Grade 1 or baseline).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding or planning to become pregnant.
Lung-specific intercurrent clinically significant illnesses.
Any autoimmune, connective tissue, or inflammatory disorders.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or breast-feeding.
Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) <= 50%.
Absolute neutrophil count (ANC) < 1,500 cells/uL.
Platelets < 75,000 cells/uL.
Total bilirubin >= 1.2 mg/dL.
Aspartate aminotransferase (AST) or alanine aminotransferase (ALT) > 3.0 x institutional upper limit of normal.
Creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min.
Uncontrolled HIV infection (viral load detectable).
Uncontrolled hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection (viral load detectable).
Untreated or active hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection (viral load detectable).
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease requiring immediate central nervous system treatment.
History of or active interstitial lung disease.
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1).
Clinically significant immunodeficiency or autoimmune disorder requiring chronic systemic steroids or systemic steroids within 7 days prior to randomization.
Known history of active tuberculosis.
Hypersensitivity to doxorubicin, pembrolizumab, or their excipients.

general_exclusion_criteria: Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1). Untreated or unstable central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis. Receipt of organ transplantation including allogeneic stem-cell transplantation. Significant acute or chronic infections including HIV, AIDS, hepatitis B virus, or hepatitis C virus. Active autoimmune disease. Symptomatic interstitial lung disease. Uncontrolled asthma. Severe cardiac dysfunction (eg symptomatic congestive heart failure New York Heart Association > class II, unstable cardiac arrhythmia, unstable angina, uncontrolled hypertension, or low cardiac ejection fraction) or recent major cardiovascular event (eg myocardial infarction, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, cerebrovascular accident, or transient ischemic attack within 6 months). Neuromuscular disorders associated with elevated creatine kinase. History of acute or chronic pancreatitis. Retinal vein occlusion or current risk factors for retinal vein occlusion or retinal degenerative disease. Requirement of anticoagulant therapy with oral vitamin K antagonists. Severe hypersensitivity reactions to monoclonal antibodies or history of anaphylaxis. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Current alcohol or drug abuse. Incarceration. Severe psychiatric condition. Liver dysfunction (eg total serum bilirubin > 1.5 times ULN, AST or ALT > 2.5 times ULN). Renal dysfunction (eg serum creatinine > 1.5 times ULN or estimated creatinine clearance < 50 mL/min). Hematologic dysfunction (eg absolute neutrophil count < 1.0 x 10^9/L, platelets < 100 x 10^9/L, or hemoglobin < 9 g/dL).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (e.g., Karnofsky <= 50% for patients > 16 years of age or Lansky <= 50% for patients <= 16 years of age)
Renal dysfunction (e.g., calculated creatinine clearance or radioisotope GFR < 70ml/min/1.73m2 or serum creatinine above age/gender-specific thresholds)
Liver dysfunction (e.g., total bilirubin > 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal, SGPT or SGOT >= 3x institutional upper limit of normal, or serum albumin < 3.2 g/dL)
Cardiac dysfunction (e.g., shortening fraction < 27% or ejection fraction < 50%)
Pulmonary dysfunction (e.g., pulse oximetry <= 94% on room air or evidence of dyspnea at rest or exercise intolerance)
Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Unstable or increasing dose of corticosteroids for disease control within 7 days prior to enrollment
Current receipt of another investigational drug
Current receipt of other anti-cancer agents, radiation therapy, or immunotherapy (with specified exceptions in the protocol)
Receipt of cyclosporine, tacrolimus, or other anti-GVHD or organ rejection agents within 4 weeks prior to enrollment
Active infection (e.g., positive blood culture within 48 hours of enrollment, fever > 38.2C with clinical signs of infection, positive fungal culture within 30 days of enrollment, or active fungal, viral, bacterial, or protozoal infection requiring IV treatment)
Known allergy to any of the drugs used in the study
Significant concurrent disease, illness, psychiatric disorder, or social issue compromising patient safety or compliance
DNA fragility syndromes (e.g., Fanconi anemia, Bloom syndrome)
Down syndrome for patients in Part 2

general_exclusion_criteria:
- Spinal cord compression, clinically active untreated CNS metastases, and/or carcinomatous meningitis.
- Uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease (including QTcF > 470 msec, myocardial infarction within 6 months, serious cardiac arrhythmia, uncontrolled angina pectoris within 6 months, left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%, New York Heart Association Class II-IV congestive heart failure, or uncontrolled hypertension).
- Clinically severe pulmonary compromise (including pulmonary emboli, severe asthma, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, restrictive lung disease, pleural effusion, autoimmune, connective tissue or inflammatory disorders with pulmonary involvement, or prior complete pneumonectomy).
- Receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days prior to the first dose of study treatment.

Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)

Grade 2 or higher peripheral neuropathy
Active or history of autoimmune disease or inflammatory disorders (except vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, or celiac disease controlled by diet alone)
Previous malignancies (except nonmelanoma skin, in situ bladder, gastric, breast, colon, or cervical cancers/dysplasia, or those with complete remission for at least 2 years and no additional therapy required)
History of solid organ transplant or allogenic organ transplantation
Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (including ongoing infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, interstitial lung disease, serious chronic gastrointestinal conditions associated with diarrhea, or psychiatric illness)
Mean QTcF interval greater than or equal to 470 ms
Impaired organ or marrow function (including leukocytes < 3,000/mcL, absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mcL, platelets < 100,000/mcL, hemoglobin <= 9.0 g/dL, total bilirubin above institutional limits, AST or ALT > 2.5 x institutional upper limit of normal, or inadequate creatinine clearance)

Biomarkers required: pathogenic or likely pathogenic somatic or germline mutation in ATM or ATR or BARD1 or BRCA1 or BRCA2 or BRIP1 or CCNE1 or CHEK1 or CHEK2 or CDK12 or CREBBP or FANCA or FANCI or FANCL or FANC2 or FANCB or FANCC or FANCD2 or FANCE or FANCF or FANCG or FANCM or FANCP or FANCQ or ERCC4 or XPF or FANCR or RAD51 or FANCT or UBE2T or FANCU or XRCC2 or FANCV or REV7 or MAD2L2 or FANCW or RFWD3 or MRE11A or MYC or NBN or NCL or PALB2 or RAD50 or RAD51B or RAD51C or RAD51D or RAD54L or SLFN11 or PTIP or MLL3 or MLL4 or EZH2 or CtIP or RBBP8 or MUS81 or CDH4 or DYNLL11 or TOPBP1 or NBS1 or CDC25A or CDC25C or RAD17 or WEE1 (assessed during screening). general_exclusion_criteria:
Malignant bowel obstruction.
Untreated or unstable brain or meningeal metastases.
Unresolved toxicity > CTCAE grade 1 from previous anti-cancer therapy (except hematological toxicity, Grade 1 or 2 neuropathy, and alopecia).
Severe or uncontrolled diseases such as active infection, unstable or uncompensated respiratory, cardiac, neurological, hepatic, renal disease or psychiatric illness.
HIV positive with CD4+ T-cell count < 350 cells/uL.
Active hepatitis B or C.
Major thoracic or abdominal surgery (within 14 days of registration or until any wound has completely healed) or a surgical incision that is not fully healed.
Concurrent systemic anti-cancer therapy, biological therapy or other novel agents.
Known photosensitivity disorders (eg xeroderma pigmentosa, porphyria).
Pregnancy or nursing.
Ophthalmological active ocular surface disease.
History of cicatricial conjunctivitis.
Radiotherapy with a limited field for palliation within 1 week of first dose, or radiotherapy involving more than 30% of bone marrow or wide field completed less than 4 weeks prior to first dose.
Marked baseline prolongation of QT/QTc interval (> 450 ms).
Additional risk factors for TdP (eg heart failure, hypokalemia, family history of long QT syndrome).
Use of concomitant medications that prolong the QT/QTc interval.
Use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitor or inducer.
Other malignancies within 5 years except for noninvasive malignancies such as cervical carcinoma in situ, non-melanomatous carcinoma of the skin, or ductal carcinoma in situ of the breast that has been surgically cured.
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 65 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: Stage I-III and non-metastatic. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any chemotherapy for current breast cancer and past treatment with docetaxel and cyclophosphamide for prior breast cancer. Biomarkers required: human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative and known estrogen receptor status and known progesterone receptor status. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to cyclophosphamide and/or docetaxel.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years of age or older. Sex allowed: Female or male. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: Localized disease defined as absence of distant metastatic disease (AJCC TNM stage M0) and regional lymph node AJCC (v7) TNM stages N0-N2, provided that if the patient is N0, the primary tumor must be 1 cm or greater, and excluding inflammatory breast cancer and locally recurrent breast cancer. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior systemic therapy for breast cancer and systemic therapy for any other malignancy in the preceding 3 years. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor negative (ER-) and progesterone receptor negative (PR-) and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (HER2-), assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Inadequate bone marrow function (eg Absolute neutrophil count < 1,500 per mm3, platelets < 100,000 per mm3, or hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL).
Inadequate organ function (eg INR > 1.5, serum creatinine >= 1.5 mg/dL or creatinine clearance < 50 mL/min, Alanine aminotransferase or aspartate aminotransferase >= 2.5 x ULN, or total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN).
Uncontrolled inter-current illness including ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness.
Clinically significant uncontrolled heart disease or cardiac repolarization abnormality including history of angina pectoris, symptomatic pericarditis, coronary artery bypass graft or myocardial infarction within 6 months, cardiac failure, known cardiomyopathy with LVEF < 50%, significant or symptomatic bradycardia, Long QT syndrome, family history of idiopathic sudden death or congenital long QT syndrome, known risk to prolong the QT interval or induce Torsade's de Pointes, uncorrected hypomagnesemia or hypokalemia, systolic blood pressure > 160 mmHg or < 90 mmHg, heart rate < 50 at rest, or QTcF > 470 ms.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Known HIV-positive status on combination antiretroviral therapy.
Known or history of hepatitis B positive or hepatitis C positive infection.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: large B-cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, not otherwise specified. Cancer burden allowed: high-risk disease defined as an International Prognostic Index (IPI) score of 4 or 5 at initial diagnosis, and no cerebrospinal fluid-malignant cells, brain metastases, history of central nervous system involvement of lymphoma, or cardiac lymphoma involvement. Prior treatment required: 1 cycle of rituximab plus chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: any prior treatment for large B-cell lymphoma other than 1 cycle of rituximab plus chemotherapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Inadequate bone marrow, renal, hepatic, pulmonary, or cardiac function.
Pregnancy in females of childbearing potential.
History of severe immediate hypersensitivity reaction to any of the agents used in the study.
Presence of central nervous system disorder, including history of stroke, transient ischemic attack, or posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome within 12 months.
History of acute or chronic active hepatitis B or C infection.
Positive for human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) unless taking appropriate anti-HIV medications, with an undetectable viral load by PCR and with a cluster of differentiation 4 (CD4) count > 200 cells/uL.
Medical conditions or residual toxicities from prior therapies likely to interfere with assessment of safety or efficacy.
History of clinically significant cardiac disease within 12 months.
History of any medical condition requiring maintenance systemic immunosuppression or systemic disease modifying agents within the last 2 years.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: castration-resistant prostate cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease (documented M1 disease on conventional imaging or identification of bone lesion(s), extra-pelvic soft tissue lesion(s), or visceral metastases on PSMA PET imaging). Prior treatment required: androgen deprivation therapy and at least one androgen receptor signaling inhibitor (enzalutamide or apalutamide or darolutamide or abiraterone acetate). Prior treatment excluded: platinum-based chemotherapy and PARP inhibitors (olaparib or rucaparib) and Radium-223 and Actinium-225 and Strontium-89 and Samarium-153 and Rheunium-186 and Rhenium-188 and chemotherapy for castration-resistant prostate cancer and radiopharmaceutical therapy (including Radium-223, Lutetium-177-PSMA-617, or Lutetium-177-PSMA-I&T) and PSMA-targeted therapy. Biomarkers required: PSMA PET-positive disease (at least one PSMA-positive metastatic lesion and no PSMA-negative lesions), assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Superscans by nuclear medicine/99mTc bone scan.
Known malignancy other than castration-resistant prostate cancer that is progressing or has required active treatment within the past 3 years and is expected to alter life expectancy or may interfere with castration-resistant prostate cancer disease assessment.
Receiving anti-coagulants or anti-platelet drugs (e.g., aspirin or nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs) who cannot discontinue use if platelet count decreases to <50,000.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: hepatocellular carcinoma. Histology allowed: hepatocellular carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable, Child-Pugh A, Barcelona Clinic Liver Cancer stage Intermediate (B) or Advanced (C), up to 5 lesions with total maximal tumor dimension < 20 cm and no single lesion > 15 cm, and extrahepatic metastases or malignant nodes <= 2 cm in sum of maximal diameters. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: systemic therapy, radiation to the region of the liver, selective internal radiotherapy, or hepatic arterial Yttrium therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Known HIV infection.
Active co-infection of hepatitis B virus and hepatitis C virus.
Active or history of autoimmune disease or immune deficiency (excluding autoimmune-related hypothyroidism on thyroid-replacement hormone, controlled Type 1 diabetes mellitus on insulin, or well-controlled eczema, psoriasis, lichen simplex chronicus, or vitiligo with dermatologic manifestations only).
History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug-induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or evidence of active pneumonitis.
Active tuberculosis.
Significant cardiovascular disease (eg New York Heart Association Class II or greater cardiac disease, myocardial infarction, or cerebrovascular accident within 3 months, unstable arrhythmia, or unstable angina).
Significant vascular disease (eg aortic aneurysm requiring surgical repair or recent peripheral arterial thrombosis within 6 months).
Major surgical procedure, open biopsy, or significant traumatic injury within 28 days, or abdominal surgery, abdominal interventions, or significant abdominal traumatic injury within 60 days.
History of malignancy other than hepatocellular carcinoma within 5 years (excluding malignancies with negligible risk of metastasis or death such as adequately treated carcinoma in situ of the cervix, superficial bladder cancer, non-melanoma skin carcinoma, localized prostate cancer, ductal carcinoma in situ, or Stage I uterine cancer).
Severe infection within 4 weeks.
Treatment with therapeutic oral or IV antibiotics within 2 weeks.
Prior allogenic stem cell or solid organ transplantation.
Clinically significant bleeding or clotting risk requiring treatment, evidence of bleeding diathesis, or significant coagulopathy.
Current or recent use of full-dose oral or parenteral anticoagulants or thrombolytic agents for therapeutic purpose.
History of abdominal or tracheoesophageal fistula, gastrointestinal perforation, or intra-abdominal abscess within 6 months.
History of intestinal obstruction or clinical signs of gastrointestinal obstruction within 6 months.
History of intra-abdominal inflammatory process within 6 months (eg peptic ulcer disease, diverticulitis, or colitis).
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg ongoing active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, active peptic ulcer disease or gastritis, active bleeding diatheses, or psychiatric illness).
Treatment with a live, attenuated vaccine within 4 weeks.
History of severe anaphylactic reactions to chimeric or humanized antibodies or fusion proteins.
Known hypersensitivity to Chinese hamster ovary cell products or components of Atezolizumab or Bevacizumab.
Untreated or incompletely treated varices with bleeding or high risk for bleeding.
Moderate or severe ascites.
Evidence of abdominal free air not explained by paracentesis or recent surgical procedure.
Serious non-healing or dehiscing wound, active ulcer, or untreated bone fracture.
History of hepatic encephalopathy.
Symptomatic, untreated, or actively progressing central nervous system metastases.
Current or recent use of aspirin (>325 mg/day), dipyramidole, ticlopidine, clopidogrel, or cilostazol.
Chronic daily treatment with a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (excluding daily Aspirin 81 mg).
Uncontrolled tumor-related pain.
Uncontrolled pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, or ascites requiring recurrent drainage procedures.
Uncontrolled or symptomatic hypercalcemia.
Treatment with investigational therapy within 28 days.
Treatment with systemic immunostimulatory agents (eg interferon or interleukin 2) within 4 weeks or 5 half-lives of the drug.
Treatment with systemic immunosuppressive medication within 2 weeks (with exceptions for acute low-dose or one-time pulse dose, mineralocorticoids, or corticosteroids for COPD, asthma, orthostatic hypotension, or adrenal insufficiency).
Inadequately controlled arterial hypertension (systolic blood pressure >=150 mmHg and/or diastolic blood pressure >100 mmHg).
Prior history of hypertensive crisis or hypertensive encephalopathy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 12 months to less than 30 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: neuroblastoma. Histology allowed: neuroblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: high-risk according to Children's Oncology Group risk classification and relapsed or refractory disease and evaluable or measurable disease per the revised International Neuroblastoma Response Criteria. Prior treatment required: standard curative measures. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnant or nursing women
Uncontrolled active infection
Concurrent receipt of other investigational agents
Additional malignancy other than non-melanomatous skin cancer or carcinoma in situ (unless not requiring active treatment and stable or disease-free for at least 3 years)
Uncontrolled central nervous system metastasis
Central nervous system disorder such as cerebrovascular ischemia/hemorrhage, dementia, cerebellar disease, or autoimmune disease with central nervous system involvement
History of severe hypersensitivity reaction to compounds used in the study
HIV, hepatitis B virus, or hepatitis C virus infection
Systemic steroid therapy (excluding physiologic replacement, inhaled steroids, and premedication for blood products)
Primary immunodeficiency or history of systemic autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression or disease modifying agents within the last 2 years
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness
Poor performance status (eg Lansky or Karnofsky performance score <50%)
Inability to comply with the study requirements

Prior or concurrent active malignancy within the past 2 years (except curatively treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, carcinoma in situ of the cervix or breast, or localized prostate cancer)
Neuropathy attributed to an etiology other than IgM-mediated neuropathy
Concurrent administration of medications that are moderate or strong inhibitors or inducers of CYP3A
Current daily use of a proton pump inhibitor
Chronic liver disease and hepatic impairment meeting Child-Pugh class C
Use of anticoagulation with warfarin or equivalent vitamin K antagonists
Peripheral neuropathy symptoms present for > 5 years
Known central nervous system lymphoma
Active bleeding or history of bleeding diathesis
Significant cerebrovascular disease or event including stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within 6 months prior to first dose
Major surgery within 4 weeks of first dose
Malabsorption syndrome or condition precluding enteral administration
Pregnant or breastfeeding female participants
Significant cardiovascular disease such as uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias, congestive heart failure, or myocardial infarction within 6 months of screening, or New York Heart Association classification III or IV heart failure
Active Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) infection
Active infection with Hepatitis B virus (HBsAg positive or HBcAb positive with detectable HBV DNA) or active infection with Hepatitis C virus (HCV antibody positive and HCV RNA detectable)
Significant bacterial, viral, or fungal infection at study entry
Inability to swallow pills
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness/social situations limiting compliance
Renal dysfunction (eg estimated GFR < 30 mL/min)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: < 50 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: Osteosarcoma. Histology allowed: Osteosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: Pulmonary metastases (limited to the lungs with no extrapulmonary metastatic disease and no chest wall or mediastinal based metastatic lesions) consisting of 4 or fewer nodules per lung where at least one nodule is 3 mm or larger and all nodules are 3 cm or smaller and all nodules are resectable via open thoracotomy or thoracoscopy without needing anatomic resection (lobectomy or pneumonectomy) and all nodules are peripheral (distal to segmental bronchi). Prior treatment required: Standard systemic therapy for newly diagnosed osteosarcoma (cisplatin-doxorubicin or ifosfamide-based drug regimens) AND (successful gross tumor resection for the primary tumor or surgical local control of the primary tumor planned to be performed simultaneously with thoracic surgery). Prior treatment excluded: Therapeutic pulmonary surgery for lung metastasis. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Significant pleural effusion.
Disease progression at either the primary or pulmonary metastatic site while on initial therapy.
Unresectable primary tumor.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: follicular lymphoma. Histology allowed: follicular lymphoma grade 1-3A without current or prior histologic transformation. Cancer burden allowed: Stage II, III, or IV AND no active central nervous system lymphoma AND at least one of the following: symptomatic adenopathy, or organ function impairment due to disease involvement including cytopenias due to marrow involvement (white blood cell count <1.5x109/L, absolute neutrophil count <1.0x109/L, hemoglobin <10g/dL, or platelets <100x109/L), or constitutional symptoms (persistent fevers >100.4 F, shaking chills, drenching night sweats, or loss of >10% of body weight within a 6 month period), or any nodal or extranodal tumor mass >7 cm in maximum diameter, or >3 nodal sites of involvement >3 cm, or local compressive symptoms or imminent risk thereof, or splenomegaly (craniocaudal diameter >16cm on CT imaging), or clinically significant pleural or peritoneal effusion, or leukemic phase (>5x109/L circulating malignant cells), or rapid generalized disease progression, or renal infiltration, or bone lesions. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: systemic therapy for follicular lymphoma. Biomarkers required: CD20+ (assessed during screening via review of diagnostic pathology specimen). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >2).
Systemic immunosuppressive therapy (eg prednisone >10 mg daily).
Bulky cervical adenopathy compressing the upper airway.
Candidate for radiation therapy with curative intent.
Recent major surgery or significant traumatic injury.
Active hepatitis B virus (DNA PCR-positive) or hepatitis C virus (RNA PCR-positive).
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) seropositivity.
Known active bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic, or other infection.
Prior history of malignancy (except for non-melanoma skin cancer or in situ cervical or breast cancer) unless disease free for at least 2 years.
Immunization with attenuated live vaccine.
Severe and/or uncontrolled medical conditions.
Myocardial infarction, congenital long QT syndrome, torsade de pointes, unstable angina, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, cardiac arrhythmia (CTCAE grade 3 or higher), clinically significant ECG abnormalities, or cerebrovascular accident within the previous 6 months.
New York Heart Association Class III or IV heart failure or ejection fraction <45%.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Prior solid organ or allogeneic stem cell transplantation.
History of hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.
History of autoimmune disease.
Severe allergic or anaphylactic reactions to anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody therapy or allergy or intolerance to epcoritamab.
Neuropathy > grade 2.
Active severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) infection.
Baseline QTcF >470 msec.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Men and women. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: Residual invasive disease in the breast and/or lymph nodes following neoadjuvant chemotherapy (if no residual invasive disease in the breast, lymph node must contain at least 2mm of invasive disease) and no clinical or radiographic evidence of metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy and definitive breast surgery (lumpectomy followed by whole breast irradiation or mastectomy with pathologically clear margins). Prior treatment excluded: Adjuvant chemotherapy and sacituzumab govitecan or irinotecan or any topoisomerase I-containing antibody-drug conjugates for early stage disease. Biomarkers required: HER2 negative (defined as ISH non-amplified or IHC 0 or IHC 1+) and estrogen receptor negative (< 10% cells expressing) and progesterone receptor negative (< 10% cells expressing) and circulating tumor DNA positive (assessed during screening after completion of all local and systemic neoadjuvant therapy). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Prior hypersensitivity to atezolizumab or sacituzumab govitecan or their excipients.
Ongoing or active infection requiring systemic therapy.
Active tuberculosis.
Serious liver disease such as cirrhosis.
Active bleeding diathesis.
Uncontrolled Type I or Type II diabetes mellitus.
Grade 2 or higher uncontrolled or untreated hypercholesterolemia, hypertriglyceridemia, or hypercalcemia.
Severe cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure (NYHA Class III or IV), myocardial infarction within 6 months prior to enrollment, unstable angina pectoris, or serious uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia.
History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug-induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or evidence of active pneumonitis.
Active autoimmune disease except hypothyroidism on thyroid-replacement hormone, celiac disease, or well-controlled psoriasis, eczema, lichen simplex chronicus, or vitiligo.
Impairment of gastrointestinal function or active gastrointestinal disease that may significantly alter absorption of study agents.
Congenital long QT syndrome or corrected QT interval > 480 ms.
Positive for human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), Hepatitis B antigen (HepBsAg), or Hepatitis C virus (HCV) RNA.
Prior malignancy other than breast cancer (unless disease-free for at least 5 years, or definitively treated thyroid cancer without spread to regional lymph nodes).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

Active uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases and/or leptomeningeal disease

Known allergy or hypersensitivity reaction to investigational medicinal products
Active cardiac disease or history of cardiac dysfunction (including unstable angina pectoris, documented myocardial infarction, symptomatic cardiac heart failure NYHA Class II-IV, poorly controlled hypertension, symptomatic pericarditis, left ventricular ejection fraction less than 55 percent, symptomatic or high-grade arrhythmias, or QTcF prolongation)
Clinically severe pulmonary compromise or history of non-infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis
Current pregnancy or lactation
Current infection with hepatitis B virus or hepatitis C virus
Active primary immunodeficiency or human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection
Other active uncontrolled infection at the time of enrollment
History of uncontrolled seizures, central nervous system disorders, or serious and/or unstable pre-existing psychiatric disability
Known substance abuse
Any other concurrent severe and/or uncontrolled medical condition that would contraindicate participation

Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Pregnant or breast-feeding females
History of non-infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease that required steroids, or current pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease
Live or live-attenuated vaccine within 30 days
Immunodeficiency, chronic systemic steroid therapy exceeding 10 mg daily of prednisone equivalent, or other immunosuppressive therapy within 7 days
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment in the past year

Hepatitis B surface antigen reactive or active Hepatitis C virus infection
Clinically significant liver disease, current alcohol abuse, or cirrhosis

Active tuberculosis
Allogeneic stem cell transplant within the last 5 years or active graft versus host disease
Peripheral neuropathy grade 2 or higher

general_exclusion_criteria:
Tumor lesion(s) in a location that may cause perforation of an organ or structure (such as the digestive track, urinary bladder, or blood vessel).
Active infectious diseases or comorbid conditions that would interfere with safety or data quality.
Active infection requiring systemic therapy or unexplained fever.
Pregnancy or breast-feeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: NA. general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Life expectancy 6 months or less
Active herpetic infections or prior complications of HSV-1 infection (eg herpetic keratitis or encephalitis)
History of organ graft rejection within 12 months
Serious systemic infection requiring intravenous antibiotics or anti-virals within 60 days prior to dosing
Intermittent or chronic use of systemic anti-virals with known anti-herpetic activity (eg acyclovir), unless for organ allograft preservation
Requirement for CTLA-4-Ig medications
Evidence of significant autoimmune disease within 5 years that required systemic immunosuppressive treatments beyond allograft maintenance
Active infection with hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus
History of transplant-related viral infections (eg BKV, EBV or CMV) within 3 months of study entry
Condition requiring an increase in usual immunosuppressive medications within 60 days of study treatment
Known active CNS metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis
Baseline ECG evidence of acute ischemia
