clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Either sex. Cancer type allowed: non-small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: epithelial origin. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable or locally advanced. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior therapy with any antibody-drug conjugate targeting EGFR or HER3 or containing a topoisomerase 1 inhibitor payload or more than two systemic chemotherapies. Biomarkers required: EGFR Exon 19 deletion or EGFR Exon 21 L858R mutation or EGFR Exon 20 S768I mutation or EGFR Exon 21 L861Q mutation or EGFR Exon 18 G719X mutation. Biomarkers excluded: EGFR Exon 20 insertion mutations as primary EGFR mutations.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Symptomatic congestive heart failure Grade 2 or higher, New York Heart Association (NYHA) Grade 2 or higher heart failure, history of transmural myocardial infarction, or unstable angina pectoris.
Prolonged QT interval (QTc >470 msec), complete left bundle branch block, or Grade 3 atrioventricular block.
Active autoimmune diseases or inflammatory diseases (except Type I diabetes, hypothyroidism controlled by standard of care, or skin diseases not requiring systemic treatment).
Other malignant tumors diagnosed within 5 years prior to first administration (except basal cell carcinoma of the skin, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, or carcinoma in situ after radical resection).
Poorly controlled hypertension (systolic blood pressure >150 mmHg or diastolic blood pressure >100 mmHg).
Grade 3 lung disease, or a history of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis.
Unstable thrombotic events (such as deep vein thrombosis, arterial thrombosis, or pulmonary embolism) requiring therapeutic intervention within 6 months before screening.
Primary tumors in the central nervous system, active or untreated central nervous system metastases, or carcinomatous meningitis.
History of allergies to recombinant humanized antibodies, human-mouse chimeric antibodies, or any components of BL-B01D1.
History of autologous or allogeneic stem cell transplantation.
Cumulative anthracycline dose exceeding 360 mg/m2.
Grade 2 or higher hypokalemia.
Human immunodeficiency virus antibody (HIVAb) positive, active tuberculosis, active Hepatitis B virus infection (HBV-DNA copy number > lower limit of detection), or active Hepatitis C virus infection (HCV antibody positive and HCV-RNA > lower limit of detection).
Active infections requiring systemic treatment (such as severe pneumonia, bacteremia, or sepsis).
Received an investigational drug within 4 weeks.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Poor performance status (eg ECOG PS >1).

Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Current corneal epithelial disease.
Use of contact lenses.
Treatment with any investigational agents.
Primary refractory disease.
Concomitant high dose corticosteroids.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Prior history of malignancies other than multiple myeloma, except for basal or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, carcinoma in situ of the cervix, ductal carcinoma in situ of the breast, or incidental histologic finding of prostate cancer (T1a or T1b) managed with surveillance.
Active plasma cell leukemia, POEMS syndrome, or primary AL amyloidosis.
Seropositive for HIV infection.
Seropositive for hepatitis B (unless resolved and HBV DNA negative).
Seropositive for hepatitis C (unless there is a sustained virologic response).
Peripheral neuropathy grade 2 or higher despite supportive therapy.
Hypersensitivity to isatuximab, belantamab mafodotin, pomalidomide, or dexamethasone.
Recent allogeneic stem cell transplant or current dependence on immunosuppressive treatment or active graft versus host disease.
Recent autologous stem cell transplant.
History of significant cardiovascular, neurological, endocrine, gastrointestinal, respiratory, or inflammatory illness (eg congestive heart failure NYHA Class 3 or 4, unstable angina, clinically significant uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, recent myocardial infarction or stroke, uncontrolled hypertension, diabetes mellitus with multiple episodes of ketoacidosis, or severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease).
Unstable liver or biliary disease.
Active bacterial, viral, or fungal infection.
Other medical, psychiatric, or social conditions that preclude participation.
Recent major surgery.
Recent live or live-attenuated vaccine.
Toxicity from previous anticancer therapy greater than grade 1, except for alopecia and peripheral neuropathy.

Poor performance status (ECOG > 2)
Inadequate bone marrow or organ function (Absolute neutrophil count < 1.5 K/cumm, Platelets < 100 K/cumm, Hemoglobin <= 9 g/dL, Total bilirubin > 1.5 x IULN, AST(SGOT)/ALT(SGPT) > 2.5 x IULN, Serum creatinine >= 1.5 x IULN and measured or calculated creatinine clearance < 50 mL/min/1.73 m2)
Known or suspected presence of another malignancy
Currently receiving other investigational agents
Diagnosis of Familial Adenomatosis Polyposis coli (FAP), Hereditary Non-Polyposis Colorectal Cancer (HNPCC), active Crohn's disease, or active ulcerative colitis
History of allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to epacadostat, pembrolizumab, 5-FU, or oxaliplatin
Active infection requiring systemic therapy
Current use of warfarin
History of serotonin syndrome
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg active tuberculosis infection, pneumonitis requiring treatment, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, or cardiac arrhythmia)
Active or inactive autoimmune disease or syndrome that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years or is receiving systemic therapy
Clinically meaningful abnormal screening ECG
Presence of a gastrointestinal condition that may affect drug absorption
Pregnant and/or breastfeeding
Known active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection

Inability or unwillingness to comply with protocol requirements

general_exclusion_criteria: Poor performance status (ECOG >2). Uncontrolled HIV. Active hepatitis B virus infection (positive HBsAg or positive PCR) or active hepatitis C virus infection (positive RNA). Chronic liver disease and hepatic impairment meeting Child-Pugh class B or C. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or planning to become pregnant. Active alcohol or drug abuse. Concurrent participation in another therapeutic clinical trial. History of another malignancy, except adequately treated local basal cell or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, cervical carcinoma in situ, superficial bladder cancer, localized prostate cancer, or other adequately treated cancer currently in complete remission. Prior or ongoing clinically significant illness, including active infections requiring antibiotics. Inability to swallow pills. Significant cardiovascular disease (defined as unstable angina, history of myocardial infarction within 6 months, left ventricular ejection fraction <= 45% within 12 months, New York Heart Association Class 3 or 4 cardiac disease, uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias, or baseline QTc >480 msec). Ongoing active infection. Active bleeding requiring blood transfusion or other medical intervention. Poor marrow or organ function (absolute neutrophil count <0.5 k/uL, platelet count <50 k/uL, total bilirubin >1.5 times the upper limit of normal unless documented liver involvement, hemolysis, or Gilbert's disease, AST or ALT >2.5 times the upper limit of normal unless documented liver involvement, or creatinine clearance <30 ml/min).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Inability to undergo MRI brain
Intracranial hemorrhage greater than Grade 2
Arterial thromboembolic events (eg cerebrovascular accident)
Active autoimmune disease requiring immunosuppressive agents or steroids
History of non-infectious pneumonitis that required steroids or current pneumonitis
Uncontrolled active systemic infection
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease (eg uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias, congestive heart failure NYHA > Class 2, unstable angina, or myocardial infarction)
Uncontrolled hypertension
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus
Known bleeding diathesis or hemophilia
Non-healing wound, ulcer, or bone fracture
Unable to swallow capsules or severe gastrointestinal dysfunction
HIV/AIDS
Hepatitis B surface antigen reactive or active Hepatitis C virus infection
Active tuberculosis
Immunodeficiency
Chronic systemic steroid therapy (exceeding 10 mg daily of prednisone equivalent)
Psychiatric or substance abuse disorders
Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Severe hypersensitivity (Grade 3 or higher) to study agents
Another malignancy that is progressing or has required active treatment within the past 3 years (except basal cell carcinoma of the skin, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, transitional cell carcinoma of urothelial cancer, or carcinoma in situ)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 28 days to < 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Any malignant or benign disease requiring allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: High-risk hematopoietic stem cell transplant thrombotic microangiopathy defined as (platelet count < 50,000/mL or decrease in platelet count > 50% from the highest value obtained following transplant AND evidence of microangiopathic hemolysis (presence of schistocytes or serum lactate dehydrogenase > upper limit of normal or haptoglobin < lower limit of normal)) AND (HSCT-TMA persistence > 2 weeks following modification of calcineurin inhibitors or sirolimus OR spot protein/creatinine ratio > 2 mg/mg OR serum creatinine > 1.5 x the creatinine level prior to TMA development OR biopsy-proven gastrointestinal TMA OR TMA-related neurological abnormality OR pericardial or pleural effusion without alternative explanation OR pulmonary hypertension without alternative explanation OR Grade III or Grade IV graft-versus-host disease OR risk for development of Grade III or Grade IV graft-versus-host disease OR serum C5b-9 > 244 ng/mL). Prior treatment required: Allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Prior treatment excluded: Eculizumab, ravulizumab, or defibrotide. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli haemolytic uraemic syndrome or ADAMTS13 activity < 10%.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Severe, uncontrolled systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection.
Malignant hypertension.
Poor prognosis with a life expectancy of less than 3 months.

Active diarrhea >= CTCAE grade 2.

Poorly controlled generalized or complex partial seizures. History of non-infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis that required steroids, current interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis, or suspected interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis. Other significant lung disease including pulmonary emboli within 3 months, severe asthma, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disorder, restrictive lung disease, pleural effusion, or prior pneumonectomy. Autoimmune, connective tissue or inflammatory disorders with documented or suspected pulmonary involvement. Active or uncontrolled clinically serious infection. Allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to trastuzumab, pertuzumab, paclitaxel, trastuzumab deruxtecan, tucatinib, or T-DM1. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström Macroglobulinemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Symptomatic disease meeting criteria for treatment using consensus panel criteria from the Second International Workshop on Waldenström macroglobulinemia and measurable disease defined as presence of immunoglobulin M (IgM) paraprotein with a minimum serum IgM level of > 2 times the upper limit normal. Prior treatment required: At least 2 prior lines of treatment, including an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody-containing regimen and a BTK inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: CD19 targeted therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG >2).
Bone marrow dysfunction (Absolute neutrophil count <1000/uL, Platelets <50,000/uL, or Hemoglobin <7 g/dL).
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin >1.5 X ULN, AST/ALT >2.5 X ULN, or clinically significant history of liver disease including cirrhosis or hepatitis).
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance <30 ml/min).
Known history of Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV), chronic hepatitis B virus (HBV) or hepatitis C (HCV) requiring active treatment.
Significant cardiovascular disease (unstable angina within the past 6 months, history of myocardial infarction within the past 6 months, New York Heart Association Functional Classification Class 3 or 4 cardiac disease, or uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias).
Pregnant or breastfeeding.
Clinically significant third space fluid accumulation (ascites requiring drainage or pleural effusion that is either requiring drainage or associated with shortness of breath) unless proven by cytology to be malignant due to Waldenström Macroglobulinemia.
Known CNS lymphoma.
History of Stevens-Johnson syndrome (SJS) or Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis (TEN).
Concurrent systemic immunosuppressant therapy.
Ongoing alcohol or drug abuse.
History of a non-lymphoma malignancy (except adequately treated local basal cell or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, cervical carcinoma in situ, superficial bladder cancer, localized prostate cancer, other adequately treated stage 1 or 2 cancer currently in complete remission, or any other cancer that is in a complete remission).
Ongoing >grade 1 toxicities from prior therapy (excluding alopecia of any grade and/or grade 2 neuropathy).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: follicular lymphoma. Histology allowed: grade 1-3A. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory and no current histologic transformation and no known central nervous system involvement. Biomarkers required: CD20 positive (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG >2).
Requirement for systemic immunosuppressive therapy (including prednisone >10 mg daily).
Bulky cervical adenopathy compressing the upper airway or risk of significant airway compression.
Recent major surgery or significant traumatic injury.
Presence of HCV antibody with detectable HCV RNA.
Presence of hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg) or hepatitis B core antibody (HBcAb) with detectable HBV DNA.
Known active bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic, or other infection requiring antimicrobial therapy.
Active severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) infection.
Prior history of another malignancy (excluding non-melanoma skin cancer, in situ cervical or breast cancer, or Gleason 6 prostate cancer managed with observation) unless disease free for at least 2 years or low likelihood of relapse.
Immunization with attenuated live vaccine shortly before or during the study.
Severe and/or uncontrolled medical conditions.
Myocardial infarction, congenital long QT syndrome, torsade de pointes, unstable angina, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, cardiac arrhythmia (CTCAE grade 3 or higher), or cerebrovascular accident within the previous 6 months.
Mobitz II second-degree or third-degree heart block without a permanent pacemaker.
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic > 170 mm Hg and diastolic > 105 mm Hg).
New York Heart Association Class III or IV heart failure or ejection fraction < 45%.
Pregnancy or breast-feeding.
Prior solid organ or allogeneic stem cell transplantation.
History of known or suspected hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH).
History of clinically significant autoimmune disease.
Inability to tolerate anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody therapy or known allergy or intolerance to components of epcoritamab.
Neuropathy > grade 1.
Requirement for warfarin or other vitamin K antagonists for anticoagulation.
Concomitant treatment with strong CYP3A inducers.
Inability to swallow capsules or disease significantly affecting gastrointestinal function.
History of severe bleeding disorder or spontaneous bleeding requiring blood transfusions.
Screening 12-lead ECG showing baseline QTcF > 480 msec.
History of stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within 6 months prior to treatment.

Non-infectious pneumonitis.

Psychiatric or substance abuse disorders.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Inability to undergo MRI of the brain.
Live vaccine received within 30 days of planned start of study therapy.
Chronic supraphysiologic doses of corticosteroids (> 10 mg/d of prednisone equivalent or > 2 mg dexamethasone).
Multifocal disease (for surgical cohort).

Prior treatment required: Between 1-4 lines of prior systemic therapy and exposure, relapse, or intolerance to a proteasome inhibitor and an immunomodulatory drug and an anti-CD38-monoclonal antibody. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Poor performance status (ECOG > 2). Inadequate hematological function (ANC < 1000/uL, platelet count < 50,000/uL, or hemoglobin < 8 g/dL). Inadequate hepatic function (total bilirubin level >= 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal, AST > 2.5 x institutional upper limit of normal, or ALT > 2.5 x institutional upper limit of normal). Inadequate renal function (calculated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min or eGFR <= 30mL/min). Hypercalcemia (Corrected serum calcium >= 14mg/dL or free ionized calcium >= 6.5 mg/dL). Cardiac dysfunction (NYHA Functional Classification Class 3 or 4). Complete cord compression or central nervous system involvement. Active or history of autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment within 2 years of the start of study drug. Allogeneic tissue or solid organ transplant recipients with chronic graft-versus-host disease requiring steroid equivalent dose of > 20 mg prednisone. Active infection requiring treatment. Psychiatric or substance abuse disorder that would interfere with cooperation with trial requirements. Legally incapacitated or limited legal capacity. Pregnancy or lactation. Plasma cell leukemia, Waldenstrom's macroglobulinemia, POEMS syndrome, or primary amyloid light-chain amyloidosis. History of allergy or hypersensitivity to trial therapies.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic. Prior treatment required: 1 or 2 prior hormonal therapies in the advanced or metastatic setting and one of these therapies must have been in combination with a cyclin-dependent kinase targeting enzymes CDK4 and CDK6 (CDK4/6) inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: alpelisib or any other phosphoinositide 3-kinase (PI3K) inhibitor, and prior chemotherapy or elacestrant in the advanced or metastatic setting, and prior therapy with investigational selective estrogen receptor degraders or investigational selective estrogen receptor modulators or selective estrogen receptor covalent antagonists or complete estrogen receptor antagonists or proteolysis-targeting chimeras in the metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: ER+ (at least 10% of tumor cells demonstrate positive nuclear staining by immunohistochemistry) and HER2- (assessed during screening) and PIK3CA mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active or newly diagnosed central nervous system metastases or meningeal carcinomatosis. Advanced symptomatic visceral spread (eg massive uncontrolled effusions, pulmonary lymphangitis, or liver involvement >50%). Germline BRCA mutation without prior treatment with a PARP inhibitor. Concurrent malignancy or history of invasive malignancy within 3 years. Uncontrolled significant active infections. Documented pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease. Major surgery within 28 days before starting trial therapy. Inability to take oral medications or uncontrolled gastrointestinal conditions impacting absorption. Intolerance to elacestrant. Pregnancy or breast-feeding. Failure to use highly effective contraception. Ongoing alcohol or drug abuse. Severe medical or psychiatric condition. Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1). clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: high-grade serous. Cancer burden allowed: platinum-resistant disease (excluding primary platinum-refractory disease). Prior treatment required: 1 to 3 prior lines or regimens (or 1 to 4 prior lines if prior mirvetuximab treatment was received) and bevacizumab (if eligible per standard of care) and PARP inhibitor (if BRCA 1/2 mutation or HRD and eligible per standard of care) and mirvetuximab (if eligible per standard of care). Prior treatment excluded: ZN-c3 or WEE1 inhibitor or ATR inhibitor or PKMYT1 inhibitor or CHK1/2 inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Cyclin E1 protein positive determined by IHC using Sponsor's investigational clinical trial assay (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG >1)
Clinically or radiographically unstable brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease requiring immediate treatment
Myocardial impairment resulting in heart failure (NYHA Class II-IV)
Severe acute or chronic medical or psychiatric condition or laboratory abnormality that may increase risk associated with study participation or interfere with results
Acute kidney injury requiring intervention or intravenous fluid in the last 14 days or presence of indwelling urinary catheter or percutaneous nephrostomy
Significant gastrointestinal abnormalities (eg inability to take oral medication, requirement for intravenous alimentation, active peptic ulcer, clinically significant chronic diarrhea or vomiting, or prior surgical procedures affecting absorption)
Active, uncontrolled infection
Evidence of bowel obstruction, recent hospitalization for small bowel obstruction within 3 months, or recurrent paracentesis or thoracentesis within 6 weeks
Unresolved toxicity of Grade >1 attributed to any prior therapies (excluding Grade <=2 neuropathy, alopecia, or skin pigmentation)
Pregnant or lactating female subject or female subject of childbearing potential with a positive serum pregnancy test
History of another malignancy in the previous 2 years (unless cured by surgery alone and continuously disease free, or appropriately treated carcinoma in situ of the cervix, non-melanoma skin carcinoma, Stage 1 uterine cancer, or other malignancies with an expected curative outcome)
Immunocompromised or HIV-positive
Known active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection
Prior wide-field radiotherapy affecting >= 20% of the bone marrow

Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of treatment. Prior treatment excluded: EO2463 and (one prior treatment and first progression of disease within 24 months of diagnosis). Biomarkers required: HLA-A2 positive. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (e.g., ECOG > 2).
Grade 3B follicular lymphoma or transformation to an aggressive lymphoma subtype.
Uncontrolled central nervous system (CNS) metastasis.
Other malignancy or prior malignancy with a disease-free interval of less than 3 years.
Abnormal laboratory values.
Persistent Grade 3 or 4 toxicities.
Suspected autoimmune or active autoimmune disorder or known history of an autoimmune neurologic condition (e.g., Guillain-Barre syndrome).
History of solid organ transplantation or allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
Pregnant or breastfeeding patients.
Presence of human immunodeficiency virus or potentially active hepatitis B virus or hepatitis C virus infection.
Clinically significant disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Diagnosed within 18 months prior to registration and asymptomatic (must not meet IWCLL specified criteria for active CLL therapy) and CLL-International Prognostic Index (CLL-IPI) score >= 4. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: anti CD20 monoclonal antibodies and any prior CLL-directed therapy (including non-protocol-related therapy, anti-cancer immunotherapy, experimental therapy, or radiotherapy). Biomarkers required: Immunoglobulin heavy chain locus variable (IgVH) gene mutation analysis (assessed prior to registration) and FISH analyses for abnormalities in chromosomes 13q, 12, 11q, and 17p (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2)
Platelet count < 100,000/mm^3
Absolute neutrophil count (ANC) < 1,000/mm^3
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance < 30mL/min)
Liver dysfunction (AST or ALT >= 3.0 x ULN or total bilirubin >= 2.0 x ULN [or 5.0 x ULN if history of Gilbert's disease])
Current clinically significant gastrointestinal malabsorption
Cirrhosis
Uncontrolled active infection with hepatitis B or C
Major surgery within 30 days prior to registration or minor surgery within 7 days prior to registration
Known bleeding disorders (eg von Willebrand's disease or hemophilia)
History of stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within 6 months prior to enrollment
Requirement for continued therapy with a strong inhibitor or inducer of CYP3A4/5
Uncontrolled autoimmune hemolytic anemia or idiopathic thrombocytopenia purpura
Active clinically significant cardiovascular disease (eg uncontrolled arrhythmia or New York Heart Association class 3 or 4 congestive heart failure)
History of myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or acute coronary syndrome within 6 months prior to enrollment
Pregnancy or nursing

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: > 5 years and <= 21 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Medulloblastoma. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent, refractory, or progressive. Prior treatment required: Failed prior standard therapy and radiation therapy and platinum chemotherapy and alkylator-based chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: MET inhibitor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnant women or nursing mothers.
Serious active infection including human immunodeficiency virus and tuberculosis.
Known active or resolved viral hepatitis infection.
Clinically significant unrelated systemic illness or significant cardiac, pulmonary, hepatic, or other organ dysfunction.
Uncontrolled hypertension.
Congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association grade 2 or higher).
Clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia.
Mean resting corrected QT interval (QTc Bazett) greater than 450 msec on screening.
Factors that may increase the risk of QTc prolongation such as chronic hypokalemia not correctable with supplements, congenital or familial long QT syndrome, or family history of unexplained sudden death under 40 years of age in first-degree relatives.
Concomitant medication known to prolong the QT interval and cause Torsade de Pointes.
Clinically important abnormalities in rhythm, conduction, or morphology of resting EKG (e.g., complete left bundle branch block, third degree heart block, second degree heart block, PR interval > 250 msec).
History of liver cirrhosis or other serious liver disease or chronic disease with relevant liver involvement (e.g., Hemochromatosis, Alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, Autoimmune hepatitis, Primary sclerosing cholangitis, Primary biliary cirrhosis, biopsy-confirmed non-alcoholic steatohepatitis with advanced fibrosis, biopsy-confirmed alcoholic steatohepatitis with advanced fibrosis, Wilson's disease, Hepatocellular carcinoma).
Prior or concurrent malignancy whose natural history or treatment has the potential to interfere with safety or efficacy assessment.
Concurrent other anticancer or investigational drug therapy.
Strong inducers of CYP3A4, strong inhibitors of CYP3A4 or CYP1A2, or CYP3A4 substrates with a narrow therapeutic index.
Current use of certain herbal preparations (e.g., cannabis products, St. John's wort, kava, ephedra, gingko biloba, dehydroepiandrosterone, yohimbe, saw palmetto, and ginseng) that cannot be discontinued.
Major surgical procedure within 28 days prior to beginning study drug or a minor surgical procedure within 7 days prior to beginning study drug.
Unable or unwilling to return for required follow-up visits or adhere to drug administration plan and study restrictions.
History of allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition.
Prisoner status.
Poor performance status (Karnofsky performance scale or Lansky performance score less than 50).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG PS > 1)
Inadequate hematologic, hepatic, renal, or coagulation function
Known additional malignancy that is progressing or has required active treatment within the past 2 years
Known active central nervous system metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis
Significant cardiovascular disease within 6 months prior to start of study drug
Evidence of an active systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring treatment
Known active hepatitis B virus (HBV), hepatitis C virus (HCV), human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) or acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS)
Failure to recover to Baseline severity or NCI-CTCAE v5.0 Grade <=1 from acute non-hematologic toxicity due to previous therapy

Severe hypersensitivity (Grade >=3) to pembrolizumab and/or PYX-201 or any of their excipients

general_exclusion_criteria:
Prior allogeneic hematologic transplant.
Concurrent use of other anticancer investigational agents.
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Short life expectancy (12 weeks or less).
Poor bone marrow function (Leukocytes < 1,000/mcL or absolute neutrophil count < 500/mcL or platelets < 50,000/mcL).
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin >= 2 x institutional ULN or AST/ALT >= 5 x institutional ULN, or AST/ALT >= 8 x institutional ULN for patients with liver metastases or autoimmune disease).

Uncontrolled HIV infection (detectable viral load).
Uncontrolled hepatitis B virus infection (detectable viral load).
Uncontrolled hepatitis C virus infection (detectable viral load).
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease requiring immediate central nervous system specific treatment.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness/social situations limiting compliance).

Poor performance status (ECOG > 1)
Known and untreated, or active central nervous system (CNS) metastases
Leptomeningeal disease or carcinomatous meningitis

Malabsorption syndrome or other condition that would interfere with enteral absorption
Known hypersensitivity to any of the components of divarasib, or sotorasib or adagrasib
Uncontrolled tumor-related pain
Unresolved toxicities from prior anticancer therapy
History of malignancy within 5 years prior to screening (except for the cancer under investigation or malignancies with a negligible risk of metastasis or death)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: non-small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable, advanced, and/or metastatic disease (Stage III or IV). Prior treatment required: at least 1 line of approved systemic standard of care (SOC) treatment regimen. Prior treatment excluded: adoptive cell and gene therapy or allogeneic stem cell transplant or solid organ transplantation. Biomarkers required: TP53 R175H variant mutation and HLA-A*02:01 positive (confirmed by CLIA-accredited laboratory-based test). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Inadequate hematological, renal, hepatic, pulmonary, or cardiac function
Any other primary malignancy within 3 years prior to enrollment (except for non-melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or low-grade prostate cancer)
Known active primary central nervous system (CNS) malignancy
History of stroke or transient ischemic attack within 12 months prior to enrollment
History of clinically significant cardiac disease within 6 months prior to enrollment or heart failure at any time prior to enrollment
Severe immediate hypersensitivity reaction to cyclophosphamide, fludarabine, or rIL-2, or known sensitivity or allergy to methotrexate, gentamicin, or other aminoglycosides
Any form of primary immunodeficiency
Live vaccine administered within 4 weeks prior to enrollment or planned prior to lymphodepleting chemotherapy and/or NT-175 treatment
Active immune-mediated disease requiring systemic steroids or other immunosuppressive treatment
Female of childbearing potential who is lactating or breast feeding
Known Li-Fraumeni syndrome or relatives diagnosed with Li-Fraumeni syndrome

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: T-cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: cutaneous T-cell lymphoma. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Pregnant women.
Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant within 6 months of starting treatment or patients with active graft versus host disease requiring immunosuppression.
Prior use of duvelisib or ruxolitinib if either agent was discontinued due to toxicity.
Ongoing use of immunosuppressant medications, including corticosteroids greater than 20mg of prednisone or equivalent.
History of chronic liver disease, veno-occlusive disease, or current alcohol abuse.
Prior surgery or gastrointestinal condition that may adversely affect drug absorption (e.g., gastric bypass surgery, gastrectomy).
HIV infection with detectable viral load, or undetectable viral load with CD4 count < 200, or not taking anti-retroviral medications.
Chronic hepatitis B or C (defined by positive hepatitis B or C serology, with specific requirement for undetectable hepatitis B DNA if HBsAg is negative and HBcAb is positive).
Active CMV infection requiring therapy.
Unable or unwilling to receive prophylaxis against pneumocystis, herpes simplex virus, or herpes zoster.
Use of medications or consumption of foods that are strong inducers or inhibitors of CYP3A.
Receipt of treatment for tuberculosis within 2 years prior to enrollment.
Receiving therapy for another primary malignancy other than T-cell lymphoma (early-stage cutaneous basal cell and squamous cell carcinomas are permissible).
Known central nervous system or meningeal involvement by T-cell lymphoma.
Unstable or severe uncontrolled medical condition (e.g., unstable cardiac function, unstable pulmonary condition).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: Stage II or Stage III and no evidence of recurrent local or distant disease and presence of detectable disseminated tumor cells in the bone marrow via IHC assessed during screening. Prior treatment required: Definitive surgery and chemotherapy and radiation and adjuvant endocrine therapy initiated 2 to 7 years prior to enrollment. Prior treatment excluded: Investigational adjuvant endocrine therapy. Biomarkers required: ER+ and Her2 negative. Biomarkers excluded: ER negative and PR positive.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Chronic high dose systemic corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive agents.
EKG demonstrating QT interval corrected (QTC) > 480 ms.
Chronic autoimmune disease.
Increased cardiovascular risk including uncontrolled arrhythmias, history of acute coronary syndromes (myocardial infarction or unstable angina), coronary angioplasty, or stenting within 6 months prior to enrollment, or NYHA Class II or higher congestive heart failure.
History of pneumonitis, interstitial lung disease, or severely impaired lung function (DLCO < 50% of normal predicted value or O2 saturation <= 88% at rest on room air).
Uncontrolled diabetes.
Active or uncontrolled severe infections.
Liver disease such as cirrhosis, chronic active hepatitis, or chronic persistent hepatitis.
HIV positive patients receiving combination anti-retroviral therapy.
Impairment of gastrointestinal function or gastrointestinal disease (ulcerative disease, uncontrolled nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, malabsorption syndrome, or small bowel resection).
Active bleeding diathesis or use of therapeutic anticoagulation.
History of retinopathy or retinal vein occlusion.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or adults of reproductive potential not using effective birth control methods.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Male or female. Cancer type allowed: Non-small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Squamous. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic (Stage IV). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior systemic treatment for metastatic non-small cell lung cancer. Biomarkers required: PD-L1 expression TPS > 50% assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: Actionable genomic alterations for which first-line approved therapies are indicated.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Symptomatic CNS metastases, CNS metastasis >= 1.5 cm, or leptomeningeal disease
CNS radiation within 7 days prior to randomization or potential need for CNS radiation within the first cycle
Other prior malignancy unless the patient has undergone curative therapy with no evidence of recurrence for 3 years prior to randomization
Active autoimmune or lung disease requiring systemic therapy
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy >= Grade 2 by CTCAE version 5
Severe infection within 4 weeks prior to randomization
Major surgical procedures or serious trauma within 4 weeks prior to randomization
History of noninfectious pneumonia requiring systemic corticosteroids or current interstitial lung disease
Concurrent enrollment in another clinical study (unless non-interventional or survival follow-up)

general_exclusion_criteria:
Suitable HLA-matched related or 8/8 high-resolution matched unrelated donor available.
Prior allogeneic transplant.
Females who are breast-feeding or pregnant.
Uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infection.
Concurrent enrollment on a preventative Graft Versus Host Disease and/or infectious disease prevention clinical trial.
Subjects who undergo desensitization to reduce anti-donor HLA antibody levels prior to transplant.
HIV positive with persistently positive viral load.
Poor cardiac function (Left ventricular ejection fraction < 40% or < 45% depending on stratum).
Renal dysfunction (Estimated creatinine clearance < 45mL/min).
Poor pulmonary function (DLCO corrected for hemoglobin <= 50% or FEV1 predicted <= 50%).
Liver dysfunction.
Poor performance status (KPS < 60% or < 70% depending on stratum).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Karnofsky Performance Status < 70%)
Positive test for HIV, HBV (HBsAg, anti-HBcAg Ab, or HBV-DNA), or HCV (HCV-RNA or HCV antibody)

Inadequate liver function (ALT, AST, ALP >= 2.5 x ULN or total bilirubin >= 2.0 x ULN)
INR > 1.5 ULN
Heart insufficiency (> Grade II, New York Heart Association (NYHA) criteria)
Clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias, LVEF < 55%, or history of QT/QTc prolongation
History within the last year of cerebrovascular disease or acute or subacute coronary syndromes (including myocardial infarction, unstable or severe stable angina pectoris)
Uncontrolled hypertension
Known arterial aneurism at high risk of rupture
Ischemic peripheral vascular disease (Grade IIb-IV according to Leriche-Fontaine classification)
Medically documented history of or active major depressive episode, bipolar disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, schizophrenia, history of suicidal attempt or ideation, homicidal ideation, or active severe personality disorders
Anxiety >= CTCAE Grade 3
Severe diabetic retinopathy
Known history of tuberculosis
Pregnancy or breast feeding
Requirement of chronic administration of high dose corticosteroids (stable or decreasing dose > 4 mg daily dexamethasone or equivalent) or other immunosuppressant drugs
Presence of active and uncontrolled infections
Concurrent malignancies (unless disease-free without intervention for at least 2 years)
Serious, non-healing wound, ulcer, or bone fracture
Anticoagulation therapy with P2Y12 antagonists or vitamin K antagonists
Inability to undergo contrast-enhanced MRI
Known history of allergy to TNF, lomustine, excipients in study medication, or intravenously administered human proteins/peptides/antibodies
Low blood counts (absolute neutrophil count < 1.5 x 10^9/L, platelets < 100 x 10^9/L, or haemoglobin < 9.0 g/dl)
Active or history of autoimmune disease

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2).
Severe cardiac disease or congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association Class III or higher) or personal history of syncope of cardiovascular etiology, ventricular arrhythmia of pathological origin, or sudden cardiac arrest.
Severe pulmonary conditions or illness, including interstitial lung disease or severe dyspnea at rest or requiring oxygen therapy.
Renal dysfunction (eg estimated creatinine clearance < 30ml/min).
Liver dysfunction.
Bone marrow suppression (eg ANC < 1000/mm3, Hemoglobin < 8 g/dl, or Platelets < 50,000/mm3).
Active systemic bacterial infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, fungal infection, or detectable viral infection (eg human immunodeficiency virus positivity, active hepatitis B, or active hepatitis C).
History of major surgical resection involving the stomach or small bowel.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: non-small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non-squamous. Cancer burden allowed: Stage IIIB or IIIC disease and not candidates for surgical resection or definitive chemoradiation, or Stage IV disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior systemic treatment for advanced or metastatic non-small cell lung cancer and prior adjuvant or neoadjuvant treatment with any agent containing a chemotherapeutic agent targeting topoisomerase I (including antibody-drug conjugates) or TROP2-targeted therapy or any anti-programmed death receptor-1 (PD-1) agent or anti-PD-L1 agent or anti-PD-ligand 2 (L2) agent or any agent directed to another stimulatory or co-inhibitory T-cell receptor (eg, CTLA-4, OX40, CD137) or any other immune checkpoint inhibitors. Biomarkers required: high programmed death receptor-1 (PD-L1) expression (TPS >=50%) assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) actionable genomic alterations and lymphoma kinase (ALK) actionable genomic alterations and proto-oncogene 1 (ROS1) actionable genomic alterations and neurotrophic tyrosine receptor kinase (NTRK) actionable genomic alterations and proto-oncogene B-raf (BRAF) actionable genomic alterations and rearranged during transfection (RET) actionable genomic alterations and mesenchymal-epithelial transition factor (MET) actionable genomic alterations and other actionable driver kinases with locally approved therapies.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Life expectancy less than 3 months.
Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) <50%.
Spinal cord compression or active and untreated central nervous system (CNS) metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.
Prior radiotherapy within 4 weeks of start of study intervention or more than 30 Gy to the lung within 6 months.
History of another primary malignancy except for curative intent malignancy with no known active disease for >=3 years and low risk of recurrence, or adequately treated non-melanoma skin cancer or lentigo maligna, or adequately treated carcinoma in situ, or prostate cancer Stage <=T2cN0M0 without biochemical recurrence or progression.
History of non-infectious interstitial lung disease (ILD) or pneumonitis requiring steroids, current ILD or pneumonitis, or suspected ILD or pneumonitis that cannot be ruled out by imaging.
Clinically severe pulmonary compromise resulting from intercurrent pulmonary illnesses, underlying pulmonary disorder, autoimmune, connective tissue or inflammatory disorders with pulmonary involvement, or prior complete pneumonectomy.
Uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease including QTcF interval >470 ms, myocardial infarction within 6 months, uncontrolled angina pectoris within 6 months, New York Heart Association Class 2 to 4 congestive heart failure, or uncontrolled hypertension (systolic >180 mmHg or diastolic >110 mmHg).
Clinically significant corneal disease.
Live vaccine or live-attenuated vaccine within 30 days prior to first dose.
Active, known, or suspected autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment in the past 2 years.
Diagnosis of immunodeficiency, chronic systemic steroid therapy (>10 mg daily of prednisone equivalent), or other immunosuppressive therapy.
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection that is not well controlled.
Active hepatitis or uncontrolled hepatitis B or active hepatitis C infection.
Uncontrolled infection requiring IV antibiotics, antivirals, or antifungals.
History of allogeneic tissue or solid organ transplant.
Severe hypersensitivity reactions to Dato-DXd or pembrolizumab or their inactive ingredients.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: clear cell. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced disease with at least 1 metastatic bone lesion not treated with prior radiation. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: cabozantinib or hemibody external radiotherapy or radium-223 dichloride or systemic radiotherapy (samarium or strontium). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (Karnofsky performance status < 60%)
Untreated or unstable brain metastases or cranial epidural disease
Imminent or established spinal cord compression or imminent or impending pathologic fracture
Significant cardiovascular disorders (e.g., symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, serious cardiac arrhythmia, uncontrolled hypertension, recent stroke, myocardial infarction, or thromboembolic event)
Active gastrointestinal disorders associated with a high risk of perforation or fistula formation (e.g., active inflammatory bowel disease, active diverticulitis, active cholecystitis, active symptomatic cholangitis, active appendicitis, active acute pancreatitis, active acute obstruction of the pancreatic or biliary duct, or active gastric outlet obstruction)
Clinically significant hematuria, hematemesis, hemoptysis, or other history of significant bleeding
Uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), hepatitis B virus (HBV), or hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection
Moderate to severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B or C)
Requirement for hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis
History of solid organ transplantation
Pregnancy or nursing
Clinically significant laboratory abnormalities (e.g., absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mm^3, platelet count < 100,000/mm^3, hemoglobin < 9 g/dl, creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min, total bilirubin > 1.5 x upper limit of normal, aspartate aminotransferase or alanine aminotransferase > 3.0 x upper limit of normal, or urine protein to creatinine ratio >= 2 mg/mg)
Chronic concomitant treatment with strong CYP3A4 inducers or inhibitors
Concomitant anticoagulation with coumarin agents, direct thrombin inhibitors, betrixaban, or platelet inhibitors (e.g., clopidogrel)
Lesions invading major pulmonary blood vessels
Serious non-healing wound or ulcer
Malabsorption syndrome
Uncompensated or symptomatic hypothyroidism
Major surgery within 6 weeks of randomization

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 3).
Renal dysfunction (eg Creatinine clearance or radioisotope GFR < 70 mL/min/1.73 m^2 or serum creatinine above age/sex specific limits).
Liver dysfunction (eg Total bilirubin >= 2 x ULN or AST or ALT >= 3 x ULN, unless due to Gilbert's disease, malignant involvement of liver, or vanishing bile duct syndrome).
Bone marrow suppression (eg ANC < 750/mm^3 or Platelet count < 75,000/mm^3).
Significant, pre-existing co-morbid respiratory disease.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Unable to use an effective contraceptive method.
Primary central nervous system germ cell tumor.
Germ cell tumor with somatic malignant transformation.
Spermatocytic seminoma.
Pure ovarian or extragonadal dysgerminoma or seminoma.
Pure mature teratoma.
Pure immature teratoma with alpha-fetoprotein >= 1000 ng/mL.
Stage I testicular cancer patients who have undergone primary retroperitoneal lymph node dissection.
Poor risk germ cell tumor (eg age >= 11 years old and COG stage IV ovarian, COG stage II-IV extragonadal, or International Germ Cell Consensus Classification intermediate or poor risk testicular).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: AL amyloidosis. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease AND progression of light chain disease defined as dFLC > 20mg/L AND cardiac stage I, II, or IIIa disease based on the 2013 European Modification of the 2004 Standard Mayo Clinic Staging AND NOT Stage IIIB Amyloidosis based on the 2004 Mayo Clinic Criteria AND NOT meeting criteria for active multiple myeloma based on presence of CRAB criteria (though a ratio of involved versus uninvolved free light chain over 100 is allowed in the absence of CRAB criteria). Prior treatment required: At least one prior line of therapy (minimum 2 cycles) AND Daratumumab + CyBorD. Prior treatment excluded: BCMA-targeting bispecific antibodies OR BCMA-targeting CAR-T therapy OR belantamab mafodotin (if the patient is refractory to belantamab mafodotin or if belantamab mafodotin was the immediate past line of therapy, unless discontinued due to intolerance or adverse event). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2 or Karnofsky < 60%).
Insufficient bone marrow or organ function (eg Absolute leukocyte count < 3,000/mcL, Absolute neutrophil count < 1,000/mcL, Absolute platelet count < 75,000/mcL, Direct bilirubin > 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal, AST or ALT > 3 x institutional upper limit of normal, or Calculated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min).
Active chronic hepatitis B virus infection with detectable viral load or untreated/uncured hepatitis C virus infection with detectable viral load.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Active clinically significant autoimmune diseases.
Seropositive for human immunodeficiency virus (HIV).
Severe, uncontrolled orthostatic hypotension resulting in syncopal or pre-syncopal events despite optimized medical management.
Plan for autologous stem cell transplant during the first 6 months of protocol therapy.
History of acute coronary syndrome or uncontrolled ventricular arrhythmias within 3 months prior to screening.
Left ventricular systolic dysfunction (LVEF < 30%).
Presence of severe valvular stenosis or severe congenital heart disease.
History of sustained ventricular tachycardia, aborted ventricular fibrillation, or atrioventricular nodal or sinoatrial nodal dysfunction without a permanent pacemaker or implantable cardioverter-defibrillator.
QT corrected by Fridericia (QTcF) > 550 msec without a permanent pacemaker or implantable cardioverter-defibrillator.
Screening EKG showing acute myocardial ischemia or active conduction system abnormalities (excluding First degree AV block, Second degree AV block Type 1, bundle branch blocks, controlled atrial fibrillation, or benign bifascicular block).
Major surgery requiring general anesthesia within 4 weeks of randomization or planned during the study.
NYHA class IV symptoms or acute decompensation of congestive heart failure.
Transplant eligible participants who have not undergone transplant.
Residual toxicities > Grade 1 from prior anti-cancer therapy (except alopecia).
Concurrent use of other investigational agents for this condition.
History of allergic reactions to elranatamab.
Active malignancy (excluding adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, in situ cervical cancer, adequately treated stage I cancer in remission for over 2 years, or low-risk prostate cancer with Gleason score < 7 and PSA < 10ng/mL).

History of allogeneic organ transplantation.
Allergy or hypersensitivity to study drugs or components.
Requirement for systemic corticosteroids (> 10 mg daily or prednisone equivalent) or other immunosuppressive medications.

Chronic concomitant treatment with strong CYP3A4 inducers.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (WHO performance status > 2).
American Society of Anaesthesiologist (ASA) score >= 3.
Congestive heart failure.
Angina pectoris.
Myocardial infarction within 1 year before randomization.
Uncontrolled arterial hypertension (blood pressure >= 150/100 mm Hg).
Uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia.
Active and uncontrolled infections.
Inflammation of the urinary bladder (interstitial cystitis) and/or obstructions of the urine flow.
Other invasive malignancy within 5 years (except adequately treated non-melanoma skin cancer, localized cervical cancer, or localized and Gleason <= 6 prostate cancer).
Uncontrolled severe illness, infection, or medical condition (including uncontrolled diabetes).
Hypersensitivity to doxorubicin, ifosfamide, dacarbazine, or their metabolites or excipients.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Female and male patients of reproductive potential who are not willing to employ effective birth control method.
Any psychological, familial, sociological or geographical condition potentially hampering compliance with the study protocol and follow-up schedule.
Known contraindication to imaging tracer and to MRI.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer and asymptomatic (no pain due to prostate cancer). Prior treatment required: Continuous androgen ablative therapy (surgical castration or LHRH agonist/antagonist) and disease progression while on combination or sequential abiraterone acetate plus androgen deprivation therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Enzalutamide or apalutamide or darolutamide or other investigational androgen receptor targeted treatment or chemotherapy with docetaxel or cabazitaxel for castration resistant prostate cancer. Biomarkers required: Serum testosterone < 50 ng/dl and screening PSA >= 1.0 ng/mL. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG >= 3)
Urinary self-catheterization for voiding due to obstruction secondary to prostate cancer or benign prostatic hyperplasia
Evidence of disease that puts the patient at risk from testosterone therapy (eg femoral metastases with fracture risk, extensive spinal metastases with spinal cord compression risk, or extensive liver metastases)
Serious or unstable pre-existing medical, psychiatric, or other condition
Active uncontrolled infection, including HIV/AIDS or hepatitis B or C
Mental impairment compromising ability to give informed consent or compliance
Use of anticoagulation therapy with warfarin, rivaroxaban, or apixaban
Prior history of a thromboembolic event within the last 12 months not treated with systemic anticoagulation
Hematocrit > 51%
Untreated severe obstructive sleep apnea
Uncontrolled or poorly controlled heart failure
Allergy to sesame seed oil or cottonseed oil
Recent major surgery requiring general anesthesia or unhealed surgical wound

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Adult. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Primary myelofibrosis. Histology allowed: Bone marrow fibrosis grade 2 or 3. Cancer burden allowed: Intermediate-2 or high-risk myelofibrosis as defined by the Dynamic International Prognostic Scoring System Plus (DIPSS+) and splenomegaly defined as palpable spleen measurement >= 5 cm below left costal margin or spleen volume >= 450 cm3 and baseline platelet count >= 100 x 10^9/L and at least 2 symptoms each with an average score >= 3 or an average total score of >= 12 as measured by the Myelofibrosis Symptom Assessment Form (MFSAF) v4.0. Prior treatment required: Ruxolitinib for >= 24 weeks that was stopped due to lack of spleen response (refractory) or loss of spleen response or symptom control after a previous response (relapsed) or was continued despite relapsed/refractory status. Prior treatment excluded: B-cell lymphoma 2 homology 3 (BH3)-mimetic compound or bromodomain and extra-terminal (BET) inhibitor or phosphoinositide 3-kinase inhibitor or telomerase inhibitor or more than 1 Janus Kinase 2 (JAK2) inhibitor or stem cell transplant. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Eligible for stem cell transplantation at the time of study entry.
Receiving medication that interferes with coagulation or platelet function.
Receiving anticancer therapy for an active malignancy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Adult. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Primary myelofibrosis. Histology allowed: Bone marrow fibrosis grade 2 or 3. general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG greater than 1).
Renal dysfunction (eg creatinine greater than 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal or estimated creatinine clearance less than 51mL/min).
Liver dysfunction (eg moderate to severe hepatic impairment Child-Pugh B or C).
Significant cardiovascular impairment (eg uncontrolled hypertension, congestive heart failure of New York Heart Association Grade II or above, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within the past 6 months, or serious cardiac arrhythmia within the past 6 months).
Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) positive.
Hepatitis B virus (HBV) positive or Hepatitis C virus (HCV) positive (unless HCV is treated and cured with an undetectable viral load).
Diagnosis of immunodeficiency.
History of radiation pneumonitis.
Respiratory impairment (eg pulse oximeter reading less than 92% at rest or requirement for supplemental oxygen).
Gastrointestinal disorder that would interfere with the passage or absorption of oral medications.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg active infection and serious non-healing wounds or ulcers).
Psychiatric illness or social situations that would limit compliance with study requirements.
Pregnant or breastfeeding.
Bone marrow dysfunction (eg absolute neutrophil count less than 1,500/mcL, hemoglobin less than 10.0 g/dL, or platelets less than 100,000/mcL).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: mantle cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: mantle cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory AND presence of evaluable disease. Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of systemic therapy for mantle cell lymphoma AND BTK inhibitor failure AND CD20 directed therapy. Prior treatment excluded: bispecific antibody therapy directed against CD20 and CD3. Biomarkers required: tumor genomic profiling AND CD20 expression. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2).
Inadequate bone marrow function (eg ANC < 500 cells/mcL, Hgb < 7.0 g/dL, or platelet count < 25,000 cells/mcL).
Renal dysfunction (eg creatinine clearance < 30 ml/min).
Hepatic dysfunction (eg total bilirubin > 5 x upper limit of normal, or AST or ALT > 5 x upper limit of normal).
Active hepatitis B or C.
History of human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) unless CD4+ T cell count >= 250 cells/mcL and no AIDS-defining opportunistic infections within 1 year and stable antiretroviral regimen with viral load < 400 copies/mL.
Active concurrent malignancy requiring active therapy within the last 3 years (except basal cell carcinoma of the skin, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, carcinoma in situ of the cervix or breast, adequately treated lentigo maligna melanoma, or localized prostate cancer).
Pregnant or lactating.
Known active bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic, or other infection.
Clinically significant history of liver disease.
Active central nervous system involvement with lymphoma.
Grade 2 or higher toxicity due to prior cancer therapy.
History of severe allergic or anaphylactic reactions to monoclonal antibody therapy.
History of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.
History of hypersensitivity to IMiDs or their excipients.
Autologous or allogenic stem cell transplantation within 3 months prior to signing informed consent, or evidence of graft-versus-host-disease, or receiving active immunosuppression for graft-versus-host-disease.
History of deep venous thrombosis, threatening thromboembolism, or known thrombophilia, or high risk for thromboembolic event if unwilling or unable to take prophylaxis.
Significant uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (eg New York Heart Association Class III or IV, Objective Assessment Class C or D, myocardial infarction <= 6 months from registration, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable arrhythmia, or unstable angina) or significant pulmonary disease (eg obstructive pulmonary disease or history of bronchospasm).
Chronic active Epstein-Barr viral infection or reactivation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2).
Known central nervous system involvement.
Diagnosed or treated for another malignancy within 3 years prior to enrollment (except complete resection of basal cell carcinoma or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, an in-situ malignancy, or low risk prostate cancer after curative therapy).
Currently active graft versus host disease.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular conditions (eg uncontrolled hypertension, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, grade 3 thromboembolic event or myocardial infarction within the past 6 months).
Seropositive for active viral infection with human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), hepatitis B (HBV), or hepatitis C viral (HCV).
Pregnancy or lactation.
History of erythema multiforme or severe hypersensitivity to prior immunomodulating agents or known allergy to study medications.
Inability to tolerate thromboprophylaxis.
Failure to have fully recovered (Grade > 2 according to CTCAE v 5) from the reversible effects of prior chemotherapy.
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance < 30 ml/min).
Liver dysfunction (Total Bilirubin > 1.5 X upper limit of normal, AST > 3x upper limit of normal, or ALT > 3x upper limit of normal).
Severe hematologic impairment (Absolute neutrophil count < 1,000 cells/dL, platelet count < 75,000 cells/dL, or hemoglobin < 8.0 g/dL).
Systemic treatment with strong CYP3A inducers or inhibitors or strong inhibitor of CYP1A2.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Short life expectancy (eg <= 12 weeks).
Inadequate organ and marrow function (eg Absolute neutrophil count < 1.5 x 10^9/L, Platelets < 100,000/uL, Hemoglobin < 8.0 g/dL, Total bilirubin > ULN, ALT or AST > 2.5 x ULN, Creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min).
Known active central nervous system metastasis.
High dose systemic corticosteroids (eg > 10 mg of prednisone or equivalent).
Uncontrolled Type 1 or Type 2 diabetes.
Crigler-Najjar syndrome or Gilbert's syndrome.
Pregnancy or nursing.
Concomitant active malignancy or previous malignancy within 2 years.
Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular disease (eg history of acute myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndromes, symptomatic congestive heart failure Grade 2 or higher, cardiac arrhythmia, conduction abnormality, or uncontrolled hypertension).
History of symptomatic drug-induced pneumonitis.
HIV infection with low CD4 count (< 350 cells/uL), history of AIDS with opportunistic infection within 12 months, or high viral load (> 400 copies/mL) while not on stable antiretroviral therapy.
Positive hepatitis B virus core antibody (unless antigen and HBV DNA PCR negative) or positive hepatitis C virus antibody (unless PCR negative for HCV RNA following curative treatment).
Congenital long QT syndrome or QTc interval (QTcF) > 470 msec.
Use of prohibited medications (eg proton pump inhibitors, moderate or strong UGT2B7 inhibitors/inducers, or specific OAT1, OAT3, BCRP, or OATP1B1 substrates).
Pre-menopausal status without agreement to ovarian suppression (for specific breast cancer cohorts).
Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) <= 50% (for specific breast cancer cohorts).

Biomarkers excluded: KRAS mutation or PTEN deleterious mutation or negative PTEN protein expression.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Short life expectancy (eg <= 12 weeks).
Inadequate organ and marrow function (eg Absolute neutrophil count < 1.5 x 10^9/L, Platelets < 100,000/uL, Hemoglobin < 8.0 g/dL, Total bilirubin > ULN, ALT or AST > 2.5 x ULN, Creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min).
Known active central nervous system metastasis.
High dose systemic corticosteroids (eg > 10 mg of prednisone or equivalent).
Uncontrolled Type 1 or Type 2 diabetes.
Crigler-Najjar syndrome or Gilbert's syndrome.
Pregnancy or nursing.
Concomitant active malignancy or previous malignancy within 2 years.
Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular disease (eg history of acute myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndromes, symptomatic congestive heart failure Grade 2 or higher, cardiac arrhythmia, conduction abnormality, or uncontrolled hypertension).
History of symptomatic drug-induced pneumonitis.
HIV infection with low CD4 count (< 350 cells/uL), history of AIDS with opportunistic infection within 12 months, or high viral load (> 400 copies/mL) while not on stable antiretroviral therapy.
Positive hepatitis B virus core antibody (unless antigen and HBV DNA PCR negative) or positive hepatitis C virus antibody (unless PCR negative for HCV RNA following curative treatment).
Congenital long QT syndrome or QTc interval (QTcF) > 470 msec.
Use of prohibited medications (eg proton pump inhibitors, moderate or strong UGT2B7 inhibitors/inducers, or specific OAT1, OAT3, BCRP, or OATP1B1 substrates).
Pre-menopausal status without agreement to ovarian suppression (for specific breast cancer cohorts).
Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) <= 50% (for specific breast cancer cohorts).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed and refractory disease. Prior treatment required: idecabtagene vicleucel AND at least 4 prior lines of therapy AND an immunomodulatory drug AND a proteasome inhibitor AND an anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody AND achievement of at least a partial response or better per International Myeloma Working Group criteria following idecabtagene vicleucel. Prior treatment excluded: anti-BCMA bispecific antibody. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1).
Smoldering multiple myeloma, plasma cell leukemia, POEMS syndrome, or amyloidosis.
Recent stem cell transplant (within 12 weeks) or active graft-versus-host disease.
Active hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, Severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV2), HIV, or any active, uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection.
Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular diseases (e.g., acute myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndromes, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias, thromboembolic or cerebrovascular events, or prolonged QT syndrome).
Any other active malignancy within 3 years prior to enrollment.
Ongoing Grade 2 or higher peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy.
History of Guillain-Barre syndrome or variants, or history of any Grade 3 or higher peripheral motor polyneuropathy.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Known or suspected hypersensitivity to the study intervention or its excipients.
Receipt of other investigational agents for this condition.
Toxicity from previous anticancer therapy > grade 1, except for alopecia and peripheral neuropathy.
Medical or psychiatric conditions (including recent suicidal ideation/behavior) that may increase risk or make the participant inappropriate for the study.
Severe organ or marrow dysfunction (e.g., LVEF < 40%, absolute neutrophil count < 1000/microlitre, platelet count < 25,000/mcL, hemoglobin < 8 g/dL, creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min, serum total bilirubin >= 2 mg/dL, or ALT/AST >= 2.5 x upper limit of normal).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 5 to 60 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: classic Hodgkin lymphoma. Histology allowed: nodular sclerosis. Cancer burden allowed: Stage I or Stage II. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior chemotherapy or radiation or antibody-based treatment for classic Hodgkin lymphoma. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active interstitial pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Poorly controlled inherited or acquired immunodeficiency or organ transplant recipient.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg infection, autoimmune conditions, cardiac arrhythmias, angina pectoris, gastrointestinal disorders affecting swallowing or absorption of pills).
Peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 1 or Charcot-Marie-Tooth syndrome.
Prior or concurrent malignancy whose natural history or treatment has the potential to interfere with safety or efficacy assessment.
Prior solid organ transplant.
Prior allogeneic stem cell transplantation.
Pregnancy.
Lactating females who plan to breastfeed.
Refusal to use highly effective contraception.
Poor performance status (eg Zubrod score > 2 for adults or Lansky score < 50 for children).
Renal dysfunction (eg adult creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min or pediatric glomerular filtration rate < 50 mL/min/1.73 m^2).
Liver dysfunction (eg total bilirubin >= 2 x upper limit of normal or AST >= 3 x upper limit of normal or ALT >= 3 x upper limit of normal, unless due to Gilbert's disease, lymphomatous involvement of liver, or vanishing bile duct syndrome).
Cardiac dysfunction (eg shortening fraction < 27% or ejection fraction < 50%).
Pulmonary dysfunction (eg diffusion capacity of the lung for carbon monoxide < 50% of predicted value and pulse oximetry <= 92% on room air).
Nodular lymphocyte predominant Hodgkin lymphoma.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 70 years and older. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: prior endocrine therapy or palbociclib or ribociclib or chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: (estrogen-receptor positive or progesterone receptor positive) and HER2-negative. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Unstable central nervous system involvement or brain metastases requiring immediate local therapy.
History of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis.
Poor hematologic function (e.g., absolute neutrophil count < 1.5 X 10^9/L, platelets < 100 x 10^9/L, or hemoglobin < 8 g/dL).
Liver dysfunction (e.g., ALT or AST > 5 x upper limit of normal with liver metastases, ALT or AST > 3.0 x upper limit of normal without liver metastases, or elevated total bilirubin).
Severe renal impairment (e.g., creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min).
Recent major surgery or failure to recover from major side effects.
Current use of prohibited medications, herbal medications, or other investigational therapies.
Active systemic bacterial, fungal, or detectable viral infection (e.g., HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C).
Impairment of gastrointestinal function or gastrointestinal disease that may significantly alter drug absorption.
History of syncope of cardiovascular etiology, ventricular arrhythmia of pathological origin, or sudden cardiac arrest.
Concurrent severe or uncontrolled medical conditions.
Inability to swallow oral medications.
Prior malignancy diagnosed within 2 years with evidence of disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: High-risk disease defined as (cT3a or cT3b or prostate specific antigen > 20 ng/mL prior to starting androgen deprivation therapy or Gleason Score 8-10 or pelvic node positive with a short axis of at least 1.0 cm) and no cT4 disease and prostate gland volume less than 100 cc prior to initiation of androgen deprivation therapy and no metastatic disease outside of the pelvic nodes. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior radiotherapy to the region of the study cancer and prior radical prostatectomy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Bone marrow biopsy showing complete remission (CR) or complete remission with incomplete count recovery (CRi) or morphologic leukemia free state (MLFS) and presence of measurable residual disease (MRD+) determined by flow cytometry or next generation sequencing or PCR (excluding cases with only persistent DNMTA, TET2 or ASXL1 mutations), and confirmed haploidentical or fully HLA-matched related donor. Prior treatment required: current treatment with hypomethylating agent (azacitidine or decitabine) and venetoclax and receipt of at least 1 cycle of hypomethylating agent (azacitidine or decitabine) and venetoclax. Prior treatment excluded: allogeneic stem cell transplant, organ transplant, donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI), CAR-T cell, or NK cell therapy. Biomarkers required: Assessed during screening; any of the following: 2022 ELN adverse risk karyotype (t(6;9)(p23.3;q34.1)/DEK::NUP214 or t(v;11q23.3)/KMT2A-rearranged or t(9;22)(q34.1;q11.2)/BCR::ABL1 or t(8;16)(p11.2;p13.3)/KAT6A::CREBBP or inv(3)(q21.3q26.2) or t(3;3)(q21.3;q26.2)/ GATA2, MECOM(EVI1) or t(3q26.2;v)/MECOM(EVI1)-rearranged or -5 or del(5q) or -7 or Complex karyotype or monosomal karyotype) or 2022 ELN adverse risk mutation (mutated TP53 or ASXL1 or BCOR or EZH2 or RUNX1 or SF3B1 or SRSF2 or STAG2 or U2AF1 or ZRSR2) or mutation associated with acquired resistance to venetoclax (mutated NRAS or KRAS or FLT3 ITD/TKD). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Hepatic dysfunction (direct bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN, except 3 x ULN for Gilbert's or disease-related hemolysis, or AST/ALT > 3 x ULN).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance < 45 mL/min).
Respiratory dysfunction (oxygen saturation < 90% on room air).
Cardiac dysfunction (LVEF < 40%, NYHA Functional Classification > 2B, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, or cardiac arrhythmia).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Autoimmune disease (history of inflammatory bowel disease including ulcerative colitis or Crohn's Disease, or symptomatic disease requiring steroids > 10 mg prednisone equivalent or other immunosuppressive therapies, including rheumatoid arthritis, systemic progressive sclerosis, systemic lupus erythematosus, autoimmune vasculitis, or motor neuropathy of autoimmune origin).
HIV-positive.
Active uncontrolled hepatitis B or C.
History of another malignancy (unless complete remission for at least 2 years, or diagnosed and treated within 2 years for nonmetastatic melanoma, surgically resected squamous cell carcinoma of skin, or nonmetastatic prostate cancer not needing systemic chemotherapy).
Severe allergic reactions to IL-2 or other agents used in study.
Receipt of other investigational agents for acute myeloid leukemia.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (including active infection, psychiatric illness, or social situations limiting compliance).
History of Grade 2 or higher hemolytic anemia.
Live vaccines within 6 months.
Moderate or strong CYP3A inhibitors (excluding antifungal medications like posaconazole or voriconazole if venetoclax dose has been adjusted).
Presence of donor-specific antibodies (DSAs) with mean fluorescence intensity (MFI) > 1000 without a desensitization protocol.
Persisting Grade > 1 non-hematologic toxicity related to prior therapy (excluding alopecia, sensory neuropathy Grade <= 2, or other Grade <= 2 not constituting a safety risk).
Systemic steroid therapy (> 10 mg prednisone equivalent) on the day of NK cell infusion.
