Biomarkers excluded: BRD4-NUT rearrangement.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Untreated central nervous system metastases.
Symptomatic or untreated leptomeningeal metastases or spinal cord compression.
Use of enzyme-inducing anticonvulsants.
History of allergic reactions to ZEN003694 or similar compounds.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg ongoing active infection requiring systemic treatment, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness).
Myocardial infarction or unstable angina within 6 months prior to first dose.
Gastrointestinal disorder affecting drug absorption (eg malabsorption syndrome or major bowel or stomach resection).
Concurrent use of other investigational agents.
Use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors, strong CYP3A4 inducers, narrow therapeutic window CYP1A2 substrates, or proton pump inhibitors.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Therapeutic-dose anticoagulation.
Radiation to more than 25% of the bone marrow.
Significant cardiac abnormalities (eg clinically significant conduction abnormalities or arrhythmias, NYHA class II or greater congestive heart failure, or acute coronary syndromes/stenting within the past 3 months).
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2 for patients 16 years or older, or Lansky < 50% for patients under 16 years).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: endometrial cancer. Histology allowed: endometrioid endometrial cancer. Cancer burden allowed: measurable stage III, measurable stage IVA, stage IVB (with or without measurable disease), or recurrent (with or without measurable disease). Prior treatment required: combination carboplatin, paclitaxel, and pembrolizumab (minimum 4 cycles and maximum 10 cycles). Prior treatment excluded: CDK4/6 inhibitors (including abemaciclib). Biomarkers required: ER-positive (>= 1 percent of tumor cell nuclei being immunoreactive by immunohistochemistry) and TP53 wild-type (determined by immunohistochemistry or CLIA-certified targeted Next-Generation Sequencing) and mismatch repair proficient (determined by immunohistochemistry or polymerase chain reaction or any CLIA-certified next generation sequencing assay). Biomarkers excluded: tumor mutational burden >= 10 mutations/megabase.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Insufficient bone marrow function (ANC < 1.5 x 10^9/L, Platelets < 100 x 10^9/L, or Hemoglobin < 8 g/dL).
Hepatic dysfunction (Total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN, except Gilbert's syndrome up to 2.0 x ULN, or ALT and AST > 3 x ULN).
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine > 1.5 x institutional ULN and Creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min/1.73 m2).
Gastrointestinal dysfunctions interfering with drug absorption (e.g., bowel obstruction, malabsorption syndrome, unresolved nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea CTCAE v 5.0 > grade 1).
Active systemic bacterial infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, fungal infection, or detectable viral infection (e.g., HIV positive, active hepatitis B, or active hepatitis C).
Major injuries or surgery within 14 days prior to randomization or planned major surgery during study.
Other malignant disease with disease-free interval <= 3 years (except curatively treated carcinoma in situ of the cervix, basal cell carcinoma of the skin, or ductal carcinoma in situ of the breast).
Active brain metastases.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Serious or uncontrolled preexisting medical conditions (e.g., interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis, severe dyspnea at rest or requiring oxygen therapy, severe renal impairment [estimated creatinine clearance < 30ml/min], history of major surgical resection involving the stomach or small bowel, preexisting Crohn's disease, or ulcerative colitis).
Requirement for concomitant therapy with strong CYP3A4 inducers or strong CYP3A4 inhibitors.
Personal history of syncope of cardiovascular etiology, ventricular arrhythmia of pathological origin, or sudden cardiac arrest.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2).

Pregnant women or breastfeeding women.
History of a prior allogeneic hematologic stem cell transplant.
Known central nervous system (CNS) involvement.
Concurrent use of other investigational agents.

Vaccination with live, attenuated vaccines within 4 weeks of trial start.
Uncontrolled active systemic infection or recent infection requiring intravenous antibiotics completed within 7 days of trial start.
Known bleeding disorders (eg von Willebrand's disease) or hemophilia.
History of stroke, intracranial hemorrhage, or recent major bleed within 6 months.
Requirement for warfarin or other vitamin K antagonists.
Concomitant treatment with medications that are moderate or strong CYP3A inhibitors or inducers.
Known history of human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), active hepatitis C virus (HCV), or hepatitis B virus (HBV).
History of allergic reactions to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to zanubrutinib or venetoclax.
Psychiatric illness or social situations that would limit compliance with study requirements.
Malabsorption syndrome, disease significantly affecting gastrointestinal function, resection of the stomach or small bowel, symptomatic inflammatory bowel disease or ulcerative colitis, or partial or complete bowel obstruction.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease (eg uncontrolled arrhythmia, NYHA Class 3 or 4 congestive heart failure, or history of myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or acute coronary syndrome within 6 months).
Any life-threatening illness, medical condition, or organ system dysfunction that could compromise participant safety or trial integrity.
Active or ongoing autoimmune anemia or autoimmune thrombocytopenia.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: non-clear cell renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: papillary. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: more than 1 systemic line of therapy or tivozanib or nivolumab. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG >2).
Inadequate organ or marrow function (e.g., absolute neutrophil count <1,000/mcL, platelets <100,000/mcL, total bilirubin >institutional ULN, AST >3 x institutional ULN, ALT >3 x institutional ULN, creatinine >1.5 x institutional ULN, or eGFR <30 ml/min).
Current hepatitis C virus infection that is not treated and cured or has a detectable viral load.
Chronic hepatitis B virus infection with a detectable viral load while on suppressive therapy.
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease requiring immediate CNS specific treatment.
Cardiac disease with New York Heart Association Functional Classification worse than class 2B.
Residual toxicities > Grade 1 from prior anti-cancer therapy (except alopecia).
Receipt of other investigational agents.
History of allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to tivozanib or nivolumab.
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic blood pressure > 150 mmHg or diastolic blood pressure > 100 mmHg on 2 or more antihypertensive medications).
History of autoimmune disorders (excluding vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, or chronic skin conditions not requiring systemic therapy, or inactive disease without treatment in the last 3 years).
Active human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection unless on effective anti-retroviral therapy with undetectable viral load within 6 months.
Current systemic corticosteroid use greater than prednisone 10 mg daily or equivalent.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Receiving concomitant CYP3A inducers.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: non-small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Histologically or cytologically confirmed. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced (metastatic or unresectable). Prior treatment required: 1 platinum-based chemotherapy regimen and PD-(L)1 antibody therapy, and approved targeted drugs (such as EGFR inhibitors or ALK inhibitors or MET inhibitors) if the tumor bears actionable mutations. general_exclusion_criteria:
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min)
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN, except for patients with Gilbert's syndrome who are excluded if total bilirubin > 3.0 x ULN or direct bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN, or ALT > 3 x ULN, or AST > 3 x ULN)
Hematologic dysfunction (ANC < 1.0 x 10^9/L, Platelet count < 75 x 10^9/L, or Hemoglobin < 9 g/dL)
Impaired cardiac function or clinically significant cardiac disease (including congestive heart failure NYHA Grade >= 2, uncontrolled hypertension, clinically significant arrhythmia, QTcF > 470 msec on screening ECG, congenital long QT syndrome, or acute myocardial infarction or unstable angina pectoris within 3 months prior to study entry)
Symptomatic CNS metastases, or CNS metastases that require local CNS-directed therapy or increasing doses of corticosteroids within 2 weeks prior to study entry
Active COVID-19 infection
Unable or unwilling to swallow capsules

general_exclusion_criteria:
Unable to swallow oral medication.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Participation in another interventional study.
Leptomeningeal disease.
Known coagulopathy.
History of another concurrent primary cancer (except curatively resected non-melanoma skin cancer or curatively treated carcinoma in situ, or other previously treated malignancies if disease-free for 3 years at screening).
Known diagnosis of replication repair-deficient glioma (eg, constitutional mismatch repair deficiency or Lynch syndrome).
Known hypersensitivity to components or metabolites of vorasidenib or temozolomide.
Significant active cardiac disease (eg, New York Heart Association Class III or IV congestive heart failure, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or stroke) or QTcF >= 450 msec or other factors increasing risk of QT prolongation or arrhythmic events.
Known active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infections, positive human immunodeficiency virus antibody results, or acquired immunodeficiency syndrome related illness (unless appropriately suppressed or immune).
Poor performance status (eg, KPS or LPPS < 70).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance < 40 mL/min).
Bone marrow dysfunction (Absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mm3 or hemoglobin < 9 g/dL or platelets < 100,000/mm3).
Hepatic dysfunction (Serum total bilirubin > 1.5x ULN, AST or ALT > ULN, or Alkaline phosphatase > 2.5x ULN).
Short life expectancy (expected survival < 3 months).

Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Inadequate bone marrow function (absolute neutrophil count < 1.5x10^9/L, platelet count < 100.0x10^9/L, or hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL).

Pregnancy or lactation.
Prior organ transplantation.

Congestive heart failure (> New York Heart Association Class II), active coronary artery disease, unevaluated new onset angina within 3 months, unstable angina, or clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias.
QTc interval > 480 msec.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced or metastatic. Prior treatment required: progressed or non responsive to available therapies and no standard or available curative therapy exists. Prior treatment excluded: prior therapy with CID-078. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Known or suspected brain metastases or spinal cord compression unless the condition has been asymptomatic, treated with surgery and/or radiation, and has been stable without requiring escalating doses of corticosteroids or anti-convulsant medications for at least four weeks.
Known hypersensitivity to CID-078 or any drugs similar in structure or class.
Past medical history or clinically active interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis.
History of congestive heart failure Class III/IV or serious cardiac arrhythmias requiring treatment.
Heart rate corrected QT (QTc) interval > 470 msec.
Current treatment with medication known to prolong the QT/QTc interval or history of additional risk factors for Torsade de Pointes.
Pregnant or lactating women.
History of another primary malignancy within two years prior to starting study drug except for adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, or cancer of the cervix in situ.
Malabsorption syndrome or other conditions that may interfere with adequate absorption of investigational product.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including uncompensated respiratory, cardiac, hepatic, or renal disease, active infection (including untreated human immunodeficiency virus and active clinical tuberculosis), symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness/social situations limiting compliance.
Unstable or poorly controlled endocrinopathies.
Active hepatitis B infection (positive HbsAg test and detectable HBV DNA).
Active hepatitis C infection (reactive HCV antibody test and detectable HCV RNA).
For patients with a history of HIV or AIDS: CD4+ T-cell count < 350 cells/uL, opportunistic infection within the previous 12 months, not on current antiretroviral therapy for a minimum of four weeks, or HIV viral load >= 400 copies/mL.
Current treatment with a strong CYP3A4 inhibitor, strong CYP3A4 inducer, Pgp inhibitor, or CYP3A4 sensitive substrate.
Active bleeding disorders.
Prior solid organ transplantation.
Unresolved toxicity of Grade >= 2 from previous anti-cancer treatment except for alopecia and skin pigmentation.

Any unresolved clinically significant Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade >= 2 toxicity.

Current use of a cytochrome P3A4 inhibitor, cytochrome P3A4 inducer, or strong uridine diphosphate-glucuronosyltransferase 1A1 (UGT1A1) inhibitors.
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. general_exclusion_criteria:
Ongoing treatment with certain anticancer agents.
Major surgery without complete recovery.
Spinal cord compression, leptomeningeal disease, or clinically active central nervous system metastases.
Toxicity from prior therapy that has not completely resolved to baseline.
Active or prior documented autoimmune or inflammatory disorders within the past 5 years.
Active, uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infections requiring systemic therapy.
Inability to swallow, impairment of gastrointestinal function, or gastrointestinal disease that may significantly alter the absorption of the trial drugs.
Inadequate cardiac and/or vascular function (including receipt of treatment for unstable angina, myocardial infarction, and/or cerebro-vascular attack within the prior 6 months, mean QTcF >= 470 ms, or Class II or greater congestive heart failure).
Other invasive malignancy within 2 years.
Poor performance status (Karnofsky Performance Status less than 70).

Biomarkers required: KRAS G12C-mutant. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Ongoing treatment with certain anticancer agents.
Major surgery without complete recovery.
Spinal cord compression, leptomeningeal disease, or clinically active central nervous system metastases.
Toxicity from prior therapy that has not completely resolved to baseline.
Active or prior documented autoimmune or inflammatory disorders within the past 5 years.
Active, uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infections requiring systemic therapy.
Inability to swallow, impairment of gastrointestinal function, or gastrointestinal disease that may significantly alter the absorption of the trial drugs.
Inadequate cardiac and/or vascular function (including receipt of treatment for unstable angina, myocardial infarction, and/or cerebro-vascular attack within the prior 6 months, mean QTcF >= 470 ms, or Class II or greater congestive heart failure).
Other invasive malignancy within 2 years.
Poor performance status (Karnofsky Performance Status less than 70).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years old. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: solid tumors. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic. Prior treatment required: disease progression after treatment with, intolerance to, ineligibility for, or refusal of available therapies, including anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 or anti-CTLA4 therapy if applicable. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Untreated or progressed brain or central nervous system metastases
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression with corticosteroids
Active hepatitis B virus, active hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus positive
History of organ transplant, including allogeneic stem cell transplantation
Clinically significant or uncontrolled cardiac disease (eg vasculitis, aneurisms, vascular malformations, or myocarditis)
Chronic systemic steroids (> 10 mg/day of prednisone or equivalent)
Chronic or current active infectious disease requiring systemic antibiotics, antifungal, or antiviral treatment
Any known additional malignancy that is progressing, requires active treatment, or occurred within 2 years
Residual toxicities of prior therapy > Grade 1
Modifications in anticoagulation therapies within the last 3 months
Bowel obstruction within 60 days prior to first dose of treatment

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: leiomyosarcoma. Histology allowed: leiomyosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease and not candidates for curative resection and grade greater than I. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: systemic therapy for metastatic disease (other than hormone therapy) or anthracyclines or lurbinectedin or trabectedin. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Inadequate hematological function (eg hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL or absolute neutrophil count < 2.0 x 10^9/L or platelet count < 100 x 10^9/L)
Hepatic dysfunction (eg ALT > 3.0 x ULN or AST > 3.0 x ULN or total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN)
Renal dysfunction (eg calculated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min)
Cardiac dysfunction (eg left ventricular ejection fraction <= 50% or myocardial infarction or angina within the year prior to enrollment or severe vascular disease or symptomatic arrhythmia)
Immunodeficiency (including HIV infection)
Chronic active hepatitis or cirrhosis
Active uncontrolled infection
Known myopathy
History of malignancies other than leiomyosarcoma within three years prior to enrollment (excluding malignancies with a negligible risk of metastasis or death)
Pregnancy or breast feeding
Inability to comply with treatment or protocol follow-up
Need for rapid tumor shrinkage

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic disease not amenable to curative resection. Prior treatment required: First line induction therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Any PI3K inhibitor or AKT inhibitor or mTOR inhibitor or any agent whose mechanism of action is to inhibit the PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway in the locally advanced or metastatic setting, and any prior systemic non-hormonal anti-cancer therapy for locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer prior to initiation of induction therapy. Biomarkers required: HER2-positive (assessed during screening via central testing) and PIK3CA-mutated (assessed during screening via central testing). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) less than 50%.
Inadequate hematologic or organ function.
History or active inflammatory bowel disease.
Type 1 diabetes or Type 2 diabetes requiring ongoing systemic treatment.
Active HBV infection.
Clinically significant and active liver disease (including severe liver impairment, viral or other hepatitis, current alcohol abuse, or cirrhosis).
Symptomatic active lung disease (including pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease).
History of leptomeningeal disease or carcinomatous meningitis.
Concurrent ocular or intraocular condition requiring medical or surgical intervention.
Active inflammatory or infectious conditions in either eye or history of idiopathic or autoimmune-associated uveitis in either eye.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: non-ocular melanoma. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable Stage III or Stage IV. Prior treatment required: anti-PD(L)1 mAb and progression on anti-PD(L)1 mAb and anti-CTLA-4 mAb. Prior treatment excluded: Immune-mobilizing monoclonal T-cell receptor Against Cancer (ImmTAC) medication. Biomarkers required: HLA-A*02:01-positive (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: BRAF V600 mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnant or lactating women.
History of a malignant disease other than non-ocular melanoma.
Ineligible to be retreated with pembrolizumab due to a treatment-related adverse event.
Known untreated or symptomatic central nervous system metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis.
Previous severe hypersensitivity reaction to treatment with another monoclonal antibody.
Active autoimmune disease requiring immunosuppressive treatment.
Clinically significant cardiac or pulmonary disease or impaired cardiac function.
Known psychiatric or substance abuse disorders.
Ongoing Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade 2 or higher clinically significant events.
Known history of chronic viral infections such as hepatitis B virus (HBV) or hepatitis C virus (HCV).
Out of range laboratory values.
History of allogenic tissue/solid organ transplant.
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: non-ocular melanoma. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable Stage III or Stage IV. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: endometrial cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced or metastatic. Prior treatment required: at least 2 lines of standard therapy (including adjuvant or neoadjuvant treatment) or cancer is refractory to standard of care or no standard treatment is available. Prior treatment excluded: anthracyclines with a cumulative dose exceeding 360 mg/m2. Biomarkers required: HER2 expression (IHC 1+ to 3+ or HER2 gene amplification or activating mutation in tumor specimen by ISH or NGS), assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG PS > 1)
Serious cardiac dysfunction (eg left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%, severe heart disease, prolonged QT interval QTc > 470 msec, complete left bundle branch block, or Grade 3 atrioventricular block)
Inadequate organ function (eg INR > 1.5xULN, APTT > 1.5xULN, or urinary protein > 2+ or > 1000 mg/24 hours)
Active autoimmune diseases or inflammatory diseases
Other malignant tumors diagnosed within 3 years prior to the first administration
Poorly controlled hypertension
Advanced or clinically significant lung diseases (eg poorly controlled chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, asthma, restrictive lung disease, or pulmonary hypertension)
Stroke or transient ischemic attack within 6 months before enrollment
Thromboembolic event (eg deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism) within 6 months before enrollment unless clinically stable and receiving adequate anticoagulant therapy
Primary tumors in the central nervous system
Active or untreated central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis
Pre-existing Grade >= 2 peripheral neuropathy
History of allergies to recombinant humanized antibodies, human-mouse chimeric antibodies, or any components of BL-M07D1
Treatment with systemic glucocorticoids > 10 mg/day equivalent of prednisone (except for COPD, antiemetic, or infusion reactions)
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection (unless undetectable HIV RNA, CD4 count >= 350 cells/uL, no AIDS-defining opportunistic infection within 12 months, and on stable antiretroviral therapy)
Active hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection
Active tuberculosis
Active infections requiring intravenous antibiotic, antiviral, or antifungal treatment
Pregnancy or breastfeeding

Serious cardiac dysfunction (eg left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%, severe heart disease, prolonged QT interval QTc > 470 msec, complete left bundle branch block, or Grade 3 atrioventricular block)
Inadequate organ function (eg INR > 1.5xULN, APTT > 1.5xULN, or urinary protein > 2+ or > 1000 mg/24 hours)
Active autoimmune diseases or inflammatory diseases
Other malignant tumors diagnosed within 3 years prior to the first administration
Poorly controlled hypertension
Advanced or clinically significant lung diseases (eg poorly controlled chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, asthma, restrictive lung disease, or pulmonary hypertension)
Stroke or transient ischemic attack within 6 months before enrollment
Thromboembolic event (eg deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism) within 6 months before enrollment unless clinically stable and receiving adequate anticoagulant therapy
Primary tumors in the central nervous system
Active or untreated central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis
Pre-existing Grade >= 2 peripheral neuropathy
History of allergies to recombinant humanized antibodies, human-mouse chimeric antibodies, or any components of BL-M07D1
Treatment with systemic glucocorticoids > 10 mg/day equivalent of prednisone (except for COPD, antiemetic, or infusion reactions)
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection (unless undetectable HIV RNA, CD4 count >= 350 cells/uL, no AIDS-defining opportunistic infection within 12 months, and on stable antiretroviral therapy)
Active hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection
Active tuberculosis
Active infections requiring intravenous antibiotic, antiviral, or antifungal treatment
Pregnancy or breastfeeding

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: epithelial (excluding low grade serous). Cancer burden allowed: recurrent and platinum-resistant (progression within 6 months of last receipt of platinum). Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of platinum-based systemic therapy. Prior treatment excluded: BCL family inhibitors. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: low grade serous histology.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2 or Karnofsky < 60%).
Insufficient bone marrow function (absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mcL, platelets < 100,000/mcL, or hemoglobin <= 9 g/dL).
Liver dysfunction (total bilirubin > 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal or AST or ALT > 3 x institutional upper limit of normal).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine > 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal and glomerular filtration rate < 50 ml/min).
Cardiac dysfunction (left ventricular ejection fraction < institutional lower limit of normal or < 50%, or New York Heart Association Functional Classification > 2B).
Uncontrolled Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection (detectable viral load).
Uncontrolled chronic hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection (detectable viral load).
Untreated or uncontrolled hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection.
Untreated brain metastases or brain metastases showing evidence of progression by scan or requiring systemic steroids for less than 4 weeks.
Pregnant or breastfeeding women.
Concurrent use of medications or substances that are strong inhibitors or inducers of CYP3A4.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness.
Retinal pathology (evidence of retinal pathology on ophthalmologic examination, neurosensory retinal detachment, right ventricular outflow (RVO), or neovascular macular degeneration).
Gastrointestinal disorder affecting absorption of oral medications (e.g., malabsorption syndrome, major bowel or stomach resection, or evidence of small or large bowel obstruction within the past 3 months).
Dependence on intravenous fluids or total parenteral nutrition.
Residual toxicities from prior anti-cancer therapy > grade 1 (except alopecia).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 11 to 50 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: testicular germ cell tumour. Histology allowed: seminoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease or non-testicular primary, and intermediate or poor prognosis as defined by IGCCC classification. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: previous chemotherapy or radiotherapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Other primary malignancy (except adequately treated non-melanomatous carcinoma of the skin, germ cell tumour, or other malignancy treated at least 5 years previously with no evidence of recurrence).
Significant cardiac disease resulting in inability to tolerate IV fluid hydration for cisplatin.
Significant co-morbid respiratory disease that contraindicates the use of bleomycin.
Peripheral neuropathy >= grade 2 or clinically significant sensorineural hearing loss or tinnitus.
Concurrent illness, including severe infection.
Inadequate contraception.
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to any of the study drugs.
Presence of any psychological, familial, sociological or geographical condition that would hamper compliance with the study protocol and follow-up schedule, including alcohol dependence or drug abuse.
Inadequate bone marrow function (ANC < 1.0 x 10^9/L or platelet count < 100 x 10^9/L).
Inadequate liver function (bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN, or > 2.0 x ULN for participants with Gilbert's Syndrome; ALT or AST > 2.5 x ULN, or > 5 x ULN if elevations are due to hepatic metastases).
Inadequate renal function (estimated creatinine clearance < 60 ml/min).
Poor performance status (ECOG > 3).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Known active infections requiring ongoing treatment (bacterial, fungal, viral including HIV positivity).
Gastrointestinal disease (eg Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, or short gut syndrome) or other malabsorption syndromes.
Mean resting corrected QT interval (QTc) > 480 msec.
Clinically important abnormalities in rhythm, conduction, or morphology of resting ECG (eg complete left bundle branch block, third degree heart block, second degree heart block, PR interval > 250 msec).
Factors that increase the risk of QTc prolongation or risk of arrhythmic events such as heart failure, congenital long QT syndrome, or family history of long QT syndrome.
Peripheral neuropathy of CTCAE grade 2 or higher.
Treatment with or anticipation of the need for treatment with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers.
Potential allergies to repotrectinib and/or its excipients.
Major surgery within 14 days of start of repotrectinib treatment.

Inability to undergo radiation therapy if necessary.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable HER2-negative (including HER2 low). Prior treatment required: If hormone receptor positive (ER positive or PR positive), must have progressed or be intolerant to all available endocrine therapy regimens, or not be candidates for further endocrine therapy-based approaches. Prior treatment excluded: More than 2 prior lines of cytotoxic chemotherapy for metastatic disease (including antibody drug conjugates and checkpoint inhibitors); clinical progression on a PARP inhibitor; receipt of a PARP inhibitor (including olaparib) within 12 months; any previous treatment with CSF1R antibody. Biomarkers required: Germline or somatic mutation in BRCA1 predicted to be deleterious or suspected deleterious. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Poor bone marrow function (leukocytes < 3000/mcL, absolute neutrophil count < 1.5 x 109/L, or platelets < 100 x 109/L).
Liver dysfunction (total bilirubin > 1.5x institutional upper limit of normal, or AST/ALT > 2.5x institutional upper limit of normal, or AST/ALT > 5x institutional upper limit of normal for participants with liver metastases).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance < 51 mL/min).
Uncontrolled HBV, HCV, or HIV infection.
Uncontrolled central nervous system metastases.
History of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukemia.
QTcF > 470msec on screening ECG.
Gastrointestinal disorders likely to interfere with absorption of oral medication.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (including uncontrolled hypertension, ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness).
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Known active or latent tuberculosis.
Major surgical procedure within 28 days prior to initiation of protocol therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Low grade serous ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: Low grade serous. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: MEK inhibitor or CDK4/6 inhibitor. Biomarkers required: (KRAS or NRAS or rare RAF fusions) assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: BRAF V600E alteration or RB1 mutation or two copy RB1 deletion.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or nursing. Poor performance status (e.g., ECOG >= 2). Absolute neutrophil count (ANC) < 1,500/mm^3. Platelet count < 100,000/mm^3. Hemoglobin <= 9 g/dL. Renal dysfunction (creatinine > 1.5 x upper limit of normal or calculated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min). Liver dysfunction (total bilirubin > 1.5 x upper limit of normal, or AST/ALT > 2.5 x upper limit of normal). Creatine phosphokinase (CPK) > 2.5 x upper limit of normal. Inability to swallow oral formulations or impaired gastrointestinal absorption due to gastrectomy or active inflammatory bowel disease. History of interstitial lung disease, idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug-induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or evidence of active pneumonitis. History of bowel perforation or intestinal fistulas in the last 6 months. Active skin disorder that has required systemic therapy within the past 1 year. History of rhabdomyolysis. Concurrent ocular disorders including history of glaucoma, retinal vein occlusion, predisposing factors for retinal vein occlusion (such as uncontrolled hypertension or uncontrolled diabetes), retinal pathology, intraocular pressure > 21 mm Hg, corneal erosion, corneal degeneration, active or recurrent keratitis, or other serious ocular surface inflammatory conditions. Hypersensitivity to any of the study drugs. Prior allogeneic stem cell or solid organ transplantation. Untreated or unstable central nervous system metastases. Residual CTCAE >= grade 2 toxicity from any prior anticancer therapy (except grade 2 alopecia). Uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), hepatitis B virus (HBV), or hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection. Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) < 50%. Chronic concomitant treatment with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or strong CYP3A4 inducers. Exposure to P-glycoprotein (P-gp) inhibitors or inducers.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Body surface area less than 0.25 m^2
Renal dysfunction (defined as serum creatinine above age/sex specific thresholds, 24-hour urine creatinine clearance less than 70 mL/min/1.73 m^2, or GFR less than 70 mL/min/1.73 m^2)
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin greater than 1.5 x upper limit of normal or SGPT/ALT greater than 10 x upper limit of normal)
Cardiac dysfunction (Shortening fraction less than 27% or Ejection fraction less than 50%)
Contraindication to Peripheral Blood Stem Cell collection
Bone marrow failure syndromes
Chronic immunosuppressive medications (except for prevention/treatment of allergic reactions and adrenal replacement therapy)
Primary immunodeficiency syndrome requiring ongoing immune globulin replacement therapy
Pregnancy
Lactation
Refusal to use effective contraceptive method (for sexually active patients of reproductive potential)

Cancer burden allowed: International Neuroblastoma Risk Group Stage L2, MS, or M. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior anti-cancer therapy (except topotecan and cyclophosphamide initiated on an emergent basis, a single cycle of chemotherapy per a low or intermediate risk neuroblastoma regimen, or localized emergency radiation). Biomarkers required: MYCN amplification. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Body surface area less than 0.25 m^2
Renal dysfunction (defined as serum creatinine above age/sex specific thresholds, 24-hour urine creatinine clearance less than 70 mL/min/1.73 m^2, or GFR less than 70 mL/min/1.73 m^2)
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin greater than 1.5 x upper limit of normal or SGPT/ALT greater than 10 x upper limit of normal)
Cardiac dysfunction (Shortening fraction less than 27% or Ejection fraction less than 50%)
Contraindication to Peripheral Blood Stem Cell collection
Bone marrow failure syndromes
Chronic immunosuppressive medications (except for prevention/treatment of allergic reactions and adrenal replacement therapy)
Primary immunodeficiency syndrome requiring ongoing immune globulin replacement therapy
Pregnancy
Lactation
Refusal to use effective contraceptive method (for sexually active patients of reproductive potential)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: adenoid cystic carcinoma. Histology allowed: adenoid cystic carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: distant metastases with 1 to 5 detectable sites of metastatic disease at any organ or site (including bone and central nervous system involvement), maximum size of 3 cm for brain lesions, all metastatic foci amenable to stereotactic body radiation therapy, and primary tumor either controlled or amenable for local treatment with stereotactic body radiation therapy. Additional sites of disease are allowed if they are less than 1 cm in maximum diameter or stable over the last 3 months. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2)
Need for urgent surgical intervention for metastatic central nervous system or spine disease
Bone metastasis in a femoral bone for which surgical stabilization is recommended
Pregnancy or lactation
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness)
Progressing additional malignancy or additional malignancy requiring active treatment

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: adenoid cystic carcinoma. Histology allowed: adenoid cystic carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: distant metastases with at least 1 site of non-osseous disease and at least one metastatic focus amenable to local ablative treatment (radiation therapy or radiofrequency ablation or microwave ablation or cryoablation or bland embolization or chemoembolization). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: NCCN low risk or NCCN intermediate risk, and localized, and node negative, and no extracapsular extension, and prostate volume less than or equal to 80cc, and no PIRADS 4 or 5 lesion on prostate MRI contacting the urethra. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: overlapping pelvic radiotherapy or transurethral resection of the prostate or prostate high-intensity focused ultrasound or cryoablation. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Poor performance status (ECOG > 2 or Karnofsky < 60%). Baseline grade 3 or higher gastrointestinal or genitourinary toxicity. AUA/IPSS score greater than 15. Active inflammatory bowel disease or active collagen vascular disease. Inability to undergo prostate MRI. Inability to undergo prostate fiducial marker placement. Ongoing receipt of cytotoxic chemotherapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, unstable cardiac arrhythmia). Psychiatric illness or social situations that would limit the safety of a biopsy and/or surgery. Uncorrectable bleeding or coagulation disorder.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: systemic anti-light chain amyloidosis. Histology allowed: histologically-proven systemic anti-light chain amyloidosis confirmed by positive Congo red staining with green birefringence on polarized light microscopy. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory; AL Amyloidosis Cardiac Risk stage I, II, or IIIa based on the 2013 European Modification of the 2004 Standard Mayo Clinic Staging (defined as NT-proBNP <= 8500 pg/mL); plasma cell burden < 60%; absence of bone lesions and other end organ disease consistent with multiple myeloma. Prior treatment required: at least one line of treatment. Prior treatment excluded: ixazomib citrate or venetoclax. Biomarkers required: translocation t(11;14) (assessed during screening or documented with a prior biopsy). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2).
Leukopenia (leukocytes < 3,000/mcL).
Neutropenia (absolute neutrophil count < 1,000/mcL).
Thrombocytopenia (platelets < 75,000/mcL).
Liver dysfunction (total bilirubin >= 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal, or AST/ALT >= 3 x institutional upper limit of normal).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance < 15 mL/min).
Cardiac dysfunction (left ventricular ejection fraction < 35%, New York Heart Association classification III/IV, or symptomatic congestive heart failure).
Central nervous system involvement.
Active serious or systemic infection.
Active hepatitis B virus or hepatitis C virus infection.
Uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or recent myocardial infarction.
History of allergic reactions to venetoclax, ixazomib citrate, or dexamethasone.
Use of strong or moderate CYP3A inhibitors or strong or moderate CYP3A inducers.
Peripheral neuropathy >= grade 3, or grade 2 with pain on clinical examination.
Grade 3 or worse diarrhea.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Known gastrointestinal disease or procedure that could interfere with oral absorption.
Psychiatric illness or social situations that would limit compliance.
Residual toxicities > grade 1 from prior anti-cancer therapy (except alopecia).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Adult. Sex allowed: Female or Male. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced breast cancer (locally advanced disease not amenable to curative treatment or metastatic disease). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Systemic anti-cancer therapy for locoregionally recurrent or metastatic disease. Biomarkers required: Hormone receptor positive and HER2-negative (IHC 0, 1+, or 2+/ ISH non-amplified) and germline tumor loss of function mutation in BRCA1. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1). History of MDS or AML. Known predisposition to bleeding. History of persisting severe cytopenia. Severe or uncontrolled systemic diseases or active uncontrolled infections. Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal disease, inability to swallow the formulated product, or previous significant bowel resection. History of another primary malignancy. Persistent toxicities (CTCAE Grade >= 2) caused by previous anti-cancer therapy. Spinal cord compression, brain metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease. Active and uncontrolled hepatitis B or hepatitis C. Active and uncontrolled HIV infection. Active tuberculosis infection. Cardiac arrhythmia and cardiovascular disease. Concurrent exogenous reproductive hormone therapy or non-topical hormonal therapy for non-cancer-related conditions.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: non-small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced, locoregionally recurrent and not amenable to curative therapy, or metastatic. Prior treatment required: exhausted all standard therapy available or intolerant to standard therapy, and for patients with actionable mutations, progressed on all approved targeted therapies or had approved targeted therapies contraindicated. Prior treatment excluded: prior treatment with any immune checkpoint discontinued due to immune-related adverse events or immune-related adverse events not resolved to Grade 1 or better or immune-related adverse events requiring steroids >10 mg prednisone equivalent per day. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1)
Inadequate hematologic function (hemoglobin <9 g/dL or absolute neutrophil count <1.0 x 10^9/L or platelet count <75 x 10^9/L)
Inadequate hepatic function (total bilirubin >1.5 times upper limit of normal or AST and ALT >3.0 times upper limit of normal, or >5.0 times upper limit of normal if liver metastases are present)
Inadequate renal function (estimated creatinine clearance <30 mL/minute)
Uncontrolled brain metastasis
Active infection requiring parenteral or oral antibiotics
Serious uncontrolled medical disorder
Psychiatric illness or social circumstance limiting compliance with trial requirements
Active second invasive malignancy (excluding stable prostate cancer on watchful waiting, in situ cervical cancer, in situ breast carcinoma, or localized non-melanoma skin cancers)
Uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease (New York Heart Association classification III or IV)
Baseline QTc >470 msec in patients without pacemaker
Autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy (daily prednisone equivalent doses >10 mg/day), excluding vitiligo, type I diabetes, Grave's disease, or Hashimoto thyroiditis
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease
Live vaccines reception within 30 days of enrollment
Known active hepatitis B or C
HIV positive status unless CD4+ T cell counts are >=350 cells/uL and no opportunistic infection within the past 12 months and on established antiretroviral therapy for at least 4 weeks and HIV viral load less than 400 copies/mL
Known or suspected allergy to trial treatment, excipients, or related products

Inadequate renal and metabolic function (serum creatinine above age/gender thresholds or creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min/1.73 m2 or amylase > 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal or lipase > 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal).
Inadequate thyroid function (TSH > 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal).
Receipt of other investigational agents.
Receipt of chronic steroids (>= 2mg/kg/day of body weight or >= 20mg/day of prednisone or equivalent for persons weighing >= 10kg administered for >= 14 consecutive days).
Receipt of anti-inflammatory or immunosuppressive medications.
Known autoimmune disease (except childhood asthma or atopic dermatitis).
History of human immunodeficiency virus or acquired immunodeficiency syndrome.
History of allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to pembrolizumab or severe allergy to monoclonal antibody therapies (anaphylaxis).
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (including ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, or psychiatric illness/social situations limiting compliance).
Prior solid organ transplantation.

Prior treatment required: progression on or following anti-PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor (unless contraindicated) and progression on or following therapy for actionable mutations (such as EGFR or ALK mutations) if present. Prior treatment excluded: ADAM9 targeted agent or more than 2 prior lines of cytotoxic chemotherapy for advanced or metastatic disease. Biomarkers required: ADAM9 expression (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Any underlying medical or psychiatric condition impairing participant's ability to receive, tolerate, or comply with the planned treatment or study procedures.
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal metastases.
Prior stem cell, tissue, or solid organ transplant.
Another malignancy that required treatment within the past 2 years, excluding non-melanomatous skin cancer, localized prostate cancer (Gleason Score < 6), or carcinoma in situ.
Active viral, bacterial, or fungal infection.
Unacceptable physical condition or laboratory values.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Adult. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: colorectal cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable, locally advanced or metastatic; relapsed or refractory. Prior treatment required: progression during or following standard therapy with fluoropyrimidine-based chemotherapy and oxaliplatin and irinotecan (unless contraindicated, refused, or unavailable) and progression after prior targeted treatment for colorectal cancer with actionable mutations (such as EGFR, KRAS, BRAF, and MSI-H/dMMR) if present. Prior treatment excluded: ADAM9 targeted agent or more than 2 lines of cytotoxic chemotherapy for advanced or metastatic disease or more than 4 lines of systemic regimens for advanced or metastatic disease. Biomarkers required: ADAM9 expression (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: primary mediastinal B-cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: B-cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: progression after at least two prior lines of therapy. Prior treatment excluded: CAR T-cell therapy or PD-1 inhibitor or PD-L1 inhibitor with less than 6 months of response. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (e.g., ECOG > 1)
Low left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF < 50%)
Inadequate hematologic function (absolute neutrophil count < 1,000/uL, hemoglobin < 8 g/dL, or platelet count < 50,000/uL)
Hepatic dysfunction (total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN, direct bilirubin > ULN, or AST/ALT > 2.5 x ULN)
Renal dysfunction (serum creatinine > 1.5 x ULN or creatinine clearance < 40 ml/min)
Patients in urgent need of cytoreductive therapy
Receiving other investigational agents
History of other malignancies, except for those treated with curative intent with no active disease for at least 2 years, adequately treated non-melanoma skin cancer, adequately treated carcinoma in situ, or low-risk prostate cancer on active surveillance
Pregnant or lactating
Known active lymphomatous involvement of the central nervous system
Uncontrolled active systemic infection or recent infection requiring intravenous antibiotics
Known history of Hepatitis B surface antigen reactive or known active Hepatitis C virus infection
History of Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV)-infection
Significant liver disease such as hepatitis or cirrhosis
Prior macrophage activation syndrome (MAS) or hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH)
Uncontrolled arrhythmia, Class 3 or 4 congestive heart failure, or history of myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or acute coronary syndrome within 6 months
Known history of central nervous system or neurologic disease including stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within 3 months or seizure disorder
Prior solid organ or allogeneic stem cell transplant or recent autologous stem cell transplant
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years
Chronic systemic steroid therapy (exceeding 10 mg daily of prednisone equivalent) or other immunosuppressive therapy
Active pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease
Severe hypersensitivity (Grade 3 or higher) to pembrolizumab
Known psychiatric or substance abuse disorders
Failure to meet requirements for standard of care CAR-T therapy

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Any advanced cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced cancer (defined as unlikely to be cured or controlled with treatment) and any brain tumors or brain metastases must have been successfully treated. Prior treatment required: Approved therapies with a known clinical benefit (must have progressed on or be intolerant to these, unless documented refusal) and chronic opioid pharmacotherapy for pain with an Oral Morphine Equivalent >= 200mg/day. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2)
Insufficient marrow or organ function (Platelets < 50,000/mcL or AST/ALT > 5 x institutional ULN)
Significant suicide risk (as defined by C-SSRS items 4 and/or 5 within the past 6 months)
Primary psychotic disorder, major depressive disorder with psychotic features, bipolar affective disorder type 1, dissociative identity disorder, or ongoing substance use disorder
Use of serotoninergic antidepressants, Serotonin and Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibitors (SNRIs), Tricyclic Antidepressants (TCAs), Efavirenz, serotonin-acting dietary supplements (5-hydroxy-tryptophan or St. John's wort), centrally-acting serotonergic agents (e.g. MAO inhibitors), antipsychotics, mood stabilizers, aldehyde dehydrogenase inhibitors, or significant inhibitors of UGT 1A0 or UGT 1A10 that cannot be tapered
Significant hematological, endocrine, cerebrovascular, cardiovascular, coronary, pulmonary, renal, gastrointestinal, immunocompromising, or neurological disease (including seizure disorder)

Risk factors for Torsade de pointes, heart failure, myocardial infarction, or coronary artery disease

Condition impairing oral intake or digestive absorption
Hypersensitivity to any ingredient of the investigational medicinal product

History of another active malignancy in the previous 3 years (excluding superficial bladder cancer, in situ carcinoma, or basal or squamous cell skin cancer treated curatively).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: asymptomatic (no present indication for treatment by iwCLL 2018 criteria and anticipated not to require treatment within the next 6 months) and no lymph node greater than 5 cm and no central nervous system involvement and no transformation of chronic lymphocytic leukemia (Richter's Syndrome). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: chemotherapy or targeted therapies or immunotherapy (including monoclonal antibodies) or radiotherapy or hormonal therapy for chronic lymphocytic leukemia and anti-PD1 or anti PD-L1 or anti PD-L2 agents. Biomarkers required: immunoglobulin heavy chain variable (IGHV) region gene unmutated (defined as <= 2% mutated compared to germline) and at least 7 designable immunizing peptides (assessed during screening via complete exome and transcriptome sequencing). Biomarkers excluded: del(17p) by fluorescence in situ hybridization in >= 10% of cells.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Impaired organ or marrow function (eg abnormal total bilirubin, AST(SGOT)/ALT(SGPT) > 2.5 x institutional upper limit of normal, or absolute neutrophil count < 1000 cells/uL).
Pregnant women or nursing women.
Active, known, or suspected autoimmune disease or immunosuppressive conditions (except vitiligo, type 1 diabetes, residual autoimmune-related hypothyroidism requiring hormone replacement, or psoriasis not requiring systemic treatment).
Uncontrolled autoimmune cytopenia.
Known chronic infections with HIV, hepatitis B or hepatitis C.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia).
History of an invasive malignancy (unless disease-free for at least 3 years and low risk for recurrence, or has history of carcinoma in situ of the breast, oral cavity or cervix, or basal cell or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, or prostate cancer managed with active surveillance not expected to limit survival to < 10 years).
History of severe allergic reactions attributed to any vaccine therapy for the prevention of infectious diseases or compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to poly-ICLC.
Participants who have never received the tetanus vaccine.
Concomitant therapy with immunosuppressive or immunomodulatory agents or chronic use of systemic corticosteroids.
Previous bone marrow or stem cell transplant.
Receiving any other investigational agents.
Serum immunoglobulin level < 400 mg/dL or currently requiring chronic intravenous immunoglobulin G (IVIG).
Lymphocyte doubling time < 6 months in patients with white blood cell count > 30,000/uL.
Any underlying medical condition, psychiatric condition or social situation that would compromise study administration or assessment of adverse events.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Adults. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumors. Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced and relapsed or refractory. Prior treatment required: All locally available standard therapies. Prior treatment excluded: Autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplant or allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant or solid organ transplant or agent targeting CD24. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- History of a previous additional malignancy (unless potentially curative treatment has been completed with no evidence of malignancy for 5 years, or the malignancy was basal cell carcinoma of the skin, Stage I melanoma, melanoma in situ, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, early-stage prostate cancer, or carcinoma in situ excluding bladder, or early-stage breast cancer with no recurrence for 2 years after treatment).
- Active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.
- Inadequate hepatic function (AST or ALT > 2.5 times ULN, or > 5 times ULN if liver metastases; total bilirubin > 1.5 times ULN, or > 3 times ULN for patients with elevations due to Gilbert syndrome; lipase or amylase > 2 times ULN).
- Inadequate renal function (calculated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min).
- Inadequate bone marrow function (absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/uL or platelet count < 100,000/uL or hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL or < 5.6 mmol/La).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 60 years or < 60 years and in the opinion of the treating physician are better served by azanucleoside-based therapy rather than intensive, cytarabine-based induction. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: NA. general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or breast-feeding.
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin > 2X institutional upper limit of normal or AST/ALT > 3.0 x institutional upper limit of normal).
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min/1.73m^2).
Baseline corrected QT interval >= 480 msec.
Active or uncontrolled infection.
Medical necessity for ongoing treatment with a strong CYP3A4 inducing drug.
Cardiac disease (New York Heart Association Functional Classification worse than class 2B).
Detectable HBV viral load while on suppressive therapy or untreated/active HCV infection.

Patients with only mature teratoma upon tumor sampling at diagnosis and negative tumor markers.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Solid malignancies. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced (unresectable) or metastatic. Prior treatment required: Progressed after all available standard therapy for the specific tumor type or no standard therapy exists or standard therapies are intolerable or considered inappropriate by the Investigator. Prior treatment excluded: Therapy directly inhibiting PPARG or RXRA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Female patients who are pregnant or breastfeeding.
Adverse events from prior therapy that have not returned to baseline or stabilized at Grade 1 (except alopecia, hearing loss, vitiligo, endocrinopathy managed with replacement therapy, and Grade 2 or lower neuropathy).
Prior major surgery (excluding placement of vascular access) within 4 weeks before study drug administration.
History of another malignancy in the previous 2 years, unless cured by surgery alone and continuously disease free (certain exceptions apply).
QT interval corrected using Fridericia's Formula (QTcF) greater than 470 msec in screening, congenital long QT syndrome, family history of long QT syndrome, or unexplained sudden death under 40 years of age in first degree relatives.
Known active diagnosis of lipodystrophy/lipoatrophy, or an ongoing need to receive medications known to cause lipodystrophy/lipoatrophy.
Any active uncontrolled systemic bacterial, viral, or fungal infection requiring treatment.
Known history of human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) seropositivity, unless there is no detectable viral load on highly active antiretroviral therapy (HAART).
Chronic hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection (unless utilizing universal prophylaxis or a pre-emptive treatment approach).
Active hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection, unless curative therapy has been completed and there is no detectable viral load.
Prior diagnosis of chronic or recurrent pancreatitis at any time or a diagnosis of acute pancreatitis within 6 months prior to screening.
Significant impairment of lung function indicated by resting oxygen saturations below 92% on room air or requiring chronic use of ambulatory supplemental oxygen.
Uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system (CNS) metastases, leptomeningeal disease, or carcinomatous meningitis.
Need for treatment with high doses of oral or intravenous steroids (greater than 10 mg/day prednisone or equivalent).
Concurrent participation in any other investigational therapeutic study.
Recent history (within 6 months) of serious uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, clinically significant ECG abnormalities (including second-degree Type II or third-degree atrioventricular node block), documented cerebrovascular event (stroke or transient ischemic attack), cardiomyopathy, myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndromes, coronary angioplasty, stenting, or bypass grafting.
Congestive heart failure (Class III or IV).
Recent history (within 6 months) of symptomatic pericarditis.
Thromboembolic events and/or bleeding disorders 28 days or less prior to the first dose of study drug.
Severe or uncontrolled systemic diseases, including uncontrolled hypertension and active bleeding diatheses.
Type 1 diabetes mellitus, or type 2 diabetes mellitus that is not adequately controlled with diet, exercise, or oral hypoglycemic agents and/or injectable agents other than insulin, or patients taking insulin.
Known hypersensitivity to FX-909 or any of its excipients.
Gastrointestinal disorders that may interfere with the ability to swallow tablets or absorb study medication.
Poor performance status (eg ECOG greater than 2).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or greater. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: cervical cancer. Histology allowed: all histologies. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic or recurrent. Prior treatment required: platinum-based therapy. Prior treatment excluded: any class of drugs targeted to the PD-1/PD-L1 pathway or any class of drugs targeting TIM3 pathway. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Poor marrow function (absolute neutrophil count < 1,500/mcL, platelets < 100,000/mcL, or hemoglobin < 9 g/dL).
Poor liver function (total bilirubin > 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal or direct bilirubin > 1.0 x institutional upper limit of normal, or AST/ALT > 2.5 x institutional upper limit of normal unless liver metastases are present, in which case > 5.0 x institutional upper limit of normal).
Poor renal function (creatinine > 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal and glomerular filtration rate < 30 mL/min/1.73 m2).
Active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.
Active autoimmune disease that might deteriorate when receiving an immunostimulatory agent.
Uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus infection (detectable HIV RNA within 4 weeks of study drug administration, presence of acquired immunodeficiency syndrome defining opportunistic infections within the past 12 months, or failure to be on antiretroviral therapy for at least 4 weeks).
Active hepatitis B, active hepatitis C, or active hepatic cirrhosis.
History of interstitial lung disease.
Uncontrolled current illness including symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, or cardiac arrhythmia.
Severe gastrointestinal conditions (clinical or radiographic evidence of bowel obstruction, uncontrolled diarrhea, or history of inflammatory bowel disease).
Prior organ transplantation, including allogeneic stem-cell transplantation.
Pregnant or breastfeeding women.
History of a different malignancy (unless disease-free for at least 5 years, deemed low risk for recurrence, or diagnosed and treated within the past 5 years for breast cancer in situ or basal cell or squamous cell carcinoma of skin).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 3 years to 75 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: severe aplastic anemia. Histology allowed: bone marrow cellularity < 25% or variable marrow cellularity with < 30% residual hematopoietic cells. Cancer burden allowed: acquired severe aplastic anemia defined by bone marrow hypocellularity and at least two of the following: neutrophils < 0.5 x10^9/L, platelets < 20 x10^9/L, or reticulocyte count < 20 x10^9/L (or < 60 x10^9/L using automated analysis), and no suitable fully matched related donor available. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: immunosuppressive therapy using antithymocyte globulin or calcineurin inhibitors or thrombopoietin receptor agonists or androgens, allogeneic stem cell transplant, solid organ transplant, and desensitization attempts for HLA antibodies (such as plasmapheresis, intravenous immunoglobulin, or mycophenolate mofetil). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: inherited bone marrow failure syndromes (such as Fanconi anemia or short telomere syndromes), clonal cytogenetic abnormalities consistent with myelodysplastic syndrome (such as monosomy 7), formal diagnosis of myelodysplastic syndrome, and donor specific HLA antibodies (DSA) with median fluorescence intensity (MFI) > 3000 (assessed during screening).
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor cardiac function (Left ventricular ejection fraction <= 40% or shortening fraction < 26%).
Hepatic dysfunction (total bilirubin >= 2.0 mg/dL unless Gilbert's disease is present).
Renal dysfunction (estimated creatinine clearance <= 60 mL/minute for patients > 13 years, or glomerular filtration rate < 90 mL/min/1.73 m2 for patients < 13 years).
Pulmonary dysfunction (DLCO <= 50% or FEV1 <= 50% predicted or FVC <= 50% predicted).
Poor performance status (Karnofsky or Lansky < 60%).
Uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infection.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Prior malignancies (except resected basal cell carcinoma, treated cervical carcinoma in situ, or cancer treated with curative intent > 5 years previously).
Known life-threatening reaction to Thymoglobulin.
Detectable viral load for HIV, Hepatitis B, or Hepatitis C.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive carcinoma. Prior treatment excluded: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy, adjuvant chemotherapy, or prior hormonal therapy. general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Pregnancy or nursing.
Unwillingness to employ adequate nonhormonal contraception.
Receiving other investigational agents for breast cancer.
Locally advanced tumors at diagnosis (eg tumors fixed to the chest wall, peau d'orange, skin ulcerations/nodules, or clinical inflammatory changes).
History of previous invasive breast cancer.
History of other malignancies (unless disease-free for at least 5 years, or the malignancy was cervical cancer in situ or non-melanoma cancer of the skin).
Unresolved systemic infection.
Renal failure requiring dialysis.
Active cardiac disease, history of congestive heart failure, or prior myocardial infarction.
Uncontrolled hypertension.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 2).
Hodgkin variant transformation of chronic lymphocytic leukemia.
Liver dysfunction (eg total bilirubin > 1.5 x ULN or 3.0 x ULN with Gilbert's Syndrome or liver involvement, or AST/ALT > 3.0 x ULN or 5.0 x ULN with liver involvement).
Renal dysfunction (eg creatinine > 2.0 x ULN or estimated CrCl < 50 ml/min).
Bone marrow dysfunction (eg absolute neutrophil count < 1.0 x 10^9/L or platelets < 30 x 10^9/L, unless bone marrow involvement is confirmed on biopsy).
Current or prior Grade 3/4 graft versus host disease (GVHD) or active GVHD.
Prior solid organ transplantation.
Current or past history of central nervous system (CNS) disease involvement, leptomeningeal disease, stroke, epilepsy, CNS vasculitis, or neurodegenerative disease.
History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug-induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, or ascites requiring recurrent drainage procedures.
History of known or suspected hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH).
Active or history of autoimmune disease.
History of severe allergic or anaphylactic reactions to monoclonal antibody therapy.
Uncontrolled HIV infection (CD4 count < 200 per microliter, detectable viral load, or unstable anti-retroviral therapy), or any HIV positive status for patients in the pirtobrutinib cohort.
History of Human T-Cell Leukemia Virus 1 (HTLV-1) infection.
Known active cytomegalovirus (CMV) infection.
Clinically significant liver disease, including cirrhosis and active viral or non-viral hepatitis.
Major episode of infection requiring hospitalization or treatment with IV antimicrobial within 4 weeks prior to start of study.
Cardiac disease (eg New York Heart Association Class III or IV heart failure, Objective Assessment Class C or D cardiac disease, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, or LVEF <= 40%).
Prolongation of the QT interval corrected for heart rate (QTcF) > 470 msec.
Clinically significant active malabsorption syndrome.
Active uncontrolled auto-immune cytopenia.
Requirement for therapeutic anticoagulation with warfarin or another vitamin K antagonist.
History of bleeding disorders (eg von Willebrand's disease, hemophilia).
History of stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within 6 months.
Pregnancy or breast-feeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: gastrointestinal stromal tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: inoperable or metastatic. Prior treatment required: prior progression on or intolerance to imatinib AND documented disease progression on sunitinib. Prior treatment excluded: bezuclastinib. Biomarkers required: primary KIT exon 11 mutation. Biomarkers excluded: PDGFRA mutation or SDHx mutation or BRAF mutation or NF1 mutation or unknown genotype.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Known or suspected hypersensitivity to bezuclastinib or sunitinib and their components.
Unacceptable toxicity with prior sunitinib.
Clinically significant cardiac disease (including clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias, congestive heart failure > New York Heart Association Class II, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within 6 months, congenital long QT syndrome, or repeated demonstration of a QTc interval > 470 milliseconds).
Arterial or venous thrombotic or embolic events (such as cerebrovascular accident, deep vein thrombosis, or pulmonary embolism) within 6 months.
Seropositive for human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) 1 or 2.
Untreated central nervous system metastatic disease.
Active, uncontrolled, systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infections.
Pregnant or nursing.
Gastrointestinal abnormalities precluding adequate absorption (e.g., significant nausea and vomiting, malabsorption, external biliary shunt, or significant bowel resection).
Any active bleeding, excluding hemorrhoidal or gum bleeding.
Severe and/or uncontrolled concurrent medical disease.

Known active hepatitis B infection (HBsAg positive) or known active hepatitis C infection (HCV RNA positive).
Human immunodeficiency virus infection that is not controlled by a stable regimen of highly active anti-retroviral therapy with a CD4 count above 250 cells/mcL and an undetectable viral load.
Primary brain tumor, brain metastases, leptomeningeal disease, or uncontrolled seizure disorders.
History of serious systemic disease including myocardial infarction, unstable angina, New York Heart Association grade II or greater congestive heart failure, unstable symptomatic arrhythmia, or significant vascular disease.
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
History of allogenic organ transplantation.
Clinical history of coagulopathy, bleeding diathesis, or thrombosis.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Requirement for total parenteral nutrition with lipids.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Clinical evidence of disease progression prior to enrollment.
Persistent prior treatment toxicities Grade 2 or above.
Inadequate renal function (creatinine clearance < 30ml/min).
Inadequate liver function (bilirubin > 2X institutional upper limit of normal, AST > 2X institutional upper limit of normal, or ALT > 2X institutional upper limit of normal).
Graft-versus-host disease grade III or IV.
Active acute or chronic GVHD of the liver.
History of veno-occlusive disease.
Concomitant use of tyrosine kinase inhibitors or sirolimus.
Second active malignancy other than non-melanoma skin cancer or carcinoma in situ.
Uncontrolled inter-current illness (including ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness/social situations that would limit compliance).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Evidence of myelodysplasia or cytogenetic abnormality indicative of myelodysplasia.
Active hepatitis B or C infection (polymerase chain reaction positive).
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >= 1 or 2 depending on trial phase).
Known allergies, hypersensitivity, or intolerance to study medications.

Severe valvular heart disease

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: colon cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Stage IIB or Stage IIC or Stage III and no radiographic evidence of overt metastatic disease and tumor distal extent greater than or equal to 12 cm from the anal verge or above the peritoneal reflection. Prior treatment required: en bloc complete gross resection (R0 resection). Prior treatment excluded: systemic chemotherapy or targeted therapy or immunotherapy or radiation therapy administered for colorectal cancer. Biomarkers required: microsatellite stable or intact mismatch repair proteins (assessed during screening) and circulating tumor DNA assessed by Signatera assay (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: MSI-H or dMMR.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1)
Inadequate hematologic function (absolute neutrophil count < 1500/mm3, except for patients with benign ethnic neutropenia where < 1300/mm3 is allowed; platelet count < 100,000/mm3; or hemoglobin < 9 g/dL)
Inadequate hepatic function (total bilirubin > ULN, alkaline phosphatase > 2.5 x ULN, AST > 2.5 x ULN, or ALT > 2.5 x ULN)
Inadequate renal function (serum creatinine > 1.5 x ULN and creatinine clearance < 50 mL/min)
Cardiac disease or history of treatment with cardiotoxic agents with New York Heart Association Functional Classification worse than class 2B
Sensory or motor neuropathy grade 2 or greater
Active seizure disorder uncontrolled by medication
Active or chronic infection requiring systemic therapy
Known homozygous dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase (DPD) deficiency
Gilbert's Syndrome or homozygosity for UGT1A1*28 polymorphism
Pregnancy or lactation
History of prior invasive colon malignancy
Other invasive malignancy within 5 years before study entry (except colonic polyps, non-melanoma skin cancer, or carcinoma-in-situ)
History of bone marrow or solid organ transplantation
Tumor-related bowel perforation
Synchronous primary rectal and/or colon cancers
Co-morbid illnesses or other concurrent disease that would make the patient inappropriate for study entry

general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1).
Major surgery within 4 weeks of start of treatment.
Wide field radiotherapy within 4 weeks or limited field radiotherapy for palliation within 2 weeks of start of treatment.
Unresolved toxicities from prior therapy greater than CTCAE Grade 1.
History of persisting severe pancytopenia.
Spinal cord compression unless asymptomatic, treated, and stable without corticosteroids > 10 mg prednisone/day.
Uncontrolled seizures, history of epileptic disorder, or use of more than 2 antiepileptic drugs.
History of severe brain injury or stroke.
Active bleeding diatheses.
Active infection including hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus (HIV).
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness within the last 12 months.
Known predisposition to bleeding.
Myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) or acute myeloid leukaemia (AML).
Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, or inability to swallow.
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to investigational product or excipients.
Contraindication to gadolinium-enhanced Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI).
Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) < 50%.
History of non-infectious interstitial lung disease (ILD) or pneumonitis that required steroids, or current suspected ILD/pneumonitis.
Prior pneumonectomy.
Pleural effusion, ascites, or pericardial effusion requiring drainage.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
