clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: bladder cancer. Histology allowed: urothelial carcinoma (predominant histology; variant histologies including neuroendocrine differentiation, sarcomatoid, micropapillary, glandular, trophoblastic, or Mullerian are allowed; any amount of squamous differentiation is allowed provided the tumor is not a pure squamous cell cancer). Cancer burden allowed: undergone radical cystectomy and lymph node dissection with no gross cancer at surgical margins and no evidence of residual cancer or metastasis, and (pT3-pT4 or pT0/x-pT4/N+) and (ineligible for adjuvant cisplatin chemotherapy due to creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min, CTCAE version 5 grade >= 2 audiometric hearing loss, CTCAE version 5 grade >= 2 peripheral neuropathy, or New York Heart Association Class III heart failure, or refuses adjuvant cisplatin chemotherapy). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: LAG-3 blockade, postoperative or adjuvant systemic therapy after radical surgery, and adjuvant radiation after radical surgery. Biomarkers required: central Signatera ctDNA assay result (ctDNA+ or ctDNA-) assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Pregnant or nursing.
Absolute Neutrophil Count < 1,200/mm^3.
Platelet count < 100,000/mm^3.
Hemoglobin < 8 g/dL.
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine > 1.5 x upper limit of normal and calculated creatinine clearance <= 30 mL/min).
Liver dysfunction (AST or ALT > 3 x upper limit of normal, or Total bilirubin > 1.5 x upper limit of normal, except in patients with Gilbert Syndrome who can have total bilirubin < 3.0 mg/dL).
Requirement for hemodialysis.
Current or prior history of myocarditis.
Grade >= 3 immune related adverse event with prior PD-1/PD-L1 blockade.
Active autoimmune disease or history of autoimmune disease that might recur, which may affect vital organ function or require immune suppressive treatment including systemic corticosteroids (> 10 mg daily prednisone equivalents).
Current pneumonitis or prior history of non-infectious pneumonitis that required steroids within the previous 5 years.
Known active hepatitis B (HBsAg reactive) or active hepatitis C (HCV RNA detected).
HIV infection with detectable viral load.
Concurrent antineoplastic therapy.
Current use of immunosuppressive agents (except permitted corticosteroids).
Live vaccine within 30 days prior to registration.

Cancer burden allowed: undergone radical surgery with no gross cancer at surgical margins and no evidence of residual cancer or metastasis, and (pT3-pT4 or pT0/x-pT4/N+) and (ineligible for adjuvant cisplatin chemotherapy due to creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min, CTCAE version 5 grade >= 2 audiometric hearing loss, CTCAE version 5 grade >= 2 peripheral neuropathy, or New York Heart Association Class III heart failure, or refuses adjuvant cisplatin chemotherapy). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: LAG-3 blockade, postoperative or adjuvant systemic therapy after radical surgery, and adjuvant radiation after radical surgery. Biomarkers required: central Signatera ctDNA assay result (ctDNA+ or ctDNA-) assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Pregnant or nursing.
Absolute Neutrophil Count < 1,200/mm^3.
Platelet count < 100,000/mm^3.
Hemoglobin < 8 g/dL.
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine > 1.5 x upper limit of normal and calculated creatinine clearance <= 30 mL/min).
Liver dysfunction (AST or ALT > 3 x upper limit of normal, or Total bilirubin > 1.5 x upper limit of normal, except in patients with Gilbert Syndrome who can have total bilirubin < 3.0 mg/dL).
Requirement for hemodialysis.
Current or prior history of myocarditis.
Grade >= 3 immune related adverse event with prior PD-1/PD-L1 blockade.
Active autoimmune disease or history of autoimmune disease that might recur, which may affect vital organ function or require immune suppressive treatment including systemic corticosteroids (> 10 mg daily prednisone equivalents).
Current pneumonitis or prior history of non-infectious pneumonitis that required steroids within the previous 5 years.
Known active hepatitis B (HBsAg reactive) or active hepatitis C (HCV RNA detected).
HIV infection with detectable viral load.
Concurrent antineoplastic therapy.
Current use of immunosuppressive agents (except permitted corticosteroids).
Live vaccine within 30 days prior to registration.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: > 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Triple negative breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: Residual invasive disease in the breast or lymph nodes after neoadjuvant therapy and surgery, without Stage IV (metastatic) disease and without evidence of recurrent disease following preoperative therapy and surgery. Prior treatment required: Neoadjuvant therapy and surgery and radiotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: Stimulatory or coinhibitory T-cell receptor agents (such as CTLA-4 or OX-40 or CD137) or HER2-directed agents or endocrine therapy for more than 4 weeks or topoisomerase 1 inhibitors or antibody-drug conjugates containing a topoisomerase inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor <= 10% and progesterone receptor <= 10% and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2-negative. Biomarkers excluded: Germline breast cancer gene (BRCA) mutations.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG > 1)
Inadequate organ function
Cardiac dysfunction (eg myocardial infarction or unstable angina pectoris within 6 months of enrollment, or history of serious ventricular arrhythmia, high-grade atrioventricular block, other cardiac arrhythmias, or Left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%)
Active serious infections requiring anti-microbial therapy
History of any prior ipsilateral or contralateral invasive breast cancer

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years of age or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumors. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or locally advanced and unresectable. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: PI3K inhibitor or AKT inhibitor or mTOR inhibitor. Biomarkers required: PI3K alpha mutation (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History (within 2 years before screening) of a solid tumor or hematological malignancy that is histologically distinct from the cancers being studied.
Symptomatic brain or spinal metastases.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (defined as HbA1c >=8% and/or FBG >=140 mg/dL and/or requiring or required insulin).
Toxicities from previous anticancer therapies that have not resolved to baseline levels or CTCAE grade <=1 (with the exception of alopecia and peripheral neuropathy).
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Inadequate organ function.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: bronchial neuroendocrine tumor. Histology allowed: well- or moderately-differentiated neuroendocrine tumor. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or locally-advanced/unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: peptide receptor radionuclide therapy (PRRT) and mammalian target of rapamycin (mTOR) inhibitors. Biomarkers required: somatostatin receptor (SSTR) positivity (defined as uptake greater than background liver in all measurable lesions) assessed via 68Ga-DOTATATE PET or other SSTR-PET scan during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or nursing.
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2).
Hemoglobin < 8.0 g/dL.
Platelet count < 75,000/mm^3.
Absolute neutrophil count (ANC) < 1,500/mm^3.
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine >= 1.5 x upper limit of normal and calculated creatinine clearance < 40 mL/min).
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin >= 2.0 x upper limit of normal, except in patients with Gilbert's syndrome where direct bilirubin is < 2.0 x upper limit of normal; or Aspartate aminotransferase (AST)/alanine aminotransferase (ALT) >= 3.0 x upper limit of normal; or Albumin < 2.8 g/dL; or decompensated liver cirrhosis).
Untreated or unstable central nervous system metastases.
Other currently active malignancy that requires therapy.
Active hepatitis B (HbsAg reactive with detectable viral load) or active hepatitis C virus.
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection with detectable viral load.
Active or uncontrolled infections requiring ongoing antifungals or antibiotics.
Receipt of live attenuated vaccines.
Symptomatic or treated drug-induced pneumonitis.
Medical condition causing an inability to swallow or impairment of gastrointestinal function that may significantly alter the absorption of an oral agent.
Hypersensitivity to everolimus or other rapamycin analogs.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Histology allowed: World Health Organization grade IV glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: Primarily supratentorial in location and residual tumor on post-operative imaging within 72 hours of resection not exceeding 1 cm in maximal diameter in biperpendicular planes. Prior treatment required: Surgical resection. Prior treatment excluded: temozolomide and anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 or anti-PD-L2 agents and stereotactic radiosurgery and placement of Gliadel (carmustine; BCNU) wafers and any other intratumoral or intracavitary treatment and brachytherapy and Novo-Tumor Treating Fields (Optune) and investigational therapeutic agents. Biomarkers required: Adequate tumor material for genomic sequencing (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg Karnofsky < 70).
Inadequate hematologic, renal, or hepatic function.
History of severe allergic reactions to vaccine therapy.
Active, known, or suspected autoimmune disease or immunosuppressive conditions requiring systemic treatment in the past 2 years.
Chronic infections with HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (eg active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia).
Pregnant or breastfeeding women.
History of an invasive malignancy (unless disease-free for 3 years, low risk for recurrence, or was carcinoma in situ of the breast, oral cavity, or cervix, or basal cell or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin).
Hypersensitivity to pembrolizumab.
Active tuberculosis.
Active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.
History of non-infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease requiring steroids, or current pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Greater than 1 year and less than 21 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Lymphoma. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Chemotherapy sensitive and in at least partial remission and planned for bone marrow infusion. Prior treatment required: Chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: Prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Biomarkers required: Available partially human leukocyte antigen (HLA)-mismatched unrelated donor (4/8-7/8 at HLA-A, -B, -C, and -DRB1) with age < 35 years. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Suitable HLA-matched related or 8/8 high-resolution matched unrelated donor available.
Subject unwilling or unable to give informed consent, or unable to comply with protocol.
Primary myelofibrosis or myelofibrosis secondary to essential thrombocythemia, polycythemia vera, or myelodysplastic syndrome with grade 4 marrow fibrosis.
Females who are breast-feeding or pregnant.
Uncontrolled bacterial, viral or fungal infection at the time of the transplant preparative regimen.
Concurrent enrollment on other interventional graft versus host disease clinical trials.
Subjects who undergo desensitization to reduce anti-donor HLA antibody levels prior to transplant.
HIV positive patients with persistently positive viral load.
Poor cardiac function (e.g., Left ventricular ejection fraction <= 45% or shortening fraction < 27%).
Renal dysfunction (e.g., estimated creatinine clearance <= 60 mL/min or glomerular filtration rate < 60ml/min/1.73m2).
Pulmonary dysfunction (e.g., DLCO corrected for hemoglobin <= 50%, FEV1 predicted <= 50%, Forced Vital Capacity < 50%, or resting pulse oximetry <= 92% without supplemental oxygen).
Liver dysfunction (e.g., Total bilirubin > 2.5 mg/dL, alanine aminotransferase or aspartate aminotransferase >= 3x the upper limit of normal, or liver function unacceptable per local institutional guidelines).
Poor performance status (e.g., Karnofsky performance status <= 70% or <= 60%, or Lansky performance score < 70%).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (ECOG > 1).
Low hemoglobin (less than 9.0 g/dL, or less than 8.0 g/dL for gastric cancer).
Low leukocytes (less than 3.0 K/cumm).
Low absolute neutrophil count (less than 1.5 K/cumm).
Low platelets (less than 100 K/cumm).
Low serum albumin (less than 2.5 g/dL).
Liver dysfunction (Total bilirubin greater than or equal to 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal, unless documented Gilbert syndrome or liver metastases where it may be up to 3 x institutional upper limit of normal; AST or ALT greater than or equal to 3 x institutional upper limit of normal, or 5 x institutional upper limit of normal if liver metastases are present).
Renal dysfunction (Creatinine greater than or equal to 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal and glomerular filtration rate less than 30 mL/min/1.73 m^2).
Coagulopathy (INR, prothrombin time, or activated partial thromboplastin time greater than 1.5 x institutional upper limit of normal in patients not receiving therapeutic anticoagulation).
Unstable human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection (CD4 count less than 250 cells/mcL within 7 days of enrollment, detectable viral load, or opportunistic infection within the past 6 months).
Chronic hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection with detectable viral load on suppressive therapy.
Chronic hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection that is not treated and cured or has a detectable viral load while on treatment.
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease with progressive clinical symptoms or a requirement for immediate central nervous system treatment.
Heart failure (New York Heart Association functional classification class III or IV, or left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50%).
Myocardial infarction within 6 months before enrollment.
Corrected QT interval (QTc) prolongation (greater than 470 ms for females or greater than 450 ms for males).
History of non-infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis that required steroids, current interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis, or suspected interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis that cannot be ruled out by imaging.
Severe pulmonary compromise (e.g., pulmonary emboli within three months, severe asthma, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, restrictive lung disease, or pleural effusion).
Autoimmune, connective tissue, or inflammatory disorders with potential pulmonary involvement (e.g., rheumatoid arthritis, Sjogren's, sarcoidosis).
Prior pneumonectomy.
Allergic reactions attributed to compounds of similar chemical or biologic composition to DS-8201a or neratinib, or severe hypersensitivity reactions to other monoclonal antibodies.
Concomitant use of medications that are moderate or strong inhibitors or inducers of CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein.
Clinically significant corneal disease.
Pleural effusion, ascites, or pericardial effusion that requires drainage, peritoneal shunt, or cell-free and concentrated ascites reinfusion therapy for gastric cancer patients.
Spinal cord compression.
Uncontrolled infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, antivirals, or antifungals.
Unresolved toxicities from previous anticancer therapy (grade greater than 1, except for alopecia).
Substance abuse.
Clinically significant cardiac or psychological conditions interfering with study participation.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Clinically significant chronic gastrointestinal disorder with diarrhea as a major symptom, or grade 2 or greater diarrhea at baseline.
Inability to swallow tablets.
Active additional malignancy or a personal history of additional malignancy that may affect disease outcome.
Prior allogeneic organ transplantation including allogeneic stem cell transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Cutaneous melanoma. Histology allowed: Cutaneous melanoma excluding blue nevus subtype, mucosal melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and uveal/ocular/choroidal melanoma. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable and/or metastatic. Prior treatment required: Progressed on anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody monotherapy or anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody combination therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: 2 copies of the fully functional form of GPER protein-coding sequence. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
Allogeneic tissue or solid organ transplant.
Unstable autoimmune or immunodeficiency disease.
Other concurrent health issues that would make participation or completion of the study difficult.
Prior reaction to anti-PD-1 therapy that would make treatment with pembrolizumab unadvisable.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: Female or male. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: more than 1 prior hormonal regimen or more than 1 prior chemotherapy or OP-1250. Biomarkers required: ER positive and HER2 negative (assessed via archival tumor tissue sample from a metastatic site). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Prior or concurrent malignancy whose natural history or treatment may interfere with the safety or efficacy assessment of the investigational regimen.
Clinically significant, uncontrolled heart disease or cardiac repolarization abnormality.
History of cerebral vascular disease within 6 months.
History of pulmonary embolism or deep venous thrombosis within 6 months, or increased risk of thrombosis.
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression.
Medical history or ongoing gastrointestinal disorders that could affect absorption of oral therapeutics.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Clinically significant liver disease consistent with Child-Pugh Class B or C, including active viral or other hepatitis, current alcohol abuse, or cirrhosis.
History of severe cutaneous reaction (eg, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, erythema multiforme, toxic epidermal necrolysis, or drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms).
Active infection or high risk of developing a serious infection.
Clinically significant co-morbidities, such as psychiatric disease.
Poor performance status (eg ECOG >1).
